name: Delete ACL Rule
description: Deletes an access control rule from a Google Calendar. Use when you need to remove sharing permissions for a user, group, or domain.

inputs.name: rule_id
inputs.desc: ACL rule identifier.

inputs.name: calendar_id
inputs.desc: Calendar identifier. To retrieve calendar IDs call the calendarList.list method. If you want to access the primary calendar of the currently logged in user, use the "primary" keyword.

name: Get ACL Rule
description: Retrieves a specific access control rule for a calendar. Use when you need to check permissions for a specific user, group, or domain.

inputs.name: rule_id
inputs.desc: ACL rule identifier. Format: 'scope_type:scope_value' or 'default'. Valid scope types: 'user' (email), 'group' (group email), 'domain' (domain name), 'default' (public access). Examples: 'user:john@example.com', 'group:team@example.com', 'domain:example.com', 'default'. Note: 'me' is NOT valid; use actual email/domain. The rule must exist - use GOOGLECALENDAR_LIST_ACL_RULES to find valid IDs.

inputs.name: calendar_id
inputs.desc: Calendar identifier. To retrieve calendar IDs call the calendarList.list method. If you want to access the primary calendar of the currently logged in user, use the "primary" keyword.

name: Create ACL Rule
description: Creates an access control rule for a calendar. Use when you need to grant sharing permissions to a user, group, or domain.

inputs.name: role
inputs.desc: The role assigned to the scope. Possible values: "none" - Provides no access; "freeBusyReader" - Provides read access to free/busy information; "reader" - Provides read access to the calendar; "writer" - Provides read and write access to the calendar; "owner" - Provides ownership of the calendar.

inputs.name: scope
inputs.desc: The extent to which calendar access is granted by this ACL rule. Specifies who gets the access (user, group, domain, or default).

inputs.name: calendar_id
inputs.desc: Calendar identifier. To retrieve calendar IDs call the calendarList.list method. If you want to access the primary calendar of the currently logged in user, use the "primary" keyword.

inputs.name: send_notifications
inputs.desc: Whether to send notifications about the calendar sharing change. Optional. The default is true.

name: List ACL Rules
description: Retrieves the list of access control rules (ACLs) for a specified calendar, providing the necessary 'rule_id' values required for updating specific ACL rules.

inputs.name: pageToken
inputs.desc: Token specifying which result page to return. Optional.

inputs.name: syncToken
inputs.desc: Token obtained from the nextSyncToken field returned on the last page of a previous list operation. It makes the result of this list operation contain only entries that have changed since then. Optional. The default is to retrieve all entries.

inputs.name: calendarId
inputs.desc: Calendar identifier. To retrieve calendar IDs call the calendarList.list method. If you want to access the primary calendar of the currently logged in user, use the "primary" keyword.

inputs.name: maxResults
inputs.desc: Maximum number of entries returned on one result page. Optional. The default is 100.

inputs.name: showDeleted
inputs.desc: Whether to include deleted ACLs in the result. Optional. The default is False.

name: Patch ACL Rule
description: Updates an existing access control rule for a calendar using patch semantics (partial update). This allows modifying specific fields without affecting other properties. IMPORTANT: The ACL rule must already exist on the calendar. This action cannot create new rules. If you receive a 404 Not Found error, the rule does not exist - use ACL insert to create it first, or use ACL list to verify available rules. Each patch request consumes three quota units. For domain-type ACL rules, if PATCH fails with 500 error, this action will automatically fallback to UPDATE method.

inputs.name: role
inputs.desc: The role assigned to the scope. Possible values are: "none" - Provides no access; "freeBusyReader" - Provides read access to free/busy information; "reader" - Provides read access to the calendar (private events appear but details are hidden); "writer" - Provides read and write access to the calendar (private events and details are visible); "owner" - Provides ownership of the calendar (all permissions of writer plus ability to see and manipulate ACLs).

inputs.name: scope
inputs.desc: The extent to which calendar access is granted by this ACL rule. Optional for patch operations. Must include `type` (one of: 'user', 'group', 'domain', 'default') and `value` (email address or domain name) for all types except 'default'.

inputs.name: rule_id
inputs.desc: ACL rule identifier of an existing rule. IMPORTANT: The rule must already exist on the calendar - this action cannot create new rules, only modify existing ones. Use the ACL list action to find existing rule IDs, or use the ACL insert action to create a new rule first. Format: 'type:value', such as 'user:email@example.com' or 'group:group@example.com'.

inputs.name: calendar_id
inputs.desc: Calendar identifier. To retrieve calendar IDs call the calendarList.list method. If you want to access the primary calendar of the currently logged in user, use the "primary" keyword.

inputs.name: send_notifications
inputs.desc: Whether to send notifications about the calendar sharing change. Note that there are no notifications on access removal. Optional. The default is True.

name: Update ACL Rule
description: Updates an access control rule for the specified calendar.

inputs.name: role
inputs.desc:
The role assigned to the scope. Possible values are: 
- "none" - Provides no access. 
- "freeBusyReader" - Provides read access to free/busy information. 
- "reader" - Provides read access to the calendar. Private events will appear to users with reader access, but event details will be hidden. 
- "writer" - Provides read and write access to the calendar. Private events will appear to users with writer access, and event details will be visible. 
- "owner" - Provides ownership of the calendar. This role has all of the permissions of the writer role with the additional ability to see and manipulate ACLs.

inputs.name: rule_id
inputs.desc: ACL rule identifier.

inputs.name: calendar_id
inputs.desc: Calendar identifier. To retrieve calendar IDs call the calendarList.list method. If you want to access the primary calendar of the currently logged in user, use the "primary" keyword.

inputs.name: send_notifications
inputs.desc: Whether to send notifications about the calendar sharing change. Note that there are no notifications on access removal. Optional. The default is True.

name: Watch ACL Changes
description: Tool to watch for changes to ACL resources. Use when you need to set up real-time notifications for access control list modifications on a calendar.

inputs.name: id
inputs.desc: A UUID or similar unique string that identifies this channel.

inputs.name: type
inputs.desc: The type of delivery mechanism used for this channel. Valid values are "web_hook" or "webhook".

inputs.name: token
inputs.desc: An arbitrary string delivered to the target address with each notification delivered over this channel. Optional.

inputs.name: params
inputs.desc: Additional parameters controlling delivery channel behavior. Optional.

inputs.name: address
inputs.desc: The address where notifications are delivered for this channel.

inputs.name: calendarId
inputs.desc: Calendar identifier. To retrieve calendar IDs call the calendarList.list method. If you want to access the primary calendar of the currently logged in user, use the "primary" keyword.

name: Add Enrichment
description: Adds an enrichment at a specified position in a defined album.

inputs.name: albumId
inputs.desc: Identifier of the album where the enrichment is to be added

inputs.name: albumPosition
inputs.desc: Position in the album where the enrichment is to be inserted. Required field with valid position types: FIRST_IN_ALBUM, LAST_IN_ALBUM, AFTER_MEDIA_ITEM (requires relativeMediaItemId), AFTER_ENRICHMENT_ITEM (requires relativeEnrichmentItemId)

inputs.name: newEnrichmentItem
inputs.desc: The enrichment to be added, can be a text, location, or map enrichment

name: Add file sharing preference (Deprecated)
description: DEPRECATED: Use GOOGLEDRIVE_CREATE_PERMISSION instead; use GOOGLEDRIVE_UPDATE_PERMISSION to modify existing permissions (avoids duplicate entries). Modifies sharing permissions for an existing Google Drive file, granting a specified role to a user, group, domain, or 'anyone'. Bulk calls may trigger 403 rateLimitExceeded (~100 req/100s/user); use jittered exponential backoff.

inputs.name: role
inputs.desc: Permission role to grant. Accepted values: 'reader', 'commenter', 'writer', 'fileOrganizer', 'organizer', 'owner'. Invalid strings cause validation failures.

inputs.name: type
inputs.desc: Type of grantee for the permission. Using 'anyone' with 'writer' or 'owner' broadly exposes the document — confirm before applying. Admin policies may block 'anyone' or domain-wide sharing. For type='anyone' with role='reader', the link must be explicitly shared; files are not publicly searchable.

inputs.name: domain
inputs.desc: Domain to grant permission to (e.g., 'example.com'). Required if 'type' is 'domain'.

inputs.name: file_id
inputs.desc: Unique identifier of the file to update sharing settings for. Must be an alphanumeric string containing only letters, numbers, hyphens, and underscores (no slashes, spaces, or other special characters). Use GOOGLEDRIVE_FIND_FILE or GOOGLEDRIVE_LIST_FILES to get valid file IDs from your Google Drive. For shared drive membership, supply the shared drive ID, not an individual document ID.

inputs.name: email_address
inputs.desc: Email address of the user or group. Required if 'type' is 'user' or 'group'.

inputs.name: transfer_ownership
inputs.desc: Whether to transfer ownership to the specified user. Required when role is 'owner'. Only a single user can be specified in the request when transferring ownership. Ownership transfer is difficult to reverse — obtain explicit confirmation before setting true.

name: Modify email labels
description: Adds and/or removes specified Gmail labels for a message; ensure `message_id` and all `label_ids` are valid (use 'listLabels' for custom label IDs).

inputs.name: user_id
inputs.desc: User's email address or 'me' for the authenticated user.

inputs.name: message_id
inputs.desc: Immutable ID of the message to modify. Gmail message IDs are 15-16 character hexadecimal strings (e.g., '1a2b3c4d5e6f7890'). IMPORTANT: Do NOT use UUIDs (32-character strings like '093ca4662b214d5eba8f4ceeaad63433'), thread IDs, or internal system IDs - these will cause 'Invalid id value' errors. Obtain valid message IDs from: (1) 'GMAIL_FETCH_EMAILS' response 'messageId' field, (2) 'GMAIL_FETCH_MESSAGE_BY_THREAD_ID' response, or (3) 'GMAIL_LIST_THREADS' and then fetching thread messages.

inputs.name: add_label_ids
inputs.desc: Label IDs to add (IDs, not display names). System labels: INBOX, SPAM, TRASH, UNREAD, STARRED, IMPORTANT, CATEGORY_PERSONAL, CATEGORY_SOCIAL, CATEGORY_PROMOTIONS, CATEGORY_UPDATES, CATEGORY_FORUMS. Use full CATEGORY_ prefix (e.g., 'CATEGORY_UPDATES' not 'UPDATES'). Custom labels: call 'listLabels' first to get the ID (format: 'Label_<number>'). Do NOT use the label display name; the API requires the ID. SENT, DRAFT, CHAT are immutable and cannot be added or removed. A label cannot appear in both add_label_ids and remove_label_ids.

inputs.name: remove_label_ids
inputs.desc: Label IDs to remove (IDs, not display names). System labels: INBOX, SPAM, TRASH, UNREAD, STARRED, IMPORTANT, CATEGORY_PERSONAL, CATEGORY_SOCIAL, CATEGORY_PROMOTIONS, CATEGORY_UPDATES, CATEGORY_FORUMS. Use full CATEGORY_ prefix (e.g., 'CATEGORY_UPDATES' not 'UPDATES'). Custom labels: call 'listLabels' first to get the ID (format: 'Label_<number>'). SENT, DRAFT, CHAT are immutable and cannot be removed. Common operations: to mark as read, remove 'UNREAD'; to archive, remove 'INBOX'. A label cannot appear in both add_label_ids and remove_label_ids.

name: Add or remove to customer list
description: AddOrRemoveToCustomerList Tool will add a contact to a customer list in Google Ads. Note: It takes 6 to 12 hours for changes to be reflected in the customer list. Email addresses must comply with Google Ads policies and applicable privacy/consent laws.

inputs.name: emails
inputs.desc: Array of emails of the contacts to be added to the customer list. Emails must be valid, normalized strings (lowercase, trimmed); malformed addresses reduce match rates.

inputs.name: operation
inputs.desc: Operation to be performed on the customer list. Either create or remove.

inputs.name: resource_name
inputs.desc: Resource name of the customer list. For example: customers/1234567890/userLists/1234567890

name: Insert File Parent (v2)
description: Tool to add a parent folder for a file using Google Drive API v2. Use when you need to add a file to an additional folder.

inputs.name: id
inputs.desc: The ID of the parent folder to add. This is the folder that will become a parent of the file.

inputs.name: fileId
inputs.desc: The ID of the file to add a parent folder to.

inputs.name: supportsAllDrives
inputs.desc: Whether the requesting application supports both My Drives and shared drives. Default is false.

inputs.name: supportsTeamDrives
inputs.desc: Deprecated: Use supportsAllDrives instead.

inputs.name: enforceSingleParent
inputs.desc: Deprecated: Adding files to multiple folders is no longer supported. Use shortcuts instead.

name: Insert Property (v2 API)
description: Tool to add a property to a file, or update it if it already exists (v2 API). Use when you need to attach custom key-value metadata to a Google Drive file.

inputs.name: alt
inputs.desc: Data format for response.

inputs.name: key
inputs.desc: API key. Your API key identifies your project and provides you with API access, quota, and reports. Required unless you provide an OAuth 2.0 token.

inputs.name: xgafv
inputs.desc: V1 error format values.

inputs.name: fields
inputs.desc: Selector specifying which fields to include in a partial response.

inputs.name: fileId
inputs.desc: The ID of the file.

inputs.name: callback
inputs.desc: JSONP callback function name.

inputs.name: quotaUser
inputs.desc: Available to use for quota purposes for server-side applications. Can be any arbitrary string assigned to a user, but should not exceed 40 characters.

inputs.name: uploadType
inputs.desc: Legacy upload protocol for media (e.g. 'media', 'multipart').

inputs.name: visibility
inputs.desc: Property visibility values.

inputs.name: oauth_token
inputs.desc: OAuth 2.0 token for the current user.

inputs.name: prettyPrint
inputs.desc: Returns response with indentations and line breaks.

inputs.name: access_token
inputs.desc: OAuth access token.

inputs.name: property_key
inputs.desc: The key of this property.

inputs.name: property_value
inputs.desc: The value of this property.

inputs.name: upload_protocol
inputs.desc: Upload protocol for media (e.g. 'raw', 'multipart').

name: Add Sheet to Existing Spreadsheet
description: Adds a new sheet to a spreadsheet. Supports three sheet types: GRID, OBJECT, and DATA_SOURCE. SHEET TYPES: - GRID (default): Standard spreadsheet with rows/columns. Use properties to set dimensions, tab color, etc. - OBJECT: Sheet containing a chart. Requires objectSheetConfig with chartSpec (basicChart or pieChart). - DATA_SOURCE: Sheet connected to BigQuery. Requires dataSourceConfig with bigQuery spec and bigquery.readonly OAuth scope. OTHER NOTES: - Sheet names must be unique; use forceUnique=true to auto-append suffix (_2, _3) if name exists - For tab colors, use EITHER rgbColor OR themeColor, not both - Avoid 'index' when creating sheets in parallel (causes errors) - OBJECT sheets are created via addChart with position.newSheet=true - DATA_SOURCE sheets require bigquery.readonly OAuth scope Use cases: Add standard grid sheet, create chart on dedicated sheet, connect to BigQuery data source.

inputs.name: title
inputs.desc: The name for the new sheet tab. Must be unique within the spreadsheet. Example: "Q3 Report", "Sales Data 2025". This is a convenience parameter - alternatively, you can set this via properties.title. Note: sheet_name is also accepted as an alias for title.

inputs.name: properties
inputs.desc: Advanced sheet properties (grid dimensions, tab color, position, etc.). For simple cases, just use the 'title' parameter directly. Use this for additional customization.

inputs.name: force_unique
inputs.desc: When True (default), automatically ensures the sheet name is unique by appending a numeric suffix (e.g., '_2', '_3') if the requested name already exists. This makes the action resilient to retries and parallel workflows. When False, the action fails with an error if a sheet with the same name already exists.

inputs.name: spreadsheet_id
inputs.desc: REQUIRED. Cannot be empty. The ID of the target spreadsheet where the new sheet will be added. This is the long alphanumeric string in the Google Sheet URL (e.g., '1BxiMVs0XRA5nFMdKvBdBZjgmUUqptlbs74OgvE2upms'). Use 'Search Spreadsheets' action to find the spreadsheet ID by name if you don't have it.

inputs.name: data_source_config
inputs.desc:
Configuration for creating a DATA_SOURCE sheet.

DATA_SOURCE sheets connect to external data sources like BigQuery.
The API uses addDataSource request which automatically creates the associated sheet.

IMPORTANT: Requires additional OAuth scope: bigquery.readonly

inputs.name: object_sheet_config
inputs.desc:
Configuration for creating an OBJECT sheet (a sheet containing a chart).

To create an OBJECT sheet, you must provide chart configuration.
The API uses addChart with position.newSheet=true to create the chart on its own sheet.

name: Aggregate Column Data
description: Searches for rows where a specific column matches a value and performs mathematical operations on data from another column.

inputs.name: operation
inputs.desc: The mathematical operation to perform on the target column values.

inputs.name: sheet_name
inputs.desc: The name of the specific sheet within the spreadsheet. Matching is case-insensitive. If no exact match is found, partial matches will be attempted (e.g., 'overview' will match 'Overview 2025').

inputs.name: search_value
inputs.desc: The exact value to search for in the search column. Case-sensitive by default. If not provided (or if search_column is not provided), all rows in the target column will be aggregated without filtering.

inputs.name: search_column
inputs.desc: The column to search in for filtering rows. Can be a letter (e.g., 'A', 'B') or column name from header row (e.g., 'Region', 'Department'). If not provided, all rows in the target column will be aggregated without filtering.

inputs.name: target_column
inputs.desc: The column to aggregate data from. Can be a letter (e.g., 'C', 'D') or column name from header row (e.g., 'Sales', 'Revenue').

inputs.name: case_sensitive
inputs.desc: Whether the search should be case-sensitive.

inputs.name: has_header_row
inputs.desc: Whether the first row contains column headers. If True, column names can be used for search_column and target_column.

inputs.name: spreadsheet_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the Google Sheets spreadsheet.

inputs.name: percentage_total
inputs.desc: For percentage operation, the total value to calculate percentage against. If not provided, uses sum of all values in target column.

inputs.name: additional_filters
inputs.desc: Extra column=value conditions applied with AND logic on top of search_column/search_value. Use this to filter on multiple columns simultaneously. Example: [{"column": "Region", "value": "APAC"}] combined with search_column=Product/search_value=Beacon returns only rows where Product=Beacon AND Region=APAC.

name: Append Dimension
description: Tool to append new rows or columns to a sheet, increasing its size. Use when you need to add empty rows or columns to an existing sheet.

inputs.name: length
inputs.desc: The number of rows or columns to append.

inputs.name: sheet_id
inputs.desc: The numeric ID of the sheet (not the sheet name). This is a non-negative integer found in the sheet's URL as the 'gid' parameter (e.g., gid=0) or in the sheet properties. The first sheet in a spreadsheet typically has sheet_id=0.

inputs.name: dimension
inputs.desc: Specifies whether to append rows or columns.

inputs.name: spreadsheet_id
inputs.desc: The ID of the spreadsheet.

inputs.name: response_ranges
inputs.desc: Limits the ranges of the spreadsheet to include in the response.

inputs.name: response_include_grid_data
inputs.desc: True if grid data should be included in the response (if includeSpreadsheetInResponse is true).

inputs.name: include_spreadsheet_in_response
inputs.desc: True if the updated spreadsheet should be included in the response.

name: Archive Custom Dimension
description: Tool to archive a CustomDimension on a property. Use when you need to remove a custom dimension from active use without permanently deleting it. Archived dimensions cannot be used in new reports.

inputs.name: name
inputs.desc: Required. The resource name of the CustomDimension to archive. Must be in the exact format: properties/{property_id}/customDimensions/{dimension_id} where property_id and dimension_id are numeric identifiers. IMPORTANT: Must start with 'properties/' prefix, contain '/customDimensions/' in the middle, have no trailing slashes, and no additional path segments. Valid example: 'properties/489591273/customDimensions/13661259421'. Invalid examples: '489591273/13661259421' (missing prefix), 'properties/489591273/customDimensions/13661259421/' (trailing slash), 'properties/489591273/customDimensions/13661259421/extra' (extra segments)

name: Autocomplete Place Predictions
description: Returns place and query predictions for text input. Use when implementing as-you-type autocomplete functionality for place searches. Returns up to five predictions ordered by relevance.

inputs.name: input
inputs.desc: Text string to search for place predictions. Can be full words, substrings, place names, addresses, or plus codes. Must be non-empty.

inputs.name: origin
inputs.desc: Latitude/longitude point for distance calculations.

inputs.name: regionCode
inputs.desc: Region code in ccTLD format for formatting responses (e.g., 'us', 'br').

inputs.name: inputOffset
inputs.desc: Zero-based Unicode character offset of cursor position in the input string.

inputs.name: languageCode
inputs.desc: Preferred language for results using IETF BCP-47 language codes (e.g., 'en-US', 'es-ES').

inputs.name: locationBias
inputs.desc: Area to bias search results toward.

inputs.name: sessionToken
inputs.desc: User-generated string grouping calls into sessions for billing purposes.

inputs.name: includedRegionCodes
inputs.desc: Up to 15 two-character country codes (ISO 3166-1 Alpha-2) to restrict results. Query predictions unavailable when this is set.

inputs.name: locationRestriction
inputs.desc: Area to restrict search results within.

inputs.name: includedPrimaryTypes
inputs.desc: Restricts results to up to five specified primary types (e.g., 'restaurant', 'cafe').

inputs.name: includeQueryPredictions
inputs.desc: Includes query predictions in response for text searches (default: false).

inputs.name: includePureServiceAreaBusinesses
inputs.desc: Includes businesses without physical locations (default: false).

name: Auto-Resize Rows or Columns
description: Auto-fit column widths or row heights for a dimension range using batchUpdate.autoResizeDimensions. Use when you need to automatically adjust row heights or column widths to fit content after writing data.

inputs.name: sheet_id
inputs.desc: The numeric ID of the sheet to resize. Either sheet_id or sheet_name must be provided. If both are provided, sheet_name takes precedence and will be resolved to sheet_id.

inputs.name: dimension
inputs.desc: The dimension to auto-resize. Use 'ROWS' to auto-fit row heights or 'COLUMNS' to auto-fit column widths.

inputs.name: end_index
inputs.desc: The zero-based end index of the dimension range to resize (exclusive). Must be greater than start_index. For example, to resize columns A-C, use start_index=0 and end_index=3.

inputs.name: sheet_name
inputs.desc: The name of the sheet to resize. Either sheet_id or sheet_name must be provided. Using sheet_name is recommended as it's more intuitive. If both sheet_id and sheet_name are provided, sheet_name takes precedence.

inputs.name: start_index
inputs.desc: The zero-based start index of the dimension range to resize (inclusive). For columns, 0 = column A. For rows, 0 = row 1.

inputs.name: spreadsheet_id
inputs.desc: The ID of the spreadsheet containing the sheet to resize.

name: Batch Add Media Items
description: Adds one or more media items to an album in Google Photos.

inputs.name: albumId
inputs.desc: Identifier of the Album that the media items are added to. Must be an album created by this application.

inputs.name: mediaItemIds
inputs.desc: Identifiers of the MediaItems to be added. Maximum 50 items. Media items must be created by this application.

name: Batch Clear Values By Data Filter
description: Clears one or more ranges of values from a spreadsheet using data filters. The caller must specify the spreadsheet ID and one or more DataFilters. Ranges matching any of the specified data filters will be cleared. Only values are cleared -- all other properties of the cell (such as formatting, data validation, etc..) are kept.

inputs.name: dataFilters
inputs.desc: The DataFilters used to determine which ranges to clear.

inputs.name: spreadsheetId
inputs.desc: The ID of the spreadsheet to update.

name: Batch Create Media Items
description: Batch upload and create media items in Google Photos. Supports three input methods: 1. 'urls': Simple list of public URLs (file names extracted automatically) 2. 'media_files': List of objects with url/file, file_name, and description 3. 'files': List of FileUploadable objects for pre-uploaded files Media items can optionally be added to an album at a specific position. Maximum 50 items per request.

inputs.name: urls
inputs.desc: Simplified input: List of public URLs of media files to upload. File names will be extracted from URLs automatically. Use 'media_files' for more control over file names and descriptions.

inputs.name: files
inputs.desc: List of files to upload to Google Photos.

inputs.name: albumId
inputs.desc: Optional identifier of the album where the media items are to be added. If not specified, media items are added to the user's library only.

inputs.name: media_files
inputs.desc: List of media files to upload. Each item can specify either a 'url' (public URL) or a 'file' (FileUploadable object). Maximum 50 items per request.

inputs.name: albumPosition
inputs.desc: Position in the album to add the media items.

name: Batch delete Gmail messages
description: Tool to permanently delete multiple Gmail messages in bulk, bypassing Trash with no recovery possible. Use when you need to efficiently remove large numbers of emails (e.g., retention enforcement, mailbox hygiene). Use GMAIL_MOVE_TO_TRASH instead when reversibility may be needed. Always obtain explicit user confirmation and verify a sample of message IDs before executing. High-volume calls may trigger 429 rateLimitExceeded or 403 userRateLimitExceeded errors; apply exponential backoff.

inputs.name: userId
inputs.desc: User's email address or 'me' for the authenticated user.

inputs.name: messageIds
inputs.desc: List of Gmail message IDs to delete. Each ID must be a 15-16 character hexadecimal string (e.g., '18c5f5d1a2b3c4d5'). Obtain IDs from actions like GMAIL_FETCH_EMAILS or GMAIL_LIST_THREADS - do not use human-readable descriptions.

name: Batch Events
description: Execute up to 1000 event mutations (create/patch/delete) in one Google Calendar HTTP batch request with per-item status/results. Use this to materially reduce round-trips for bulk operations like migrations, cleanup, or large-scale updates.

inputs.name: fail_fast
inputs.desc: If true, stop processing after the first batch containing any 4xx error (except 404 on DELETE). Default is false.

inputs.name: operations
inputs.desc: List of batch operations to execute. Maximum 1000 operations per request.

name: Batch Execute Google Tasks Operations
description: Executes multiple Google Tasks API operations in a single HTTP batch request and returns structured per-item results. Use this to reduce LLM tool invocations when performing bulk operations like updating many tasks, moving tasks, or deleting multiple items. Note: Each sub-request still counts toward API quota; batching primarily reduces HTTP overhead and tool call count.

inputs.name: requests
inputs.desc: Array of sub-requests to execute in this batch. Each sub-request is independent and will return its own result.

inputs.name: max_requests
inputs.desc: Maximum number of sub-requests allowed in a single batch. Google's typical cap is 1000. Lower values can control response size.

name: Batch get spreadsheet
description: Retrieves data from specified cell ranges in a Google Spreadsheet.

inputs.name: ranges
inputs.desc: A list of cell ranges in A1 notation from which to retrieve data. If this list is omitted, empty, or contains only empty strings, all data from the first sheet of the spreadsheet will be fetched. Empty strings in the list are automatically filtered out. Supported formats: (1) Bare sheet name like 'Sheet1' to get all data from that sheet, (2) Sheet with range like 'Sheet1!A1:B2', (3) Just cell reference like 'A1:B2' (uses first sheet). For sheet names with spaces or special characters, enclose in single quotes (e.g., "'My Sheet'" or "'My Sheet'!A1:B2"). IMPORTANT: For large sheets, always use bounded ranges with explicit row limits (e.g., 'Sheet1!A1:Z10000' instead of 'Sheet1!A:Z'). Unbounded column ranges like 'A:Z' on sheets with >10,000 rows may cause timeouts or errors. If you need all data from a large sheet, fetch in chunks of 10,000 rows at a time.

inputs.name: majorDimension
inputs.desc: The major dimension for organizing data in results.

inputs.name: spreadsheet_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the Google Spreadsheet from which data will be retrieved. This is the ID found in the spreadsheet URL after /d/. You can provide either the spreadsheet ID directly or a full Google Sheets URL (the ID will be extracted automatically).

inputs.name: valueRenderOption
inputs.desc: How values should be rendered in the output. FORMATTED_VALUE: Values are calculated and formatted (default). UNFORMATTED_VALUE: Values are calculated but not formatted. FORMULA: Values are not calculated; the formula is returned instead.

inputs.name: dateTimeRenderOption
inputs.desc: How dates and times should be rendered in the output. SERIAL_NUMBER: Dates are returned as serial numbers (default). FORMATTED_STRING: Dates returned as formatted strings.

inputs.name: empty_strings_filtered
inputs.desc: Indicates whether empty strings were filtered from the response.

name: Batch Get Media Items
description: Returns the list of media items for the specified media item identifiers.

inputs.name: mediaItemIds
inputs.desc: Identifiers of the media items to be requested. Must not contain duplicates.

name: Batch modify Gmail messages
description: Modify labels on multiple Gmail messages in one efficient API call. Supports up to 1,000 messages per request for bulk operations like archiving, marking as read/unread, or applying custom labels. High-volume calls may return 429 rateLimitExceeded or 403 userRateLimitExceeded; apply exponential backoff.

inputs.name: userId
inputs.desc: User's email address or 'me' for the authenticated user.

inputs.name: messageIds
inputs.desc: List of message IDs to modify. Maximum 1,000 message IDs per request. Get message IDs from GMAIL_FETCH_EMAILS or GMAIL_LIST_THREADS actions. Accepts 'messageIds', 'ids', or 'message_ids' as the parameter name.

inputs.name: addLabelIds
inputs.desc: List of label IDs to add to the messages. IMPORTANT: Use label IDs, NOT label display names. System labels use their name as ID: INBOX, STARRED, IMPORTANT, SENT, DRAFT, SPAM, TRASH, UNREAD, CATEGORY_PERSONAL, CATEGORY_SOCIAL, CATEGORY_PROMOTIONS, CATEGORY_UPDATES, CATEGORY_FORUMS. Custom labels MUST use their ID (format: 'Label_XXX', e.g., 'Label_1', 'Label_25'), NOT the display name (e.g., do NOT use 'Work' or 'Projects'). Call GMAIL_LIST_LABELS first to get the 'id' field for custom labels. At least one of add_label_ids or remove_label_ids must be provided. CONSTRAINT: Label IDs must NOT overlap with remove_label_ids - cannot add and remove the same label.

inputs.name: removeLabelIds
inputs.desc: List of label IDs to remove from the messages. IMPORTANT: Use label IDs, NOT label display names. System labels use their name as ID: INBOX, STARRED, IMPORTANT, SENT, SPAM, TRASH, UNREAD. Custom labels MUST use their ID (format: 'Label_XXX', e.g., 'Label_1', 'Label_25'), NOT the display name (e.g., do NOT use 'Work' or 'Projects'). Call GMAIL_LIST_LABELS first to get the 'id' field for custom labels. Common use cases: Remove 'UNREAD' to mark as read, remove 'INBOX' to archive. Note: 'DRAFT' cannot be removed - use GMAIL_DELETE_DRAFT instead. At least one of add_label_ids or remove_label_ids must be provided. CONSTRAINT: Label IDs must NOT overlap with add_label_ids - cannot add and remove the same label.

name: Batch Run Pivot Reports
description: Tool to return multiple pivot reports in a batch for a GA4 property. Use when you need to fetch multiple pivot table reports with multi-dimensional analysis in a single request.

inputs.name: property
inputs.desc: Required. The GA4 property resource name. Format: properties/{property_id}

inputs.name: requests
inputs.desc: Required. Up to 5 individual RunPivotReportRequest objects. Each request can have dimensions, metrics, dateRanges, pivots, dimensionFilter, metricFilter, currencyCode, cohortSpec, keepEmptyRows, returnPropertyQuota, and comparisons fields. CRITICAL CONSTRAINT: Every dimension defined in 'dimensions' MUST be used in at least one of: pivots (fieldNames), dimensionFilter, or orderBys. Dimensions not used anywhere will cause a 400 error. The only exception is 'dateRange' dimensions. Additionally, all fieldNames in pivots must reference dimensions defined in the request's 'dimensions' array, and no two pivots can share the same dimension.

name: Batch Run Reports
description: Tool to return multiple analytics data reports in a batch. Use when you need to fetch multiple reports for one GA4 property in a single request.

inputs.name: property
inputs.desc: Required. The property resource name. Format: properties/{property_id}

inputs.name: requests
inputs.desc: Required. Up to 5 individual RunReportRequest objects (minimum 1, maximum 5). CRITICAL: Non-cohort requests MUST contain 'dateRanges' (list of {startDate, endDate}); cohort requests (containing 'cohortSpec') must NOT include 'dateRanges'. Key fields per request: dateRanges, dimensions (list of {name}), metrics (list of {name}), dimensionFilter/metricFilter (FilterExpression: use andGroup/orGroup/notExpression/filter at top level), cohortSpec, offset, limit, orderBys, keepEmptyRows. NOTE: Do NOT include 'pivots' field - use batchRunPivotReports action for pivot reports.

inputs.name: unwrapped_filters
inputs.desc: Internal field to track which filters were unwrapped during validation

name: Batch update spreadsheet (Deprecated)
description: DEPRECATED: Use GOOGLESHEETS_VALUES_UPDATE instead. Write values to ONE range in a Google Sheet, or append as new rows if no start cell is given. IMPORTANT - This tool does NOT accept the Google Sheets API's native batch format: - WRONG: {"data": [{"range": "...", "values": [[...]]}], ...} - CORRECT: {"sheet_name": "...", "values": [[...]], "first_cell_location": "...", ...} To update MULTIPLE ranges, make SEPARATE CALLS to this tool for each range. Features: - Auto-expands grid for large datasets (prevents range errors) - Set first_cell_location to write at a specific position (e.g., "A1", "B5") - Omit first_cell_location to append values as new rows at the end Requirements: Target sheet must exist and spreadsheet must contain at least one worksheet.

inputs.name: values
inputs.desc: A 2D array of cell values where each inner array represents a row. Values can be strings, numbers, booleans, or None/null for empty cells. Ensure columns are properly aligned across rows.

inputs.name: sheet_name
inputs.desc: The name of the specific sheet (tab) within the spreadsheet to update (required, separate from cell reference). Case-insensitive matching is supported (e.g., 'sheet1' will match 'Sheet1'). Note: Default sheet names are locale-dependent (e.g., 'Sheet1' in English, 'Foglio1' in Italian, 'Hoja 1' in Spanish, '시트1' in Korean, 'Feuille 1' in French). If you specify a common default name like 'Sheet1' and it doesn't exist, the action will automatically use the first sheet in the spreadsheet.

inputs.name: spreadsheet_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the Google Sheets spreadsheet to be updated. Must be an alphanumeric string (with hyphens and underscores allowed) typically 44 characters long. Can be found in the spreadsheet URL between '/d/' and '/edit'. Example: 'https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1BxiMVs0XRA5nFMdKvBdBZjgmUUqptlbs74OgvE2upms/edit' has ID '1BxiMVs0XRA5nFMdKvBdBZjgmUUqptlbs74OgvE2upms'.

inputs.name: value_input_option
inputs.desc: How input data should be interpreted. 'USER_ENTERED': Values are parsed as if typed by a user (e.g., strings may become numbers/dates, formulas are calculated). 'RAW': Values are stored exactly as provided without parsing (e.g., '123' stays as string, '=SUM(A1:B1)' is not calculated).

inputs.name: first_cell_location
inputs.desc: The starting cell for the update range, specified as a single cell in A1 notation WITHOUT sheet prefix (e.g., 'A1', 'B2', 'AA931'). The update will extend from this cell to the right and down based on the provided values. Sheet name must be provided separately in the 'sheet_name' field. If omitted or set to null, values are appended as new rows to the sheet. Note: Use only a single cell reference (e.g., 'AA931'), NOT a range (e.g., 'AA931:AF931') or sheet-prefixed notation (e.g., 'Sheet1!A1').

inputs.name: include_values_in_response
inputs.desc: If set to True, the response will include the updated values in the 'spreadsheet.responses[].updatedData' field. The updatedData object contains 'range' (A1 notation), 'majorDimension' (ROWS), and 'values' (2D array of the actual cell values after the update).

name: Batch Update Values by Data Filter
description: Tool to update values in ranges matching data filters. Use when you need to update specific data in a Google Sheet based on criteria rather than fixed cell ranges.

inputs.name: data
inputs.desc: The new values to apply to the spreadsheet. If more than one range is matched by the specified DataFilter the specified values are applied to all of those ranges. Can be provided as a JSON string or as a list of DataFilterValueRange objects.

inputs.name: spreadsheetId
inputs.desc: The ID of the spreadsheet to update.

inputs.name: valueInputOption
inputs.desc: How the input data should be interpreted. RAW: Values are stored exactly as entered, without parsing. USER_ENTERED: Values are parsed as if typed by a user (numbers stay numbers, strings prefixed with '=' become formulas, etc.). INPUT_VALUE_OPTION_UNSPECIFIED: Default input value option is not specified.

inputs.name: includeValuesInResponse
inputs.desc: Determines if the update response should include the values of the cells that were updated. By default, responses do not include the updated values.

inputs.name: responseValueRenderOption
inputs.desc: Determines how values in the response should be rendered. The default render option is FORMATTED_VALUE.

inputs.name: responseDateTimeRenderOption
inputs.desc: Determines how dates, times, and durations in the response should be rendered. This is ignored if responseValueRenderOption is FORMATTED_VALUE. The default dateTime render option is SERIAL_NUMBER.

name: Bulk Insert Tasks (Deprecated)
description: DEPRECATED: Use BatchExecute instead. Creates multiple tasks in a Google Tasks list in a single operation using HTTP batching. Use when you need to create many tasks efficiently (reducing round-trips compared to individual insert calls).

inputs.name: tasks
inputs.desc: Array of tasks to create. Each task requires at least a title.

inputs.name: batch_size
inputs.desc: Maximum number of tasks per batch request. Default: 50. Tasks exceeding this will be split into multiple batch calls.

inputs.name: tasklist_id
inputs.desc: Identifier for the Google Task list where tasks will be created. Use actual task list IDs, not '@default'.

name: Remove Calendar from List
description: Tool to remove a calendar from the user's calendar list. Use when you need to unsubscribe from or hide a calendar from the user's list.

inputs.name: calendar_id
inputs.desc: Calendar identifier. To retrieve calendar IDs call the calendarList.list method. If you want to access the primary calendar of the currently logged in user, use the 'primary' keyword.

name: Get Single Calendar by ID
description: Retrieves metadata for a SINGLE specific calendar from the user's calendar list by its calendar ID. This action requires a calendarId parameter and returns details about that one calendar only. NOTE: This does NOT list all calendars. To list all calendars in the user's calendar list, use GOOGLECALENDAR_CALENDAR_LIST_LIST instead.

inputs.name: calendarId
inputs.desc: Required. The calendar identifier for the single calendar to retrieve. Use 'primary' for the primary calendar of the authenticated user, or provide a specific calendar ID (e.g., an email address or group calendar ID). To find calendar IDs, first use GOOGLECALENDAR_CALENDAR_LIST_LIST to list all calendars.

name: Insert Calendar into List
description: Inserts an existing calendar into the user's calendar list, making it visible in the UI. Calendars (e.g., newly created ones) won't appear in the list or UI until explicitly inserted.

inputs.name: id
inputs.desc: The identifier of the calendar to insert. Must be a valid calendar ID in email format (e.g., ‘user@example.com’ for a user’s calendar or ‘calendarid@group.calendar.google.com’ for a shared calendar). Note: The ‘primary’ keyword is not supported for this operation - use the actual email address of the primary calendar instead. The calendar must exist and you must have appropriate access permissions.

inputs.name: hidden
inputs.desc: Whether the calendar has been hidden from the list. Accepts only boolean values: true or false. If not specified, the API defaults to false.

inputs.name: colorId
inputs.desc: The color of the calendar. This is an ID referring to an entry in the calendarCore color palette.

inputs.name: selected
inputs.desc: Whether the calendar is selected and visible in the calendar list. Accepts only boolean values: true or false. If not specified, the API defaults to false.

inputs.name: backgroundColor
inputs.desc: The background color of the calendar in the Web UI. (Hexadecimal color code)

inputs.name: foregroundColor
inputs.desc: The foreground color of the calendar in the Web UI. (Hexadecimal color code)

inputs.name: summaryOverride
inputs.desc: The summary that the authenticated user has set for this calendar.

inputs.name: color_rgb_format
inputs.desc: Whether to use the foregroundColor and backgroundColor fields to write the calendar colors (RGB). If this feature is used, the index-based colorId field will be set to the best matching option automatically. Optional. The default is False.

inputs.name: defaultReminders
inputs.desc: The default reminders that the authenticated user has for this calendar.

inputs.name: notificationSettings
inputs.desc: The notifications that the authenticated user is receiving for this calendar.

name: Patch Calendar List Entry
description: Updates an existing calendar on the user's calendar list using patch semantics. This method allows partial updates, modifying only the specified fields.

inputs.name: hidden
inputs.desc: Whether calendar is hidden.

inputs.name: colorId
inputs.desc: ID for calendar color from colors endpoint.

inputs.name: selected
inputs.desc: Whether calendar content shows in UI.

inputs.name: calendar_id
inputs.desc: Calendar identifier. To retrieve calendar IDs call the calendarList.list method. Use "primary" keyword for the currently logged in user's primary calendar.

inputs.name: colorRgbFormat
inputs.desc: Whether to use RGB for foreground/background colors.

inputs.name: backgroundColor
inputs.desc: Hex color for calendar background.

inputs.name: foregroundColor
inputs.desc: Hex color for calendar foreground.

inputs.name: summaryOverride
inputs.desc: User-set summary for the calendar.

inputs.name: defaultReminders
inputs.desc: List of default reminders.

inputs.name: notificationSettings
inputs.desc: Notification settings for the calendar.

name: Update Calendar List Entry
description: Updates a calendar list entry's display/subscription settings (color, visibility, reminders, selection) for the authenticated user — does not modify the underlying calendar resource (title, timezone, etc.). To modify the calendar itself, use GOOGLECALENDAR_CALENDARS_UPDATE.

inputs.name: hidden
inputs.desc: Whether calendar is hidden.

inputs.name: colorId
inputs.desc: ID for calendar color from colors endpoint.

inputs.name: selected
inputs.desc: Whether calendar content shows in UI.

inputs.name: calendar_id
inputs.desc: Calendar identifier. Must be an actual calendar ID (e.g., "examplecalendar@group.calendar.google.com" or "c_abc123...@group.calendar.google.com"). To retrieve valid calendar IDs, use the GOOGLECALENDAR_LIST_CALENDARS action first. The "primary" alias is not valid for calendarList.update.

inputs.name: colorRgbFormat
inputs.desc: Whether to use RGB for foreground/background colors.

inputs.name: backgroundColor
inputs.desc: Hex color for calendar background.

inputs.name: foregroundColor
inputs.desc: Hex color for calendar foreground.

inputs.name: summaryOverride
inputs.desc: User-set summary for the calendar.

inputs.name: defaultReminders
inputs.desc: List of default reminders.

inputs.name: notificationSettings
inputs.desc: Notification settings for the calendar.

name: Watch Calendar List
description: Watch for changes to CalendarList resources using push notifications. Use this to receive real-time updates when calendar list entries are modified.

inputs.name: id
inputs.desc: A UUID or similar unique string that identifies this channel. Maximum 64 characters.

inputs.name: type
inputs.desc: The type of delivery mechanism used for this channel. Must be "web_hook" or "webhook".

inputs.name: token
inputs.desc: An arbitrary string delivered to the target address with each notification. Maximum 256 characters. Used for channel verification and message routing.

inputs.name: params
inputs.desc: Additional parameters controlling delivery channel behavior.

inputs.name: address
inputs.desc: The HTTPS URL where notifications are delivered for this channel. Must have a valid SSL certificate.

inputs.name: expiration
inputs.desc: Unix timestamp in milliseconds indicating when the API should stop sending notifications.

name: Delete Calendar
description: Deletes a secondary calendar that you own or have delete permissions on. Deletion is permanent and irreversible — verify the correct calendar_id before calling. You cannot delete your primary calendar or calendars you only have read/write access to. Use calendarList.list to find calendars with owner accessRole. For primary calendars, use calendars.clear instead. Parallel calls may trigger userRateLimitExceeded; sequence bulk deletions.

inputs.name: calendar_id
inputs.desc: Calendar identifier for a secondary calendar you own or have delete permissions on. Use calendarList.list to find deletable calendar IDs (look for accessRole "owner"). Primary calendars cannot be deleted; use the Clear Calendar action instead.

name: Update Calendar
description: Full PUT-style update that overwrites all calendar metadata fields; unspecified optional fields are cleared. Use GOOGLECALENDAR_PATCH_CALENDAR to update only a subset of fields. Mutates the underlying calendar resource (title, description, timeZone, etc.); use GOOGLECALENDAR_CALENDAR_LIST_UPDATE to change per-user display properties like color.

inputs.name: summary
inputs.desc: Title of the calendar. Must be a non-empty string; passing an empty string clears the calendar title.

inputs.name: location
inputs.desc: Geographic location of the calendar as free-form text. Optional.

inputs.name: timeZone
inputs.desc: The time zone of the calendar. (Formatted as an IANA Time Zone Database name, e.g. "Europe/Zurich".) Optional.

inputs.name: calendarId
inputs.desc: Calendar identifier. Use 'primary' to update the primary calendar of the currently logged in user, or provide a specific calendar ID (typically in email format like 'abc123@group.calendar.google.com'). To retrieve calendar IDs call the calendarList.list method. IMPORTANT: This is NOT the calendar's display name/title. NOTE: For better performance, prefer providing the actual calendar ID instead of 'primary', as the 'primary' alias requires an additional API call to resolve.

inputs.name: description
inputs.desc: Description of the calendar. Optional.

name: Stop Channel
description: Tool to stop watching resources through a notification channel. Use when you need to discontinue push notifications for a specific channel subscription.

inputs.name: id
inputs.desc: A UUID or similar unique string that identifies this channel.

inputs.name: token
inputs.desc: An arbitrary string delivered to the target address with each notification delivered over this channel. Optional.

inputs.name: resourceId
inputs.desc: An opaque ID that identifies the resource being watched on this channel. Stable across different API versions.

name: Check Compatibility
description: Tool to list dimensions and metrics compatible with a GA4 report request. Use when you need to validate compatibility of chosen dimensions or metrics before running a report.

inputs.name: metrics
inputs.desc: Optional. List of metrics to check compatibility for. Maximum of 10 metrics allowed per request.

inputs.name: property
inputs.desc: Required. GA4 property resource name. Format: properties/{property_id}.

inputs.name: dimensions
inputs.desc: Optional. List of dimensions to check compatibility for.

inputs.name: metricFilter
inputs.desc: Optional. A FilterExpression for metrics; must follow GA4 FilterExpression JSON schema.

inputs.name: dimensionFilter
inputs.desc: Optional. A FilterExpression for dimensions; must follow GA4 FilterExpression JSON schema.

inputs.name: compatibilityFilter
inputs.desc:
Compatibility status for dimensions or metrics per Google Analytics Data API v1beta.
Valid values: COMPATIBILITY_UNSPECIFIED, COMPATIBLE, INCOMPATIBLE.
Note: Values like 'REPORT_COMPATIBLE' are NOT valid - use 'COMPATIBLE' instead.

name: Clear Basic Filter
description: Tool to clear the basic filter from a sheet. Use when you need to remove an existing basic filter from a specific sheet within a Google Spreadsheet.

inputs.name: sheet_id
inputs.desc: The ID of the sheet on which the basic filter should be cleared.

inputs.name: spreadsheet_id
inputs.desc: The ID of the spreadsheet.

inputs.name: response_ranges
inputs.desc: Limits the ranges included in the response spreadsheet. Only applicable when include_spreadsheet_in_response is true.

inputs.name: response_include_grid_data
inputs.desc: True if grid data should be returned in the response. Only applicable when include_spreadsheet_in_response is true.

inputs.name: include_spreadsheet_in_response
inputs.desc: Determines if the update response should include the spreadsheet resource.

name: Clear Calendar
description: Clears a primary calendar by deleting all events from it. The calendar itself is preserved; only its events are removed. Primary calendars cannot be deleted entirely.

inputs.name: calendar_id
inputs.desc: Calendar identifier. To retrieve calendar IDs call the `calendarList.list` method. If you want to access the primary calendar of the currently logged in user, use the "`primary`" keyword.

name: Clear tasks
description: Permanently and irreversibly clears all completed tasks from a specified Google Tasks list; this action is destructive, idempotent, and cannot be undone. Always require explicit user confirmation before invoking.

inputs.name: tasklist
inputs.desc: The identifier of the task list from which to clear completed tasks. Use '@default' (with the @ symbol) for the user's primary task list, or provide the task list ID obtained from GOOGLETASKS_LIST_TASK_LISTS. Note: 'default' without the @ prefix is not valid.

name: Clear spreadsheet values
description: Clears cell content (preserving formatting and notes) from a specified A1 notation range in a Google Spreadsheet; the range must correspond to an existing sheet and cells.

inputs.name: range
inputs.desc: The A1 notation of the range to clear values from (e.g., 'Sheet1!A1:B2', 'MySheet!C:C', or 'A1:D5'). If the sheet name is omitted (e.g., 'A1:B2'), the operation applies to the first visible sheet.

inputs.name: spreadsheet_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the Google Spreadsheet from which to clear values. This ID can be found in the URL of the spreadsheet.

name: Get Color Definitions
description: Returns the color definitions for calendars and events. Use when you need to retrieve the available color palette for styling calendars or events.

name: Compute Route Matrix
description: Calculates travel distance and duration matrix between multiple origins and destinations using the modern Routes API; supports OAuth2 authentication and various travel modes. Matrix is capped at 625 elements (e.g., 25×25); chunk larger sets to avoid RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED errors. Response elements may be returned out of input order — always use originIndex and destinationIndex to map results. Only use elements where condition='ROUTE_EXISTS'; the matrix may be incomplete.

inputs.name: units
inputs.desc: Unit system (e.g., 'METRIC' for kilometers, 'IMPERIAL' for miles) for displaying distances. Note: distanceMeters in responses is always in meters regardless of this setting; units affects only human-readable text output.

inputs.name: origins
inputs.desc: List of origin locations. Each can be specified as an address or latitude/longitude coordinates.

inputs.name: fieldMask
inputs.desc: Comma-separated list of response fields to include (e.g., 'originIndex,destinationIndex,duration,distanceMeters'). Use '*' for all fields.

inputs.name: travelMode
inputs.desc: Mode of transportation for the route matrix calculation.

inputs.name: destinations
inputs.desc: List of destination locations. Each can be specified as an address or latitude/longitude coordinates.

inputs.name: languageCode
inputs.desc: BCP-47 language code (e.g., 'en-US', 'es') for textual information.

inputs.name: routingPreference
inputs.desc: Specifies factors to consider when calculating the route.

name: Copy Google Document
description: Tool to create a copy of an existing Google Document. Use this to duplicate a document, for example, when using an existing document as a template. The copied document will have a default title (e.g., 'Copy of [original title]') if no new title is provided, and will be placed in the user's root Google Drive folder.

inputs.name: title
inputs.desc: The title for the new copied document. If not provided, the title will be 'Copy of [original document's title]'.

inputs.name: document_id
inputs.desc: The ID of the Google Document to be copied.

inputs.name: include_shared_drives
inputs.desc: Whether to support copying documents from shared drives. Defaults to True.

name: Copy file (Deprecated)
description: DEPRECATED: Use GOOGLEDRIVE_COPY_FILE_ADVANCED instead. Duplicates an existing file (not folders) in Google Drive by `file_id`; copy lands in same folder as original — use GOOGLEDRIVE_MOVE_FILE afterward for precise placement. Copy receives a new `file_id`; update stored references accordingly. For shared drives, requires organizer/manager rights.

inputs.name: file_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier for the file on Google Drive that you want to copy. This ID can be retrieved from the file's shareable link or via other Google Drive API calls. Pass only the raw ID, not a full URL. Name-based searches may return multiple files — confirm the correct `file_id` before calling.

inputs.name: new_title
inputs.desc: The title to assign to the new copy of the file. If not provided, the copied file will have the same title as the original, prefixed with 'Copy of '.

name: Copy file with advanced options
description: Creates a copy of a file and applies any requested updates with patch semantics. Use when you need to duplicate a file with advanced options like label inclusion, visibility settings, or custom metadata.

inputs.name: alt
inputs.desc: Data format for response.

inputs.name: key
inputs.desc: API key. Your API key identifies your project and provides you with API access, quota, and reports. Required unless you provide an OAuth 2.0 token.

inputs.name: name
inputs.desc: The name of the copied file. If not provided, the copied file will have the same name as the original, prefixed with 'Copy of '.

inputs.name: xgafv
inputs.desc: V1 error format options.

inputs.name: fields
inputs.desc: Selector specifying which fields to include in a partial response. Use comma-separated field paths.

inputs.name: fileId
inputs.desc: The ID of the file to copy. This is the unique identifier for the file on Google Drive.

inputs.name: parents
inputs.desc: The IDs of the parent folders which contain the file. If not specified as part of a copy request, the file inherits any discoverable parents of the source file.

inputs.name: starred
inputs.desc: Whether the user has starred the file.

inputs.name: trashed
inputs.desc: Whether the file has been trashed.

inputs.name: callback
inputs.desc: JSONP callback parameter.

inputs.name: mimeType
inputs.desc: The MIME type of the file. Google Drive attempts to automatically detect an appropriate value from uploaded content, if no value is provided.

inputs.name: quotaUser
inputs.desc: Available to use for quota purposes for server-side applications. Can be any arbitrary string assigned to a user, but should not exceed 40 characters.

inputs.name: properties
inputs.desc: A collection of arbitrary key-value pairs which are visible to all apps. Entries with null values are cleared in update and copy requests.

inputs.name: uploadType
inputs.desc: Legacy upload protocol for media (e.g. 'media', 'multipart').

inputs.name: createdTime
inputs.desc: The time at which the file was created (RFC 3339 date-time).

inputs.name: description
inputs.desc: A short description of the copied file.

inputs.name: oauth_token
inputs.desc: OAuth 2.0 token for the current user.

inputs.name: ocrLanguage
inputs.desc: A language hint for OCR processing during image import (ISO 639-1 code).

inputs.name: prettyPrint
inputs.desc: Returns response with indentations and line breaks for improved readability.

inputs.name: access_token
inputs.desc: OAuth access token.

inputs.name: modifiedTime
inputs.desc: The last time the file was modified by anyone (RFC 3339 date-time). Note that setting modifiedTime will also update modifiedByMeTime for the user.

inputs.name: appProperties
inputs.desc: A collection of arbitrary key-value pairs which are private to the requesting app. Entries with null values are cleared in update and copy requests. These properties can only be retrieved using an authenticated request with an OAuth 2 client ID.

inputs.name: includeLabels
inputs.desc: A comma-separated list of IDs of labels to include in the labelInfo part of the response.

inputs.name: folderColorRgb
inputs.desc: The color for a folder or a shortcut to a folder as an RGB hex string. The supported colors are published in the folderColorPalette field of the About resource.

inputs.name: upload_protocol
inputs.desc: Upload protocol for media (e.g. 'raw', 'multipart').

inputs.name: writersCanShare
inputs.desc: Whether users with only writer permission can modify the file's permissions. Not populated for items in shared drives.

inputs.name: supportsAllDrives
inputs.desc: Whether the requesting application supports both My Drives and shared drives.

inputs.name: supportsTeamDrives
inputs.desc: Deprecated: Use supportsAllDrives instead.

inputs.name: enforceSingleParent
inputs.desc: Deprecated. Copying files into multiple folders is no longer supported. Use shortcuts instead.

inputs.name: keepRevisionForever
inputs.desc: Whether to set the 'keepForever' field in the new head revision. This is only applicable to files with binary content in Google Drive. Only 200 revisions for the file can be kept forever. If the limit is reached, try deleting pinned revisions.

inputs.name: ignoreDefaultVisibility
inputs.desc: Whether to ignore the domain's default visibility settings for the created file. Domain administrators can choose to make all uploaded files visible to the domain by default; this parameter bypasses that behavior for the request. Permissions are still inherited from parent folders.

inputs.name: includePermissionsForView
inputs.desc: Specifies which additional view's permissions to include in the response. Only 'published' is supported.

inputs.name: copyRequiresWriterPermission
inputs.desc: Whether the options to copy, print, or download this file should be disabled for readers and commenters.

name: Create Album
description: Creates a new album in Google Photos.

inputs.name: title
inputs.desc: Name of the album to be created in Google Photos. Maximum 500 characters.

name: Create Audience Export
description: Tool to create an audience export for Google Analytics. Use when you need to export a snapshot of users in an audience at a specific point in time. This initiates a long-running asynchronous request that returns an operation resource name immediately. The export begins in CREATING state with rowCount=0; the operation must complete before export data is accessible for querying.

inputs.name: parent
inputs.desc: Parent property resource name where the audience export will be created. Format: properties/{property_id}.

inputs.name: audience
inputs.desc: Audience resource name identifying the audience to export. Format: properties/{property_id}/audiences/{audience_id}.

inputs.name: dimensions
inputs.desc: Optional list of dimensions requested and displayed in the export. Each dimension is specified by dimensionName.

name: Create Audience List
description: Tool to create an audience list for later retrieval by initiating a long-running asynchronous request. Use when you need to create a snapshot of users currently in an audience. The method returns quickly with an Operation resource while processing occurs in the background.

inputs.name: parent
inputs.desc: Required. The parent property resource name. Format: 'properties/{property_id}'. Example: 'properties/489591273'

inputs.name: audience
inputs.desc: Required. The audience resource name identifying the audience being listed. Format: 'properties/{property_id}/audiences/{audience_id}'. Example: 'properties/489591273/audiences/11228260226'

inputs.name: dimensions
inputs.desc: Required. The dimensions requested and displayed in the query response. At least one dimension is required. Each entry must be an object with a `dimensionName` key (e.g., `[{'dimensionName': 'deviceId'}]`). Only dimensions supported for audience lists on that property are valid; unsupported values return a 400 INVALID_ARGUMENT error. Use GOOGLE_ANALYTICS_GET_METADATA to retrieve valid dimension names.

inputs.name: webhookNotification
inputs.desc: Webhook notification configuration for audience list operation updates.

name: Create Chart in Google Sheets
description: Create a chart in a Google Sheets spreadsheet using the specified data range and chart type. Conditional requirements: - Provide either a simple chart via chart_type + data_range (basicChart), OR supply a full chart_spec supporting all chart types. Exactly one approach should be used. - When using chart_spec, set exactly one of the union fields (basicChart | pieChart | bubbleChart | candlestickChart | histogramChart | waterfallChart | treemapChart | orgChart | scorecardChart).

inputs.name: title
inputs.desc: Optional title for the chart.

inputs.name: sheet_id
inputs.desc: The numeric sheetId (not the sheet name/title) of the worksheet where the chart will be created. This is a unique integer identifier for the sheet within the spreadsheet. The first/default sheet typically has sheetId=0. IMPORTANT: Use 'Get Spreadsheet Info' action to retrieve valid sheetIds - look for sheets[].properties.sheetId in the response. The sheetId must exist in the target spreadsheet; using an ID from a different spreadsheet will fail.

inputs.name: subtitle
inputs.desc: Optional subtitle for the chart.

inputs.name: chart_spec
inputs.desc: Optional full ChartSpec object to send to the Google Sheets API. Use this to support ALL chart types and advanced options. Must set exactly one of: basicChart, pieChart, bubbleChart, candlestickChart, histogramChart, treemapChart, waterfallChart, orgChart, scorecardChart. See https://developers.google.com/workspace/sheets/api/reference/rest/v4/spreadsheets/charts#ChartSpec.

inputs.name: chart_type
inputs.desc: The type of chart to create. Case-insensitive. Supported types: BAR, LINE, AREA, COLUMN, SCATTER, COMBO, STEPPED_AREA (basic charts with axes), PIE (pie/donut charts), HISTOGRAM, BUBBLE, CANDLESTICK (requires 4+ data columns for low/open/close/high), TREEMAP, WATERFALL, ORG (organizational charts), SCORECARD. Each chart type uses its appropriate Google Sheets API spec structure. For advanced customization, provide chart_spec instead.

inputs.name: data_range
inputs.desc: A single contiguous range of data for the chart in A1 notation (e.g., 'A1:C10' or 'Sheet1!B2:D20'). Must be a single continuous range - comma-separated multi-ranges (e.g., 'A1:A10,C1:C10') are not supported. When chart_spec is not provided, the first column is used as the domain/labels and the remaining columns as series. IMPORTANT: PIE charts require at least 2 columns - the first column for category labels (domain) and the second column for numeric values (series). Single-column ranges are not supported for PIE charts.

inputs.name: x_axis_title
inputs.desc: Optional title for the X-axis.

inputs.name: y_axis_title
inputs.desc: Optional title for the Y-axis.

inputs.name: background_red
inputs.desc: Red component of chart background color (0.0-1.0). If not specified, uses default.

inputs.name: spreadsheet_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the Google Sheets spreadsheet where the chart will be created. Must be the actual spreadsheet ID from the URL (e.g., '1BxiMVs0XRA5nFMdKvBdBZjgmUUqptlbs74OgvE2upms'), NOT the spreadsheet name or title. Find it in the URL: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/SPREADSHEET_ID/edit

inputs.name: background_blue
inputs.desc: Blue component of chart background color (0.0-1.0). If not specified, uses default.

inputs.name: legend_position
inputs.desc: Position of the chart legend. Options: BOTTOM_LEGEND, TOP_LEGEND, LEFT_LEGEND, RIGHT_LEGEND, NO_LEGEND.

inputs.name: background_green
inputs.desc: Green component of chart background color (0.0-1.0). If not specified, uses default.

name: Create Comment
description: Tool to create a comment on a file in Google Drive. Returns a nested `data` object; extract `data.id` for the resulting comment identifier. Omit `anchor` and `quoted_file_content_*` for general file-level comments.

inputs.name: anchor
inputs.desc: A JSON string defining the region of the document to which the comment is anchored. Format: {"region": {"kind": "drive#commentRegion", "<classifier>": <value>, "rev": "head"}}. Supported classifiers: (1) "line" for text lines (e.g., "line": 12), (2) "page" for page numbers (e.g., "page": {"p": 0}), (3) "txt" for text ranges (e.g., "txt": {"o": 100, "l": 50}), (4) "rect" for rectangles in images (e.g., "rect": {"x": 10, "y": 20, "w": 100, "h": 50}), (5) "time" for video timestamps (e.g., "time": {"t": "00:01:30"}), (6) "matrix" for spreadsheet cells (e.g., "matrix": {"c": 2, "r": 5}). Note: On blob files, only unanchored comments are supported. Google Workspace editors may treat API-set anchors as unanchored.

inputs.name: content
inputs.desc: The plain text content of the comment.

inputs.name: file_id
inputs.desc: The ID of the file. The `id` field from GOOGLEDOCS_SEARCH_DOCUMENTS results can be used directly without conversion.

inputs.name: quoted_file_content_value
inputs.desc: The quoted content itself.

inputs.name: quoted_file_content_mime_type
inputs.desc: The MIME type of the quoted content.

name: Create Custom Dimension
description: Tool to create a CustomDimension for a Google Analytics property. Use when you need to add a new custom dimension to track specific user properties, event parameters, or eCommerce item parameters.

inputs.name: scope
inputs.desc: Required. Immutable. The scope of this dimension. Must be one of: EVENT, USER, or ITEM

inputs.name: parent
inputs.desc: Required. The property in which to create the CustomDimension. Format: properties/{property_id}

inputs.name: description
inputs.desc: Optional. Description for this custom dimension. Max length of 150 characters

inputs.name: displayName
inputs.desc: Required. Display name for this custom dimension as shown in the Analytics UI. Max length of 82 characters, alphanumeric plus space and underscore starting with a letter

inputs.name: parameterName
inputs.desc: Required. Immutable. Tagging parameter name for this custom dimension. For user-scoped dimensions, this is the user property name. For event-scoped dimensions, this is the event parameter name. For item-scoped dimensions, this is the parameter name in the eCommerce items array. May only contain alphanumeric and underscore characters, starting with a letter. Max length of 24 characters for user-scoped dimensions, 40 characters for event-scoped dimensions

inputs.name: disallowAdsPersonalization
inputs.desc: Optional. If set to true, sets this dimension as NPA and excludes it from ads personalization. This is currently only supported by user-scoped custom dimensions

name: Create customer list
description: Creates a customer list in Google Ads. Note: Requires an authenticated Google Ads connection with customer_id configured. Email-based lists must comply with Google Ads policies and applicable privacy/consent laws. Membership updates can take many hours to propagate; targeting eligibility is not immediate after creation.

inputs.name: name
inputs.desc: Name of the customer list.

inputs.name: description
inputs.desc: Description of the customer list.

name: Create Custom Metric
description: Tool to create a custom metric in Google Analytics. Use when you need to define a new custom metric for tracking specific event parameters.

inputs.name: scope
inputs.desc: Required. Immutable. The scope of this custom metric.

inputs.name: parent
inputs.desc: Required. The property where the custom metric will be created. Format: properties/{property_id}

inputs.name: description
inputs.desc: Optional. Description for this custom metric. Max length of 150 characters.

inputs.name: displayName
inputs.desc: Required. Display name for this custom metric as shown in the Analytics UI. Max length of 82 characters, alphanumeric plus space and underscore starting with a letter.

inputs.name: parameterName
inputs.desc: Required. Immutable. Tagging name for this custom metric. If this is an event-scoped metric, then this is the event parameter name. May only contain alphanumeric and underscore characters, starting with a letter. Max length of 40 characters for event-scoped metrics.

inputs.name: measurementUnit
inputs.desc: Required. The type for the custom metric's value.

inputs.name: restrictedMetricType
inputs.desc: Optional. Types of restricted data that this metric may contain. Required for metrics with CURRENCY measurement unit. Must be empty for metrics with a non-CURRENCY measurement unit.

name: Create a document
description: Creates a new Google Docs document using the provided title as filename and inserts the initial text at the beginning if non-empty, returning the document's ID and metadata (excluding body content).

inputs.name: text
inputs.desc: Optional initial text content to insert at the beginning of the new document. If not provided, an empty document will be created. For very large text (over 50,000 characters), the text will be inserted in chunks to avoid API limits.

inputs.name: title
inputs.desc: Title for the new document, used as its filename in Google Drive.

name: Create blank document (Deprecated)
description: DEPRECATED: Use GOOGLEDOCS_CREATE_DOCUMENT instead. Tool to create a blank Google Docs document with a specified title. Use when you need to create a new, empty document.

inputs.name: title
inputs.desc: The title of the document to create. This will be used as the document name in Google Drive.

name: Create Document Markdown
description: Creates a new Google Docs document, optionally initializing it with a title and content provided as Markdown text.

inputs.name: title
inputs.desc: The title for the new Google Docs document.

inputs.name: image_assets
inputs.desc: Optional uploaded images that Markdown can reference by name. Use Markdown image syntax like ![Alt](attachment://diagram). Public HTTPS image URLs in markdown do not need image_assets.

inputs.name: markdown_text
inputs.desc: The initial content for the document, formatted as Markdown. Also accepts 'content' as an alias. Supports headings, real Google Docs lists including nested lists, multiple Markdown tables, task-list markers, images via publicly accessible URLs or matching image_assets uploads (max 2KB URL length after resolution; formats: JPEG, PNG, GIF; SVG not supported), blockquotes, code blocks, hyperlinks (e.g., [text](url)), and text formatting (bold, italic, etc.). If empty or omitted, creates document with title only.

name: Create Shared Drive
description: Tool to create a new shared drive. Use when you need to programmatically create a new shared drive for collaboration or storage.

inputs.name: name
inputs.desc: The name of this shared drive.

inputs.name: hidden
inputs.desc: Whether the shared drive is hidden from default view.

inputs.name: themeId
inputs.desc: The ID of the theme from which the background image and color will be set. The set of possible driveThemes can be retrieved from a drive.about.get response. When not specified on a drive.drives.create request, a random theme is chosen from which the background image and color are set. This is a write-only field; it can only be set on requests that don't set colorRgb or backgroundImageFile.

inputs.name: colorRgb
inputs.desc: The color of this shared drive as an RGB hex string. It can only be set on a drive.drives.update request that does not set themeId.

inputs.name: requestId
inputs.desc: Optional. An ID for idempotent creation of a shared drive. If not provided, a UUID will be auto-generated. Each requestId can only be used ONCE to successfully create a drive. If retrying a request that succeeded previously with the same requestId, the existing drive will be returned.

inputs.name: backgroundImageFile
inputs.desc: An image file and cropping parameters from which a background image for this shared drive is set. This is a write only field; it can only be set on drive.drives.update requests that don't set themeId. When specified, all fields of the backgroundImageFile must be set.

name: Create email draft
description: Creates a Gmail email draft. While all fields are optional per the Gmail API, practical validation requires at least one of recipient_email, cc, or bcc and at least one of subject or body. Supports To/Cc/Bcc recipients, subject, plain/HTML body (ensure `is_html=True` for HTML), attachments, and threading. Returns a draft_id that must be used as-is with GMAIL_SEND_DRAFT — synthetic or stale IDs will fail. When creating a draft reply to an existing thread (thread_id provided), leave subject empty to stay in the same thread; setting a subject will create a NEW thread instead. HTTP 429 may occur on rapid creation/send sequences; apply exponential backoff.

inputs.name: cc
inputs.desc: Carbon Copy (CC) recipients' email addresses. Each must be a valid email address (e.g., 'user@example.com') or display name format (e.g., 'John Doe <user@example.com>'). Plain names without email addresses are NOT valid. Optional for drafts (recipients can be added later before sending).

inputs.name: bcc
inputs.desc: Blind Carbon Copy (BCC) recipients' email addresses. Each must be a valid email address (e.g., 'user@example.com') or display name format (e.g., 'Bob Jones <user@example.com>'). Plain names without email addresses are NOT valid. Optional for drafts (recipients can be added later before sending).

inputs.name: body
inputs.desc: Email body content (plain text or HTML); `is_html` must be True if HTML. Optional - drafts can be created without a body and edited later before sending. Can also be provided as 'message_body'.

inputs.name: is_html
inputs.desc:
Set to True if `body` is already formatted HTML. When False, plain text newlines are auto-converted to
tags. Both modes result in HTML email; this flag controls whether the body content is treated as raw HTML or plain text that gets HTML formatting applied.

inputs.name: subject
inputs.desc: Email subject line. Optional - drafts can be created without a subject and edited later before sending. When creating a draft reply to an existing thread (thread_id provided), leave this empty to stay in the same thread. Setting a subject will create a NEW thread instead.

inputs.name: user_id
inputs.desc: User's email address or 'me' for the authenticated user.

inputs.name: thread_id
inputs.desc: ID of an existing Gmail thread to reply to; omit for new thread. If the thread ID is invalid or inaccessible, the draft will be created as a new thread instead of failing.

inputs.name: attachment
inputs.desc: File to attach to the email. Must be a dict with fields: name (filename), mimetype (e.g., 'application/pdf'), and s3key (obtained from a prior upload/download response — local paths or guessed keys will fail). Total message size including base64-encoded attachments must be under 25 MB; use shareable links (e.g., Google Drive) for larger files.

inputs.name: recipient_email
inputs.desc: Primary recipient's email address. Must be a valid email address (e.g., 'user@example.com') or display name format (e.g., 'John Doe <user@example.com>'). A plain name without an email address (e.g., 'John Doe') is NOT valid - the '@' symbol and domain are required. Optional for drafts (recipients can be added later before sending). Use extra_recipients if you want to send to multiple recipients.

inputs.name: extra_recipients
inputs.desc: Additional 'To' recipients' email addresses (not Cc or Bcc). Each must be a valid email address (e.g., 'user@example.com'), display name format (e.g., 'Jane Doe <user@example.com>'), or 'me' for the authenticated user.
