inputs.desc: Email body content (plain text or HTML); is_html must be True if HTML. If not provided, previous body is preserved. Can also be provided as 'message_body'.

inputs.name: is_html
inputs.desc:
Set to True if body is already formatted HTML. When False, plain text newlines are auto-converted to
tags.

inputs.name: subject
inputs.desc: Email subject line. If not provided, previous subject is preserved.

inputs.name: user_id
inputs.desc: User's email address or 'me' for the authenticated user.

inputs.name: draft_id
inputs.desc: The ID of the draft to update. Must be a valid draft ID from GMAIL_LIST_DRAFTS or GMAIL_CREATE_EMAIL_DRAFT.

inputs.name: thread_id
inputs.desc: ID of an existing Gmail thread. If provided, the draft will be part of this thread.

inputs.name: attachment
inputs.desc: File to attach to the draft. Replaces any existing attachments.

inputs.name: recipient_email
inputs.desc: Primary recipient's email address. Must be a valid email address (e.g., 'user@example.com') or display name format (e.g., 'John Doe <user@example.com>'). Optional - if not provided, previous recipients are preserved.

inputs.name: extra_recipients
inputs.desc: Additional 'To' recipients' email addresses. Each must be a valid email address or display name format. Should only be used if recipient_email is also provided.

name: Update Shared Drive
description: Tool to update the metadata for a shared drive. Use when you need to modify properties like the name, theme, background image, or restrictions of a shared drive.

inputs.name: name
inputs.desc: The new name for the shared drive.

inputs.name: hidden
inputs.desc: Whether the shared drive is hidden from the default view.

inputs.name: driveId
inputs.desc: The ID of the shared drive to update.

inputs.name: themeId
inputs.desc: The ID of a theme to apply to the shared drive. Cannot be set if colorRgb or backgroundImageFile are set.

inputs.name: colorRgb
inputs.desc: The color of this shared drive as an RGB hex string (e.g., "#FF0000"). Cannot be set if themeId is set.

inputs.name: restrictions
inputs.desc: A set of restrictions to apply to the shared drive.

inputs.name: backgroundImageFile
inputs.desc: An image file and cropping parameters for the shared drive's background. Cannot be set if themeId is set.

inputs.name: useDomainAdminAccess
inputs.desc: If set to true, the request is issued as a domain administrator.

name: Update Google event
description: Updates an existing event in Google Calendar. REQUIRES event_id - you MUST first search for the event using GOOGLECALENDAR_FIND_EVENT or GOOGLECALENDAR_EVENTS_LIST to obtain the event_id. This is a full PUT replacement: omitted fields (including attendees, reminders, recurrence, conferencing) are cleared. Always provide the complete desired event state. Use GOOGLECALENDAR_PATCH_EVENT instead for partial edits.

inputs.name: summary
inputs.desc: Summary (title) of the event.

inputs.name: event_id
inputs.desc: REQUIRED. The unique identifier of the event to update. This parameter is MANDATORY - events cannot be updated by title, date, or other criteria. You MUST first retrieve the event_id by using GOOGLECALENDAR_FIND_EVENT or GOOGLECALENDAR_EVENTS_LIST to search for the event, then use the returned 'id' field here.

inputs.name: location
inputs.desc: Geographic location of the event as free-form text.

inputs.name: timezone
inputs.desc: IANA timezone name from the timezone database (e.g., 'America/New_York', 'Europe/London', 'Asia/Jerusalem', 'UTC'). Required if datetime is naive. For recurring events, start and end must include a timeZone. If not provided, UTC is used. If datetime includes timezone info (Z or offset), this field is optional and defaults to UTC. IMPORTANT: Must be a valid IANA timezone identifier. Values like 'EST', 'PST', 'ISRAEL TIME', or other abbreviations are NOT valid IANA timezone names.

inputs.name: attendees
inputs.desc: List of attendees. Each attendee can be either: (1) A string email address (e.g., 'user@example.com'), or (2) An object with 'email' (required), 'optional' (boolean, default false), 'displayName' (string), 'comment' (string), 'additionalGuests' (integer), and 'resource' (boolean). To mark an attendee as optional (not required), use object format: {'email': 'user@example.com', 'optional': true}. IMPORTANT: Only valid email addresses are accepted. Plain names cannot be used.

inputs.name: eventType
inputs.desc: Type of the event, immutable post-creation. 'workingLocation' (REQUIRES Google Workspace Enterprise). Note: 'fromGmail' events cannot be created via API.

inputs.name: recurrence
inputs.desc: List of RRULE, EXRULE, RDATE, EXDATE lines for recurring events. Supported frequencies: DAILY, WEEKLY, MONTHLY, YEARLY. For recurring events, start.timeZone and end.timeZone must be present. UNTIL values follow RFC 5545: date-only (YYYYMMDD) for all-day events, or UTC datetime with Z suffix (YYYYMMDDTHHMMSSZ) for timed events. UNTIL values with time but missing Z suffix are auto-corrected. Provide an empty list to remove recurrence so the event becomes non-recurring.

inputs.name: visibility
inputs.desc: Event visibility: 'default', 'public', 'private', or 'confidential'.

inputs.name: calendar_id
inputs.desc: Identifier of the Google Calendar where the event resides. The value 'primary' targets the user's primary calendar.

inputs.name: description
inputs.desc: Description of the event. Can contain HTML. Optional. Must be omitted for 'birthday' event type.

inputs.name: end_datetime
inputs.desc: Event end time in ISO 8601 format: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS. When provided, this parameter takes precedence over event_duration_hour and event_duration_minutes. If not provided, the end time is calculated using start_datetime + duration. Must be after start_datetime. Fractional seconds and timezone info will be automatically stripped if provided. Examples: '2025-01-16T14:30:00', '2025-01-16T14:30'.

inputs.name: send_updates
inputs.desc: Options for who should receive notifications about event changes.

inputs.name: transparency
inputs.desc: 'opaque' (busy) or 'transparent' (available).

inputs.name: start_datetime
inputs.desc: REQUIRED. Event start time in ISO 8601 format: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS. IMPORTANT: Natural language expressions like 'tomorrow', 'next Monday', '2pm tomorrow' are NOT supported and will be rejected. You must provide the exact date and time in ISO format. Fractional seconds (e.g., .000) and timezone info (Z, +, -) will be automatically stripped if provided. Examples: '2025-01-16T13:00:00', '2025-01-16T13:00'.

inputs.name: guestsCanModify
inputs.desc: If True, guests can modify the event.

inputs.name: birthdayProperties
inputs.desc: Properties for birthday events.

inputs.name: create_meeting_room
inputs.desc: Defaults to True. When True, for CREATE operations creates a Google Meet link; for UPDATE operations preserves existing conference data if present, or adds a new Meet link if none exists. Google Workspace accounts will successfully receive a Meet link. Personal Gmail accounts and other unsupported accounts will gracefully fallback to creating an event without a Meet link when conference creation fails. Set to False to skip Meet link operations (won't create new or modify existing conference data). The fallback ensures event creation succeeds even when conference features are unavailable due to account limitations.

inputs.name: event_duration_hour
inputs.desc: Number of hours for the event duration. Supports multi-day events (e.g., 240 hours = 10 days). For durations under 1 hour, use event_duration_minutes instead. Ignored if end_datetime is provided.

inputs.name: extended_properties
inputs.desc: Extended properties of the event for storing custom metadata. Contains 'private' (visible only on this calendar) and/or 'shared' (visible to all attendees) dictionaries mapping string keys to string values. Example: {'private': {'key1': 'value1'}, 'shared': {'key2': 'value2'}}

inputs.name: focusTimeProperties
inputs.desc: Properties for focusTime events. REQUIRES Google Workspace Enterprise account with Focus Time feature enabled.

inputs.name: guestsCanInviteOthers
inputs.desc: Whether attendees other than the organizer can invite others to the event.

inputs.name: outOfOfficeProperties
inputs.desc: Properties for outOfOffice events.

inputs.name: event_duration_minutes
inputs.desc: Duration in minutes (0-59 ONLY). NEVER use 60+ minutes - use event_duration_hour=1 instead. Maximum value is 59. Combined duration (hours + minutes) must be greater than 0. Ignored if end_datetime is provided.

inputs.name: guestsCanSeeOtherGuests
inputs.desc: Whether attendees other than the organizer can see who the event's attendees are.

inputs.name: workingLocationProperties
inputs.desc:
Properties for workingLocation events. REQUIRES Google Workspace Enterprise.

Constraints discovered from testing:
- Must set transparency='transparent' and visibility='public'
- Description must be omitted
- Depending on 'type', include one of 'homeOffice', 'officeLocation', or 'customLocation'

name: Update existing document
description: Applies programmatic edits, such as text insertion, deletion, or formatting, to a specified Google Doc using the `batchUpdate` API method.

inputs.name: edit_docs
inputs.desc:
(Required) A list of requests to execute on the document. Each request must be a dict containing exactly ONE of the valid request types listed below.

Common request types:
replaceAllText, insertText, updateTextStyle, createParagraphBullets, deleteParagraphBullets, createNamedRange, deleteNamedRange, updateParagraphStyle, deleteContentRange, insertInlineImage, insertTable, insertTableRow, insertTableColumn, deleteTableRow, deleteTableColumn, insertPageBreak, deletePositionedObject, updateTableColumnProperties, updateTableCellStyle, updateTableRowStyle, replaceImage, updateDocumentStyle, mergeTableCells, unmergeTableCells, createHeader, createFooter, createFootnote, replaceNamedRangeContent, updateSectionStyle, insertSectionBreak, deleteHeader, deleteFooter, pinTableHeaderRows, addDocumentTab, deleteTab, updateDocumentTabProperties, insertPerson.

Do NOT use request types from other Google APIs (Slides, Sheets, etc.).
For full request schemas, see https://developers.google.com/docs/api/reference/rest/v1/documents/batchUpdate.

SEGMENT IDs: Use empty string "" for document body, or a header/footer/footnote ID from the document. Internal 'kix.' IDs are NOT valid segment IDs and will cause "Segment with ID ... was not found" errors. Call GOOGLEDOCS_GET_DOCUMENT_BY_ID to get valid IDs.

TAB IDs: When omitted, operations target the first tab. Invalid tab IDs cause errors. Get valid tab IDs from GOOGLEDOCS_GET_DOCUMENT_BY_ID at tabs[].tabProperties.tabId.

BATCH INSERT OPERATIONS: Operations execute sequentially. Each insertText shifts subsequent indices. For multiple inserts at specific positions, process in REVERSE index order (highest first). For appending, use endOfSegmentLocation for each insert. Auto-correction: insertText without location/endOfSegmentLocation automatically appends to document body.

INDEX BOUNDARIES: location.index must be LESS than the segment end index (valid: 1 to N-1). To insert at the end, use endOfSegmentLocation instead.

PARAGRAPH BOUNDARIES: Text can only be inserted inside existing paragraphs, not at structural boundaries (e.g., a table's start index). For table cells, add +1 or +2 to the cell's startIndex.

deleteContentRange: The range CANNOT include the trailing newline at segment end. If segment ends at N, use endIndex: N-1.

deleteTableRow: Requires tableCellLocation object (NOT top-level rowIndex/tableStartLocation). Structure: { tableCellLocation: { tableStartLocation: { index: N }, rowIndex: R, columnIndex: C } }.

updateTableCellStyle: Use EITHER tableStartLocation (all cells) OR tableRange (specific cells), not both.

Style updates (updateParagraphStyle, updateTextStyle, updateTableCellStyle, updateDocumentStyle): The 'fields' parameter is auto-populated from style keys if omitted.

Document title CANNOT be updated via batchUpdate - use Google Drive API instead.

updateDocumentStyle valid fields: background, marginTop, marginBottom, marginLeft, marginRight, marginHeader, marginFooter, pageSize, pageNumberStart, useEvenPageHeaderFooter, useFirstPageHeaderFooter, flipPageOrientation (NOT "title").

inputs.name: document_id
inputs.desc: (Required) The unique identifier of the Google Docs document to be updated.

name: Update File Metadata (PATCH v2)
description: Tool to update file metadata using the Drive API v2 PATCH method. Use when you need to modify file properties like title, description, or labels using patch semantics.

inputs.name: ocr
inputs.desc: Whether to attempt OCR on .jpg, .png, .gif, or .pdf uploads.

inputs.name: title
inputs.desc: The title of the file. Used to change the name of the file.

inputs.name: fileId
inputs.desc: The ID of the file to update.

inputs.name: labels
inputs.desc: A group of labels for the file. For example: {'starred': true, 'trashed': false, 'restricted': false, 'viewed': true}.

inputs.name: pinned
inputs.desc: Whether to pin the new revision. A file can have a maximum of 200 pinned revisions.

inputs.name: mimeType
inputs.desc: The MIME type of the file.

inputs.name: addParents
inputs.desc: Comma-separated list of parent IDs to add.

inputs.name: properties
inputs.desc: The list of properties.

inputs.name: description
inputs.desc: A short description of the file.

inputs.name: newRevision
inputs.desc: Whether a blob upload should create a new revision. If not set, a new revision is created.

inputs.name: ocrLanguage
inputs.desc: If ocr is true, hints at the language to use. Valid values are BCP 47 codes.

inputs.name: modifiedDate
inputs.desc: Last time this file was modified by anyone (RFC 3339 date-time). Requires setModifiedDate=true.

inputs.name: includeLabels
inputs.desc: A comma-separated list of IDs of labels to include in the labelInfo part of the response.

inputs.name: indexableText
inputs.desc: Indexable text attributes for the file (can be used to improve fulltext queries).

inputs.name: removeParents
inputs.desc: Comma-separated list of parent IDs to remove.

inputs.name: setModifiedDate
inputs.desc: Whether to set the modified date using the value supplied in the request body.

inputs.name: writersCanShare
inputs.desc: Whether writers can share the document with other users.

inputs.name: updateViewedDate
inputs.desc: Whether to update the view date after successfully updating the file.

inputs.name: supportsAllDrives
inputs.desc: Whether the requesting application supports both My Drives and shared drives. Defaults to true.

inputs.name: timedTextLanguage
inputs.desc: The language of the timed text.

inputs.name: timedTextTrackName
inputs.desc: The timed text track name.

inputs.name: includePermissionsForView
inputs.desc: Specifies which additional view's permissions to include in the response. Only 'published' is supported.

inputs.name: useContentAsIndexableText
inputs.desc: Whether to use the content as indexable text.

name: Update Property (v2 API)
description: Tool to update a property on a file using Google Drive API v2. Use when you need to modify an existing custom property attached to a file.

inputs.name: alt
inputs.desc: Data format for response.

inputs.name: key
inputs.desc: API key. Your API key identifies your project and provides you with API access, quota, and reports. Required unless you provide an OAuth 2.0 token.

inputs.name: xgafv
inputs.desc: V1 error format values.

inputs.name: fields
inputs.desc: Selector specifying which fields to include in a partial response.

inputs.name: fileId
inputs.desc: The ID of the file.

inputs.name: callback
inputs.desc: JSONP callback function name.

inputs.name: quotaUser
inputs.desc: Available to use for quota purposes for server-side applications. Can be any arbitrary string assigned to a user, but should not exceed 40 characters.

inputs.name: uploadType
inputs.desc: Legacy upload protocol for media (e.g. 'media', 'multipart').

inputs.name: visibility
inputs.desc: Visibility options for the property.

inputs.name: oauth_token
inputs.desc: OAuth 2.0 token for the current user.

inputs.name: prettyPrint
inputs.desc: Returns response with indentations and line breaks.

inputs.name: propertyKey
inputs.desc: The key of the property.

inputs.name: access_token
inputs.desc: OAuth access token.

inputs.name: property_value
inputs.desc: The value of this property.

inputs.name: upload_protocol
inputs.desc: Upload protocol for media (e.g. 'raw', 'multipart').

inputs.name: property_visibility
inputs.desc: Visibility options for the property.

name: Update File (Metadata)
description: Updates file metadata. Uses PATCH semantics (partial update) as per Google Drive API v3 — only explicitly provided fields are updated, so omit fields you do not intend to overwrite. Use this tool to modify attributes of an existing file like its name, description, or parent folders. To move a file, supply add_parents and remove_parents together; omitting remove_parents creates multiple parents, omitting add_parents can orphan the file. Bulk updates may trigger 429 Too Many Requests; apply exponential backoff. Note: supports metadata updates only; file content updates are not yet implemented.

inputs.name: name
inputs.desc: The name of the file. Google Drive does not enforce name uniqueness within a folder; duplicate names are allowed and can cause ambiguous results when searching by name.

inputs.name: fileId
inputs.desc: The ID of the file to update.

inputs.name: starred
inputs.desc: Whether the user has starred the file.

inputs.name: mime_type
inputs.desc: The MIME type of the file. Google Drive will attempt to automatically detect an appropriate value from uploaded content if no value is provided. The value cannot be changed unless a new revision is uploaded.

inputs.name: add_parents
inputs.desc: Comma-separated list of folder IDs (not folder names) to add as parents. Folder IDs are alphanumeric strings typically 20+ characters long (e.g., '1A2B3C4D5E6F7G8H9I0J'). Folder names will not work and will cause a 'Parent folder not found' error. Moving a file requires pairing with remove_parents (source folder ID); omitting remove_parents results in multiple parents. Reparenting to a shared folder changes collaborator access to that folder's permissions.

inputs.name: description
inputs.desc: A short description of the file.

inputs.name: ocr_language
inputs.desc: A language hint for OCR processing during image import (ISO 639-1 code).

inputs.name: remove_parents
inputs.desc: Comma-separated list of folder IDs (not folder names) to remove as parents. Folder IDs are alphanumeric strings typically 20+ characters long (e.g., '1A2B3C4D5E6F7G8H9I0J'). Folder names will not work and will cause a 'Parent folder not found' error.

inputs.name: writers_can_share
inputs.desc: Whether writers can share the document with other users.

inputs.name: supports_all_drives
inputs.desc: Whether the requesting application supports both My Drives and shared drives. Defaults to true to ensure compatibility with shared drive files.

inputs.name: keep_revision_forever
inputs.desc: Whether to set this revision of the file to be kept forever. This is only applicable to files with binary content in Google Drive. Only 200 revisions for the file can be kept forever. If the limit is reached, try deleting pinned revisions.

inputs.name: use_domain_admin_access
inputs.desc: Whether the requesting application is using domain-wide delegation to access content belonging to a user in a different domain. This is only applicable to files with binary content in Google Drive.

inputs.name: viewers_can_copy_content
inputs.desc: Whether viewers are prevented from copying content of the file.

name: Update File Revision Metadata
description: Updates ONLY the metadata properties of a specific file revision (keepForever, published, publishAuto, publishedOutsideDomain). IMPORTANT: This action does NOT update file content. To update file content, use EDIT_FILE or UPDATE_FILE_PUT instead. This action requires BOTH file_id AND revision_id parameters. Use LIST_REVISIONS to get available revision IDs for a file. Valid parameters: file_id (required), revision_id (required), keep_forever, published, publish_auto, published_outside_domain. Invalid parameters (use other actions): file_contents, mime_type, content, name - these are NOT supported by this action.

inputs.name: file_id
inputs.desc: Required. The ID of the file whose revision metadata you want to update. Use LIST_FILES or FIND_FILE to get the file ID.

inputs.name: published
inputs.desc: Whether this revision is published. This is only applicable to Docs Editors files.

inputs.name: keepForever
inputs.desc: Whether to keep this revision forever, even if it is no longer the head revision. If not set, the revision will be automatically purged 30 days after newer content is uploaded. This can be set on a maximum of 200 revisions for a file. This field is only applicable to files with binary content in Drive.

inputs.name: publishAuto
inputs.desc: Whether subsequent revisions will be automatically republished. This is only applicable to Docs Editors files.

inputs.name: revision_id
inputs.desc: Required. The ID of the revision to update. Use LIST_REVISIONS to get available revision IDs for a file.

inputs.name: publishedOutsideDomain
inputs.desc: Whether this revision is published outside the domain. This is only applicable to Docs Editors files.

name: Update IMAP settings
description: Tool to update IMAP settings for a Gmail account. Use when you need to modify IMAP configuration such as enabling/disabling IMAP, setting auto-expunge behavior, or configuring folder size limits.

inputs.name: enabled
inputs.desc: Whether IMAP is enabled for the account.

inputs.name: user_id
inputs.desc: The user's email address. The special value 'me' can be used to indicate the authenticated user.

inputs.name: autoExpunge
inputs.desc: If this value is true, Gmail will immediately expunge a message when it is marked as deleted in IMAP. Otherwise, Gmail will wait for an update from the client before expunging messages marked as deleted.

inputs.name: maxFolderSize
inputs.desc: An optional limit on the number of messages that an IMAP folder may contain. Legal values are 0, 1000, 2000, 5000 or 10000. A value of zero is interpreted to mean that there is no limit.

inputs.name: expungeBehavior
inputs.desc: The action that will be executed on a message when it is marked as deleted and expunged from the last visible IMAP folder. Possible values: 'expungeBehaviorUnspecified' (Unspecified behavior), 'archive' (Archive messages marked as deleted), 'trash' (Move messages marked as deleted to the trash), 'deleteForever' (Immediately and permanently delete messages marked as deleted).

name: Update Label
description: Tool to update the properties of an existing Gmail label. Use when you need to modify label name, visibility settings, or color.

inputs.name: id
inputs.desc: The ID of the label to update.

inputs.name: name
inputs.desc: The display name of the label.

inputs.name: color
inputs.desc: Color settings for the label. Both backgroundColor and textColor must be provided together.

inputs.name: userId
inputs.desc: The user's email address. The special value `me` can be used to indicate the authenticated user.

inputs.name: labelListVisibility
inputs.desc: Visibility of the label in the label list (Gmail sidebar).

inputs.name: messageListVisibility
inputs.desc: Visibility of messages with this label in the message list.

name: Update Language Settings
description: Tool to update the language settings for a Gmail user. Use when you need to change the display language preference for the authenticated user or a specific Gmail account. The returned displayLanguage may differ from the requested value if Gmail selects a close variant.

inputs.name: user_id
inputs.desc: The email address of the Gmail user whose language settings are to be updated, or the special value 'me' to indicate the currently authenticated user.

inputs.name: display_language
inputs.desc: The language to display Gmail in, formatted as an RFC 3066 Language Tag (e.g., 'en-GB' for British English, 'fr' for French, 'ja' for Japanese, 'es' for Spanish, 'de' for German, 'en' for English). The set of languages supported by Gmail evolves over time. Note: Gmail may save a close variant if the requested language is not directly supported. For example, if you request a regional variant that's not available, Gmail may save the base language instead.

name: Update Media Item
description: Updates a media item's description in Google Photos.

inputs.name: description
inputs.desc: New description for the media item. Must be shorter than 1000 characters.

inputs.name: mediaItemId
inputs.desc: Identifier of the media item to update

name: Update Permission
description: Tool to update a permission with patch semantics. Use when you need to modify an existing permission for a file or shared drive. Inherited or domain-managed permissions may not be editable; verify editability with GOOGLEDRIVE_LIST_PERMISSIONS before updating.

inputs.name: fileId
inputs.desc: The ID of the file or shared drive.

inputs.name: permission
inputs.desc: The permission resource to update. Only 'role' and 'expirationTime' can be updated. Role changes take effect immediately and can be difficult to reverse; confirm intent before applying.

inputs.name: permissionId
inputs.desc: The ID of the permission. For anyone-type permissions, use 'anyone' as the permission ID.

inputs.name: removeExpiration
inputs.desc: Whether to remove the expiration date.

inputs.name: supportsAllDrives
inputs.desc: Whether the requesting application supports both My Drives and shared drives. Must be set to true when operating on shared drives; omitting this causes the request to fail.

inputs.name: transferOwnership
inputs.desc: Whether to transfer ownership to the specified user and downgrade the current owner to a writer. This parameter is required as an acknowledgement of the side effect when set to true.

inputs.name: useDomainAdminAccess
inputs.desc: Issue the request as a domain administrator; if set to true, then the requester will be granted access if the file ID parameter refers to a shared drive and the requester is an administrator of the domain to which the shared drive belongs.

inputs.name: enforceExpansiveAccess
inputs.desc: Whether the request should enforce expansive access rules. This field is deprecated, it is recommended to use `permissionDetails` instead.

name: Update POP settings
description: Tool to update POP settings for a Gmail account. Use when you need to configure POP access window or message disposition behavior.

inputs.name: user_id
inputs.desc: The user's email address. The special value 'me' can be used to indicate the authenticated user.

inputs.name: disposition
inputs.desc: The action that will be executed on a message after it has been fetched via POP.

inputs.name: access_window
inputs.desc: The range of messages which are accessible via POP.

name: Update Property
description: Tool to update an existing GA4 Property. Use when you need to modify property settings such as display name, time zone, currency code, or industry category.

inputs.name: name
inputs.desc: Required. The resource name of the Property to update. Must be in the exact format: properties/{property_id} where property_id is a numeric identifier. IMPORTANT: Must start with 'properties/' prefix, contain no forward slashes in the property_id, have no trailing slashes, and no additional path segments. Valid example: 'properties/489591273'. Invalid examples: '489591273' (missing prefix), 'properties/489591273/' (trailing slash), 'properties/489591273/extra' (extra segments)

inputs.name: parent
inputs.desc: Immutable. Resource name of this property's logical parent. Format: accounts/{account}, properties/{property}. Example: 'accounts/100', 'properties/101'

inputs.name: timeZone
inputs.desc: Required. Reporting Time Zone, used as the day boundary for reports. Format: IANA time zone. Example: 'America/Los_Angeles'

inputs.name: updateMask
inputs.desc: Required. The list of fields to be updated. Field names must be in snake case (e.g., 'display_name', 'time_zone', 'currency_code'). Omitted fields will not be updated. To replace the entire entity, use one path with the string '*' to match all fields. Common fields: 'display_name', 'time_zone', 'currency_code', 'industry_category'

inputs.name: displayName
inputs.desc: Human-readable display name for this property. The max allowed display name length is 100 UTF-16 code units.

inputs.name: currencyCode
inputs.desc: The currency type used in reports involving monetary values. Format: ISO 4217. Examples: 'USD', 'EUR', 'JPY'

inputs.name: propertyType
inputs.desc: Property type for a property.

inputs.name: industryCategory
inputs.desc: Industry category for a property.

name: Update Reply
description: Tool to update a reply to a comment on a Google Drive file. Use when you need to modify the content of an existing reply.

inputs.name: fields
inputs.desc: Selector specifying which fields to include in a partial response. If not provided, defaults to '*' to return all fields.

inputs.name: content
inputs.desc: The new plain text content of the reply.

inputs.name: file_id
inputs.desc: The ID of the file.

inputs.name: reply_id
inputs.desc: The ID of the reply.

inputs.name: comment_id
inputs.desc: The ID of the comment.

name: Update send-as alias
description: Tool to update a send-as alias for a Gmail user. Use when you need to modify display name, signature, reply-to address, or SMTP settings for a send-as email address. Gmail sanitizes HTML signatures before saving. Addresses other than the primary can only be updated by service accounts with domain-wide authority.

inputs.name: user_id
inputs.desc: The email address of the Gmail user whose send-as alias to update, or the special value 'me' to indicate the authenticated user.

inputs.name: smtp_msa
inputs.desc: SMTP relay configuration for the send-as alias.

inputs.name: signature
inputs.desc: Optional HTML signature for messages composed with this alias in Gmail web UI. Gmail sanitizes HTML before saving. Only added to new emails.

inputs.name: is_default
inputs.desc: Set to true to make this the default 'From:' address for composing messages and vacation auto-replies. Setting true makes the previous default false. Only legal writable value is true.

inputs.name: display_name
inputs.desc: Name to appear in 'From:' header. For custom from addresses, Gmail populates with primary account name if empty. Admin restrictions may silently fail updates to primary login name.

inputs.name: send_as_email
inputs.desc: The send-as alias email address to update. This is the email address that appears in the 'From:' header.

inputs.name: treat_as_alias
inputs.desc: Whether Gmail treats this address as an alias for the user's primary email. Only applies to custom from aliases.

inputs.name: reply_to_address
inputs.desc: Optional email address for 'Reply-To:' header. Gmail omits header if empty.

name: Update Sheet Properties
description: Tool to update properties of a sheet (worksheet) within a Google Spreadsheet, such as its title, index, visibility, tab color, or grid properties. Use this when you need to modify the metadata or appearance of a specific sheet.

inputs.name: spreadsheetId
inputs.desc: The ID of the spreadsheet containing the sheet to update.

inputs.name: responseRanges
inputs.desc: Limits the ranges included in the response spreadsheet. Meaningful only if includeSpreadsheetInResponse is true. Ranges should be in A1 notation (e.g., 'Sheet1!A1:B2').

inputs.name: updateSheetProperties
inputs.desc: The details of the sheet properties to update.

inputs.name: responseIncludeGridData
inputs.desc: True if grid data should be returned. Meaningful only if includeSpreadsheetInResponse is true. When true, the response will include cell data for the specified ranges.

inputs.name: includeSpreadsheetInResponse
inputs.desc: Determines if the update response should include the spreadsheet resource. When true, the response will include the full updated spreadsheet.

name: Update Google Meet Space
description: Updates the settings of an existing Google Meet space. Requires organizer/host privileges and the meetings.space.created OAuth scope. REQUIRED PARAMETER: - name: The space identifier (e.g., 'spaces/jQCFfuBOdN5z'). This is always required to identify which space to update. OPTIONAL PARAMETERS: - config: The new configuration settings to apply (accessType, entryPointAccess, moderation, etc.) - updateMask: Specify which fields to update. If omitted, all provided config fields are updated. Example: To change access type, provide name='spaces/abc123' and config={'accessType': 'OPEN'}

inputs.name: name
inputs.desc: REQUIRED. The unique identifier of the Google Meet space to update. Format: 'spaces/{space}' where {space} is the space ID.

inputs.name: config
inputs.desc: Configuration settings to update for the meeting space. Provide the fields you want to modify (e.g., accessType, entryPointAccess, moderation settings). Some settings (moderation modes, attendanceReportGenerationType, artifact generation) require specific Google Workspace editions and admin policies; unsupported settings may apply silently with no effect. Certain combinations of accessType, entryPointAccess, or moderation values may be rejected based on domain or account policies.

inputs.name: updateMask
inputs.desc: Field mask specifying which fields to update. Comma-separated list of fully qualified field names (e.g., "config.accessType,config.entryPointAccess"). If not provided, only the fields you explicitly set in config will be updated. WARNING: Using "*" will attempt to update ALL config fields, which may cause errors if required fields like attendanceReportGenerationType are not provided. Recommend specifying only the fields being updated.

name: Update Spreadsheet Properties
description: Tool to update SPREADSHEET-LEVEL properties such as the spreadsheet's title, locale, time zone, or auto-recalculation settings. Use when you need to modify the overall configuration of a Google Spreadsheet. NOTE: To update individual SHEET properties (like renaming a specific sheet/tab), use GOOGLESHEETS_UPDATE_SHEET_PROPERTIES instead.

inputs.name: fields
inputs.desc: Field mask specifying which properties to update (comma-separated for multiple fields). Supports nested paths using dot notation (e.g., 'iterativeCalculationSettings.maxIterations') per Protocol Buffers FieldMask specification. The root 'properties' is implied and must not be included. Special case: When updating 'spreadsheetTheme', use the field mask 'spreadsheetTheme' (not nested paths like 'spreadsheetTheme.primaryFontFamily') and provide the complete theme object with all required fields. Wildcard '*' updates all properties.

inputs.name: properties
inputs.desc: The spreadsheet-level properties to update (e.g., title, locale, timeZone, autoRecalc). At least one field within properties must be set. NOTE: To update individual sheet/tab properties (like renaming a specific sheet), use GOOGLESHEETS_UPDATE_SHEET_PROPERTIES instead.

inputs.name: spreadsheetId
inputs.desc: The ID of the spreadsheet to update.

inputs.name: responseRanges
inputs.desc: Limits the ranges included in the response spreadsheet. Only meaningful if includeSpreadsheetInResponse is true. Ranges should be in A1 notation (e.g., 'Sheet1!A1:B2').

inputs.name: responseIncludeGridData
inputs.desc: Determines if grid data (cell values) should be included in the response. Only meaningful if includeSpreadsheetInResponse is true. When true, the response will include cell data for the specified ranges or entire spreadsheet.

inputs.name: includeSpreadsheetInResponse
inputs.desc: Determines if the update response should include the full spreadsheet resource. When true, the response will include the entire updated spreadsheet with all sheets, properties, and metadata.

name: Update Table Row Style
description: Tool to update the style of a table row in a Google Document. Use when you need to modify the appearance of specific rows within a table, such as setting minimum row height or marking rows as headers.

inputs.name: documentId
inputs.desc: The ID of the document to update.

inputs.name: updateTableRowStyle
inputs.desc: The request body for updating the table row style.

name: Update Task (Deprecated)
description: DEPRECATED: Use GOOGLETASKS_PATCH_TASK instead. Full-update (PUT-style) operation that overwrites unspecified fields with empty/default values, which can cause data loss. Prefer GOOGLETASKS_PATCH_TASK unless a complete field replacement is explicitly required.

inputs.name: due
inputs.desc: Due date of the task (RFC 3339 timestamp).

inputs.name: notes
inputs.desc: Notes describing the task.

inputs.name: title
inputs.desc: Title of the task.

inputs.name: status
inputs.desc: Status of the task. Valid values: "needsAction" or "completed". When setting to "completed", the completion date will be auto-set to the current time if not explicitly provided via the completed field.

inputs.name: task_id
inputs.desc: Task identifier.

inputs.name: completed
inputs.desc: Completion date of the task (RFC 3339 timestamp).

inputs.name: tasklist_id
inputs.desc: Task list identifier.

name: Update Task (Full Replacement)
description: Tool to fully replace an existing Google Task using PUT method. Use when you need to update the entire task resource, not just specific fields. This method requires all required fields (id, title) and replaces the complete task, unlike PATCH which supports partial updates.

inputs.name: id
inputs.desc: Task identifier (REQUIRED). Must match the task_id parameter. This field is required by the Google Tasks API for PUT operations.

inputs.name: due
inputs.desc: Due date for the task. NOTE: Google Tasks only stores the date portion (YYYY-MM-DD). Accepts RFC3339 format (e.g., '2025-09-28T00:00:00Z') or date strings like '2025-09-28'.

inputs.name: etag
inputs.desc: ETag of the resource for concurrency control. Prevents accidental overwrites when multiple clients modify the same task.

inputs.name: links
inputs.desc: Collection of links attached to the task. This collection is read-only.

inputs.name: notes
inputs.desc: Additional details or description for the task. Supports plain text only. Maximum 8192 characters.

inputs.name: title
inputs.desc: Title/name of the task (REQUIRED). This is the main text displayed for the task. Maximum 1024 characters.

inputs.name: hidden
inputs.desc: Whether the task is hidden. This is read-only and set by the API when task list is cleared. Default: false

inputs.name: parent
inputs.desc: Parent task identifier for subtasks. This field is read-only. Use the 'move' method to change parent.

inputs.name: status
inputs.desc: Current status of the task. Must be either 'needsAction' (task is pending) or 'completed' (task is done).

inputs.name: deleted
inputs.desc: Whether the task has been deleted. Deleted tasks are marked but not immediately removed. Default: false

inputs.name: task_id
inputs.desc: Identifier of the Google Task to be fully replaced within the specified task list. This must also be included in the 'id' field of the request body.

inputs.name: position
inputs.desc: Position of the task among siblings. This field is read-only. Use the 'move' method to change position.

inputs.name: completed
inputs.desc: Date/time when the task was marked as completed. Only applicable when status='completed'. Unlike 'due', this field preserves the time component. Accepts RFC3339 format.

inputs.name: tasklist_id
inputs.desc: Identifier of the Google Task list that contains the task to be updated.

name: Update Task List
description: Updates the authenticated user's specified task list.

inputs.name: title
inputs.desc: Title of the task list. Maximum length allowed: 1024 characters.

inputs.name: tasklist_id
inputs.desc: Task list identifier.

name: Update Team Drive (Deprecated)
description: Tool to update a Team Drive's metadata. Deprecated: Use the drives.update endpoint instead. Use when you need to modify Team Drive properties.

inputs.name: name
inputs.desc: The name of this Team Drive.

inputs.name: themeId
inputs.desc: The ID of the theme from which the background image and color will be set. This is a write-only field; it can only be set on requests that don't set colorRgb or backgroundImageFile.

inputs.name: colorRgb
inputs.desc: The color of this Team Drive as an RGB hex string. It can only be set on a drive.teamdrives.update request that does not set themeId.

inputs.name: teamDriveId
inputs.desc: The ID of the Team Drive to update.

inputs.name: restrictions
inputs.desc: A set of restrictions that apply to this Team Drive or items inside this Team Drive.

inputs.name: backgroundImageFile
inputs.desc: An image file and cropping parameters from which a background image for this Team Drive is set. This is a write only field; it can only be set on drive.teamdrives.update requests that don't set themeId.

inputs.name: useDomainAdminAccess
inputs.desc: Issue the request as a domain administrator; if set to true, then the requester will be granted access if they are an administrator of the domain to which the Team Drive belongs.

name: Update User Attributes Values
description: Update user attribute values for a resource. Use this action to set or update custom attributes for a user within an organization or project. When setting a value for an attribute key that also exists in SAML, the Sanity value will take precedence and shadow the SAML value.

inputs.name: userId
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the user whose attributes to update.

inputs.name: attributes
inputs.desc: A dictionary of attribute key-value pairs to set for the user. Values can be strings, numbers, booleans, arrays, or nested objects. These will shadow any SAML values for the same keys.

inputs.name: resourceId
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the resource. For organizations, this is the organization ID.

inputs.name: resourceType
inputs.desc: The type of resource that scopes the user attributes (e.g., 'organization' or 'project').

name: Update Vacation Settings
description: Tool to update vacation responder settings for a Gmail user. Use when you need to configure out-of-office auto-replies.

inputs.name: userId
inputs.desc: The user's email address. The special value 'me' can be used to indicate the authenticated user.

inputs.name: endTime
inputs.desc: An optional end time for sending auto-replies (epoch ms). When this is specified, Gmail will automatically reply only to messages that it receives before the end time. If both startTime and endTime are specified, startTime must precede endTime.

inputs.name: startTime
inputs.desc: An optional start time for sending auto-replies (epoch ms). When this is specified, Gmail will automatically reply only to messages that it receives after the start time. If both startTime and endTime are specified, startTime must precede endTime.

inputs.name: enableAutoReply
inputs.desc: Flag that controls whether Gmail automatically replies to messages.

inputs.name: responseSubject
inputs.desc: Optional text to prepend to the subject line in vacation responses. In order to enable auto-replies, either the response subject or the response body must be nonempty.

inputs.name: responseBodyHtml
inputs.desc: Response body in HTML format. Gmail will sanitize the HTML before storing it. If both response_body_plain_text and response_body_html are specified, response_body_html will be used.

inputs.name: restrictToDomain
inputs.desc: Flag that determines whether responses are sent to recipients who are outside of the user's domain. This feature is only available for Google Workspace users.

inputs.name: restrictToContacts
inputs.desc: Flag that determines whether responses are sent to recipients who are not in the user's list of contacts.

inputs.name: responseBodyPlainText
inputs.desc: Response body in plain text format. If both response_body_plain_text and response_body_html are specified, response_body_html will be used.

name: Batch update spreadsheet values
description: Tool to set values in one or more ranges of a spreadsheet. Use when you need to update multiple ranges in a single operation for better performance.

inputs.name: data
inputs.desc: The new values to apply to the spreadsheet. Each ValueRange specifies a range and the values to write to that range. Multiple ranges can be updated in a single request.

inputs.name: spreadsheet_id
inputs.desc: The ID of the spreadsheet to update. This ID can be found in the URL of the spreadsheet (e.g., https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/{spreadsheet_id}/edit). Must be a valid Google Sheets spreadsheet ID.

inputs.name: valueInputOption
inputs.desc: How the input data should be interpreted. RAW: Values are stored exactly as entered, without parsing. USER_ENTERED: Values are parsed as if typed by a user (numbers stay numbers, strings prefixed with '=' become formulas, etc.). INPUT_VALUE_OPTION_UNSPECIFIED: Default input value option is not specified.

inputs.name: includeValuesInResponse
inputs.desc: Determines if the update response should include the values of the cells that were updated. By default, responses do not include the updated values.

inputs.name: responseValueRenderOption
inputs.desc: Determines how values in the response should be rendered. Only used if includeValuesInResponse is true. FORMATTED_VALUE (default): Values are formatted as displayed in the UI. UNFORMATTED_VALUE: Values are unformatted. FORMULA: Formulas are not evaluated.

inputs.name: responseDateTimeRenderOption
inputs.desc: Determines how dates, times, and durations in the response should be rendered. Only used if includeValuesInResponse is true. SERIAL_NUMBER (default): Dates are returned as numbers. FORMATTED_STRING: Dates are returned as formatted strings.

name: Upload File
description: Uploads a file (max 5MB) to Google Drive, placing it in the specified folder or root if no valid folder ID is provided. Always creates a new file (never updates existing); use GOOGLEDRIVE_EDIT_FILE to update with a stable file_id. Uploaded files are private by default; configure sharing via GOOGLEDRIVE_ADD_FILE_SHARING_PREFERENCE.

inputs.name: file_to_upload
inputs.desc: File to upload to Google Drive (max 5MB). Must be a dict with fields: `name` (sanitized filename, no slashes or control characters), `mimetype` (accurate MIME type, e.g. `application/pdf`; incorrect values cause Drive to convert or misrender the file), and `s3key` (path from a previously staged Composio object — not an s3url, not a local path, not a fabricated key). When chaining with TEXT_TO_PDF_CONVERT_TEXT_TO_PDF, pass the returned `s3key` field, not `s3url`.

inputs.name: folder_to_upload_to
inputs.desc: Optional ID of the target Google Drive folder; can be obtained using 'Find Folder' or similar actions. Invalid or missing IDs silently fall back to Drive root with no error — resolve the correct folder ID first using GOOGLEDRIVE_FIND_FILE.

name: Upload File from URL to Drive
description: Tool to fetch a file from a provided URL server-side and upload it into Google Drive. Use when you need to reliably persist externally hosted files into Drive without client-side downloads or temporary storage.

inputs.name: name
inputs.desc: Name for the file in Google Drive, including extension (e.g., 'report.pdf', 'image.png').

inputs.name: mime_type
inputs.desc: Target MIME type for the file in Google Drive. If not specified, Drive auto-detects from content. Google Workspace MIME types (application/vnd.google-apps.*) trigger automatic conversion from compatible source formats: google-apps.document converts Word/ODT/HTML/RTF/TXT/PDF/images (OCR); google-apps.spreadsheet converts Excel/ODS/CSV/TSV; google-apps.presentation converts PowerPoint/ODP. Conversion requires the source content to be in a compatible format. Incompatible formats will cause upload errors.

inputs.name: source_url
inputs.desc: URL of the file to download and upload to Google Drive. Must be a publicly accessible URL or include necessary authentication in source_headers.

inputs.name: verify_ssl
inputs.desc: Whether to verify SSL certificates when downloading from HTTPS URLs. Set to false to bypass SSL verification for URLs with certificate issues (expired certificates, hostname mismatches, self-signed certificates). Only disable for trusted sources.

inputs.name: source_headers
inputs.desc: Optional HTTP headers to include when downloading from source_url. Use for authentication tokens, signed URLs, or CDN-specific headers.

inputs.name: parent_folder_id
inputs.desc: ID of the parent folder in Google Drive. If not specified, the file will be uploaded to the root of My Drive.

inputs.name: supports_all_drives
inputs.desc: Whether the request supports both My Drives and shared drives. Defaults to true for broader compatibility.

name: Upload Media
description: Upload a media file to Google Photos. Supports images (up to 200MB) and videos (up to 20GB).

inputs.name: url
inputs.desc: Public URL of the media file to upload (must be directly accessible). The action will download the file from this URL and upload it to Google Photos. Alternative to 'file_to_upload'. Supported formats: images (JPEG, PNG, GIF, HEIC, etc.) and videos (MP4, MOV, etc.).

inputs.name: file_name
inputs.desc: File name for the uploaded media. Required when using 'url' parameter. Should include the file extension (e.g., 'photo.jpg', 'video.mp4').

inputs.name: description
inputs.desc: Optional description for the media item

inputs.name: file_to_upload
inputs.desc: The media file to upload to Google Photos.

name: Upload/Update File Content
description: Tool to update file content in Google Drive by uploading new binary content. Use when you need to replace the contents of an existing file with new file data.

inputs.name: fileId
inputs.desc: The ID of the file to update with new content.

inputs.name: addParents
inputs.desc: Comma-separated list of parent folder IDs to add.

inputs.name: uploadType
inputs.desc: The type of upload request. 'media' for simple upload (content only), 'multipart' for metadata + content, 'resumable' for large files.

inputs.name: ocrLanguage
inputs.desc: Language hint for OCR processing (ISO 639-1 code, e.g., 'en').

inputs.name: removeParents
inputs.desc: Comma-separated list of parent folder IDs to remove.

inputs.name: file_to_upload
inputs.desc: The file content to upload.

inputs.name: supportsAllDrives
inputs.desc: Whether the app supports both My Drives and shared drives. Defaults to true.

inputs.name: keepRevisionForever
inputs.desc: Whether to set the 'keepForever' field in the new head revision.

inputs.name: useContentAsIndexableText
inputs.desc: Whether to use the uploaded content as indexable text for search.

name: Upsert Rows (Smart Update/Insert)
description: Upsert rows - update existing rows by key, append new ones. Automatically handles column mapping and partial updates. Use for: CRM syncs (match Lead ID), transaction imports (match Transaction ID), inventory updates (match SKU), calendar syncs (match Event ID). Features: - Auto-adds missing columns to sheet - Partial column updates (only update Phone + Status, preserve other columns) - Column order doesn't matter (auto-maps by header name) - Prevents duplicates by matching key column Example inputs: - Contact update: keyColumn='Email', headers=['Email','Phone','Status'], data=[['john@ex.com','555-0101','Active']] - Inventory sync: keyColumn='SKU', headers=['SKU','Stock','Price'], data=[['WIDGET-001',50,9.99],['GADGET-002',30,19.99]] - CRM lead update: keyColumn='Lead ID', headers=['Lead ID','Score','Status'], data=[['L-12345',85,'Hot']] - Partial update: keyColumn='Email', headers=['Email','Phone'] (only updates Phone, preserves Name/Address/etc)

inputs.name: rows
inputs.desc: 2D array of data rows to upsert. IMPORTANT: If 'headers' is NOT provided, the FIRST row is treated as column headers and remaining rows as data - so you need at least 2 rows (1 header + 1 data). If 'headers' IS provided separately, then ALL rows in this array are treated as data rows. Each row should have the same number of values as headers. If a row has MORE values than headers: with strict_mode=true (default), an error is returned showing which rows are affected; with strict_mode=false, extra values are silently truncated. If a row has FEWER values than headers, an error is returned during execution. Cell values can be strings, numbers, booleans, or null. Example with headers provided: headers=['Email','Status'], rows=[['john@ex.com','Active']] (1 data row). Example without headers: rows=[['Email','Status'],['john@ex.com','Active']] (row 1 = headers, row 2 = data).

inputs.name: headers
inputs.desc: List of column names for the data. These will be matched against sheet headers. If a column doesn't exist in the sheet, it will be added automatically. Order doesn't need to match sheet order. Can be auto-derived from the first row in 'rows' if not provided. Example inputs: ['Email', 'Phone', 'Status'] for contact updates, ['Lead ID', 'Name', 'Score'] for CRM, ['SKU', 'Stock', 'Price'] for inventory.

inputs.name: keyColumn
inputs.desc: The column NAME (header text) to use as unique identifier for matching rows. Must be an actual header name from the sheet (e.g., 'Email', 'Lead ID', 'SKU'), NOT a column letter (e.g., 'A', 'B', 'C'). If you provide a column letter like 'A', it will be automatically converted to the header name at that column position. If neither 'key_column' nor 'key_column_index' is provided, defaults to the first column (index 0).

inputs.name: sheetName
inputs.desc: The name of the sheet/tab within the spreadsheet. Note: Google Sheets creates default sheets with localized names based on account language (e.g., 'Sheet1' for English, '工作表1' for Chinese, 'Hoja1' for Spanish, 'Feuille1' for French, 'Planilha1' for Portuguese, 'Лист1' for Russian). If you specify a common default name and the sheet is not found, the action will automatically use the first sheet if only one exists.

inputs.name: strictMode
inputs.desc: Controls how rows with mismatched column counts are handled. When True (default), an error is returned if any row has more values than headers - the error message shows exactly which rows are affected and what values would need to be removed. When False, extra values are silently truncated to match the header count. Set to False only if you explicitly want automatic truncation of extra values.

inputs.name: tableStart
inputs.desc: Cell where the table starts (where headers are located). Defaults to 'A1'. Use this if your table is offset (e.g., 'C5', 'D10').

inputs.name: spreadsheetId
inputs.desc: The ID of the spreadsheet. Must be a non-empty string, typically a 44-character alphanumeric string found in the spreadsheet URL.

inputs.name: key_column_index
inputs.desc: The 0-based column index to use as unique identifier for matching rows. Alternative to 'key_column' - will be converted to column name using headers. If neither 'key_column' nor 'key_column_index' is provided, defaults to 0 (first column). Example: 0 for first column, 1 for second column.

inputs.name: normalization_message
inputs.desc: Internal field to track input normalization (e.g., row truncation). Not part of API.

name: Validate Events
description: Tool to validate Measurement Protocol events before sending them to production. Use when you need to verify event structure and parameters are correct before sending real data.

inputs.name: events
inputs.desc: Required. Array of events to validate. Each event must have a 'name' field and optional 'params' object.

inputs.name: consent
inputs.desc: Optional. The consent state for the user.

inputs.name: user_id
inputs.desc: Optional. A unique identifier for a user.

inputs.name: client_id
inputs.desc: Required. Unique client identifier. This uniquely identifies a user instance of a web client.

inputs.name: api_secret
inputs.desc: Required. The API secret from Google Analytics for the Measurement Protocol.

inputs.name: measurement_id
inputs.desc: Required. The measurement ID for web streams. Format: G-XXXXXXXXXX where X is alphanumeric.

inputs.name: user_properties
inputs.desc: Optional. The user properties for the measurement.

inputs.name: timestamp_micros
inputs.desc: Optional. A Unix timestamp (in microseconds) for the time to associate with the event.

name: Get spreadsheet values
description: Returns a range of values from a spreadsheet. Use when you need to read data from specific cells or ranges in a Google Sheet.

inputs.name: range
inputs.desc: The A1 notation or R1C1 notation of the range to retrieve values from. If the sheet name contains spaces or special characters, wrap the sheet name in single quotes (e.g., "'My Sheet'!A1:B2"). Without single quotes, the API will return a 400 error for sheet names with spaces. Examples: 'Sheet1!A1:B3', "'Sheet With Spaces'!A1:D5", 'A1:D5' (no sheet name uses first visible sheet), 'Sheet1!A:A' (entire column), 'SheetName' (entire sheet).

inputs.name: end_row
inputs.desc: 1-based row number to stop reading at (inclusive). Use with start_row for pagination to avoid large response errors. Example: start_row=501, end_row=1000 fetches rows 501-1000.

inputs.name: start_row
inputs.desc: 1-based row number to start reading from (inclusive). Use with end_row for pagination to avoid large response errors. Example: start_row=1, end_row=500 fetches the first 500 rows.

inputs.name: spreadsheet_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the Google Spreadsheet from which to retrieve values. This is the long alphanumeric string found in the spreadsheet URL between '/d/' and '/edit' (e.g., '1BxiMVs0XRA5nFMdKvBdBZjgmUUqptlbs74OgvE2upms'). WARNING: Do NOT use the spreadsheet name or title (e.g., 'My Sales Report'); you must use the actual ID from the URL.

inputs.name: major_dimension
inputs.desc: The major dimension for results.

inputs.name: value_render_option
inputs.desc: How values should be rendered in the output. FORMATTED_VALUE: Values are calculated and formatted (default). UNFORMATTED_VALUE: Values are calculated but not formatted. FORMULA: Values are not calculated; the formula is returned instead.

inputs.name: date_time_render_option
inputs.desc: How dates, times, and durations should be represented in the output. SERIAL_NUMBER: Dates are returned as serial numbers (default). FORMATTED_STRING: Dates returned as formatted strings.

name: Update spreadsheet values
description: Tool to set values in a range of a Google Spreadsheet. Use when you need to update or overwrite existing cell values in a specific range.

inputs.name: range
inputs.desc: The A1 notation of the range to update values in (e.g., 'Sheet1!A1:C2', 'MySheet!C:C', or 'A1:D5'). Must be actual cell references, not placeholder values. If the sheet name is omitted (e.g., 'A1:B2'), the operation applies to the first visible sheet. IMPORTANT: The range must not exceed the sheet's grid dimensions. By default, new sheets have 1000 rows and 26 columns (A-Z). If you need to write to columns beyond Z (e.g., AA, AB), first expand the sheet using GOOGLESHEETS_APPEND_DIMENSION or check the current dimensions using GOOGLESHEETS_GET_SPREADSHEET_INFO.

inputs.name: values
inputs.desc: The data to write. This is an array of arrays, the outer array representing all the data and each inner array representing a major dimension. Each item in the inner array corresponds with one cell.

inputs.name: spreadsheet_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the Google Spreadsheet to update. This ID can be found in the URL of the spreadsheet (e.g., https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/{spreadsheet_id}/edit). Must be a non-empty string.

inputs.name: major_dimension
inputs.desc: The major dimension of the values. ROWS (default) means each inner array is a row of values. COLUMNS means each inner array is a column of values. Defaults to ROWS if unspecified.

inputs.name: auto_expand_sheet
inputs.desc: If True (default), automatically expands the sheet's dimensions (adds columns/rows) when the target range exceeds the current grid limits. If False, the operation will fail with an error if the range exceeds grid limits.

inputs.name: value_input_option
inputs.desc: How the input data should be interpreted. RAW: Values are stored exactly as entered, without parsing (dates, formulas, etc. remain as strings). USER_ENTERED: Values are parsed as if typed by a user (numbers stay numbers, strings prefixed with '=' become formulas, etc.).

inputs.name: include_values_in_response
inputs.desc: Determines if the update response should include the values of the cells that were updated. By default, responses do not include the updated values.

inputs.name: response_value_render_option
inputs.desc: Determines how values in the response should be rendered. Only used if includeValuesInResponse is true. FORMATTED_VALUE (default): Values are formatted as displayed in the UI. UNFORMATTED_VALUE: Values are unformatted (numbers, booleans, formulas). FORMULA: Formulas are not evaluated and remain as text.

inputs.name: response_datetime_render_option
inputs.desc: Determines how dates, times, and durations in the response should be rendered. Only used if includeValuesInResponse is true. SERIAL_NUMBER (default): Dates are returned as numbers. FORMATTED_STRING: Dates are returned as strings formatted per the cell's locale.

name: Watch Drive Changes
description: Tool to subscribe to changes for a user or shared drive in Google Drive. Use when you need to monitor a Google Drive for modifications and receive notifications at a specified webhook URL. Notifications may be batched rather than per-change; design handlers to be idempotent and fetch all changes since the last known page_token on each notification.

inputs.name: id
inputs.desc: A unique string that identifies this channel. UUIDs are recommended. Must be unique per active channel; reusing an ID can cause missed, delayed, or duplicate notifications.

inputs.name: type
inputs.desc: The type of delivery mechanism for notifications.

inputs.name: token
inputs.desc: An arbitrary string that will be delivered with each notification. Can be used for verification.

inputs.name: params
inputs.desc:
Optional parameters for the notification channel.
Example: {"ttl": "3600"} for a 1-hour time-to-live (actual support depends on Google API).

inputs.name: spaces
inputs.desc: A comma-separated list of spaces to query within the corpora. Supported values are 'drive' and 'appDataFolder'.

inputs.name: address
inputs.desc: The URL where notifications are to be delivered. Must be a publicly reachable HTTPS URL with a valid SSL certificate; HTTP, localhost, and private network endpoints are rejected by the API.

inputs.name: drive_id
inputs.desc: The shared drive from which changes will be returned. If specified, change IDs will be specific to the shared drive.

inputs.name: page_size
inputs.desc: The maximum number of changes to return per page.

inputs.name: expiration
inputs.desc: Timestamp in milliseconds since the epoch for when the channel should expire. If not set, channel may not expire or have a default expiration. Channels are invalidated after expiry; re-establish the watch with a new channel before or after expiration to avoid missed changes.

inputs.name: page_token
inputs.desc: The token for continuing a previous list request on the next page. This should be set to the value of 'nextPageToken' from the previous response or to the response from the getStartPageToken method. Persist this token per channel so change processing can resume correctly after restarts or interruptions.

inputs.name: include_labels
inputs.desc: A comma-separated list of IDs of labels to include in the labelInfo part of the response.

inputs.name: include_removed
inputs.desc: Whether to include changes indicating that items have been removed from the list of changes (e.g., by deletion or loss of access).

inputs.name: supports_all_drives
inputs.desc: Whether the requesting application supports both My Drives and shared drives. Recommended to set to true if driveId is used or if interactions with shared drives are expected.

inputs.name: restrict_to_my_drive
inputs.desc: Whether to restrict the results to changes inside the My Drive hierarchy. This omits changes to files like those in the Application Data folder or shared files not added to My Drive.

inputs.name: include_corpus_removals
inputs.desc: Whether changes should include the file resource if the file is still accessible by the user at the time of the request, even when a file was removed from the list of changes.

inputs.name: include_permissions_for_view
inputs.desc: Specifies which additional view's permissions to include in the response.

inputs.name: include_items_from_all_drives
inputs.desc: Whether both My Drive and shared drive items should be included in results.

name: Watch File for Changes
description: Tool to subscribe to push notifications for changes to a specific file. Use when you need to monitor a file for modifications and receive real-time notifications at a webhook URL.

inputs.name: id
inputs.desc: A UUID or similar unique string that identifies this notification channel (max 64 characters).

inputs.name: type
inputs.desc: The type of delivery mechanism used for this channel.

inputs.name: token
inputs.desc: An arbitrary string delivered to the target address with each notification for verification (max 256 characters).

inputs.name: fileId
inputs.desc: The ID of the file to watch for changes.

inputs.name: params
inputs.desc: Additional parameters controlling delivery channel behavior.

inputs.name: address
inputs.desc: The HTTPS address where notifications are delivered for this channel. Must have a valid SSL certificate.

inputs.name: payload
inputs.desc: Whether payload data should be included in notifications.

inputs.name: expiration
inputs.desc: Date and time of notification channel expiration as Unix timestamp in milliseconds. Default: 3600 seconds, max: 86400 seconds for files.

inputs.name: includeLabels
inputs.desc: A comma-separated list of IDs of labels to include in the labelInfo part of the response.

inputs.name: acknowledgeAbuse
inputs.desc: Whether the user is acknowledging the risk of downloading known malware or other abusive files. Only applicable to file owner/organizer.

inputs.name: supportsAllDrives
inputs.desc: Whether the requesting application supports both My Drives and shared drives.

inputs.name: supportsTeamDrives
inputs.desc: Deprecated. Use supportsAllDrives instead.

inputs.name: includePermissionsForView
inputs.desc: Specifies which additional view's permissions to include in the response.

name: Abort Repository Migration
description: Tool to abort a repository migration that is queued or in progress. Use when you need to cancel an ongoing migration operation.

inputs.name: migrationId
inputs.desc: The ID of the repository migration to abort. This is the migration ID returned when a migration was queued (e.g., 'RM_kgDOABCDEF').

inputs.name: clientMutationId
inputs.desc: A unique identifier for the client performing the mutation. This can be used to track the mutation in logs or for idempotency purposes.

name: Accept a repository invitation
description: Accepts a PENDING repository invitation that has been issued to the authenticated user.

inputs.name: invitation_id
inputs.desc: Unique identifier of the repository invitation sent TO the authenticated user. Get this ID by listing your pending invitations using the 'List repository invitations for the authenticated user' action.

name: Add app access restrictions
description: Adds GitHub Apps to the list of apps allowed to push to a protected branch. The branch must already have protection rules with restrictions enabled. This endpoint only works for organization repositories, not personal repositories. Apps must be installed on the repository with 'contents' write permissions.

inputs.name: apps
inputs.desc: The list of GitHub App slugs with push access to grant. Apps must be installed on the repository and have 'contents' write permissions. The app slug is the URL-friendly name of the GitHub App (e.g., 'github-actions', 'dependabot').

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The organization name that owns the repository. This field is not case-sensitive. Note: This endpoint only works for organization-owned repositories.

inputs.name: branch
inputs.desc: The name of the branch to which restrictions will be applied. Wildcard characters (e.g., '*') are not allowed in the branch name when using this REST API endpoint. For operations involving wildcard branch names, consult the GitHub GraphQL API.

name: Add a repository collaborator
description: Adds a GitHub user as a repository collaborator, or updates their permission if already a collaborator; `permission` applies to organization-owned repositories (personal ones default to 'push' and ignore this field), and an invitation may be created or permissions updated directly.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account owner of the repository. This name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: username
inputs.desc: The GitHub handle for the user account to add as a collaborator. Cannot be the same as the repository owner, as owners are automatically collaborators.

inputs.name: permission
inputs.desc: Permission level for the collaborator. For organization-owned repositories, valid values are `pull` (read-only), `triage` (manage issues/PRs), `push` (read/write), `maintain` (manage most settings), `admin` (full control), or a custom repository role name if defined by the owning organization. This parameter is ignored for personal repositories, which grant `push` access.

name: Add assignees to an issue
description: Adds assignees to a GitHub issue. This action only adds users - it does not remove existing assignees. Changes are silently ignored if the authenticated user lacks push access to the repository.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The username of the account or the organization name that owns the repository. This field is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: assignees
inputs.desc: A list of GitHub usernames to add as assignees to the issue. Up to 10 assignees total can be on an issue. NOTE: This endpoint ADDS assignees - it does not replace existing ones. Users already assigned are kept. To remove assignees, use the 'remove_assignees_from_an_issue' action instead. The authenticated user must have push access to the repository; otherwise changes are silently ignored.

inputs.name: issue_number
inputs.desc: The unique number identifying the issue within the repository. Must be a positive integer (GitHub issue numbers start at 1).

name: Add email for auth user
description: Adds one or more email addresses (which will be initially unverified) to the authenticated user's GitHub account; use this to associate new emails, noting an email verified for another account will error, while an existing email for the current user is accepted.

inputs.name: emails
inputs.desc: Array of email addresses to add to the authenticated user account. Must contain at least one email address.

name: Add field to user project
description: Tool to add a custom field to a user-owned GitHub Projects V2 project. Use when you need to add fields like status, priority, or custom data to organize project items.

inputs.name: name
inputs.desc: The name of the field to create.

inputs.name: username
inputs.desc: The handle for the GitHub user account.

inputs.name: data_type
inputs.desc: The data type of the field. Use 'single_select' for dropdown fields, 'iteration' for sprint fields, or other types for simple fields.

inputs.name: project_number
inputs.desc: The project's number.

inputs.name: single_select_options
inputs.desc: Options for single select fields. Required when data_type is 'single_select'.

inputs.name: iteration_configuration
inputs.desc: Configuration for iteration fields.

name: Add item to user project
description: Tool to add an issue or pull request to a user-owned GitHub project. Use when you need to add existing repository items to a project board.

inputs.name: id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the issue or pull request to add to the project. Required if owner, repo, and number are not provided.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The repository name. Required with owner and number if id is not provided.

inputs.name: type
inputs.desc: The type of item to add to the project. Must be either Issue or PullRequest.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The repository owner login. Required with repo and number if id is not provided.

inputs.name: number
inputs.desc: The issue or pull request number. Required with owner and repo if id is not provided.

inputs.name: username
inputs.desc: The handle for the GitHub user account.

inputs.name: project_number
inputs.desc: The project's number.

name: Add labels to an issue
description: Adds labels (provided in the request body) to a repository issue; labels that do not already exist are created.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: Repository name, without the `.git` extension (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: Username or organization name owning the repository (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: labels
inputs.desc: Array of label names to add to the issue. Labels that don't exist will be created automatically.

inputs.name: issue_number
inputs.desc: Positive integer that uniquely identifies the issue within the repository. Must be >= 1.

name: Add org runner labels
description: Adds new custom labels to an existing self-hosted runner for an organization; existing labels are not removed, and duplicates are not added.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The organization's name (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: labels
inputs.desc: A list of custom label names to add to the self-hosted runner. Previously applied labels are not removed.

inputs.name: runner_id
inputs.desc: Unique identifier of the self-hosted runner.

name: Add or update team membership for a user
description: Adds a GitHub user to a team or updates their role (member or maintainer), inviting them to the organization if not already a member; idempotent, returning current details if no change is made.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The name of the GitHub organization. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: role
inputs.desc: The role to assign to the user within the team. A 'maintainer' has broader permissions than a 'member', such as managing team members and settings.

inputs.name: username
inputs.desc: The GitHub username of the user to add or whose membership to update.

inputs.name: team_slug
inputs.desc: The slug (URL-friendly version) of the team's name. Must be lowercase with hyphens instead of spaces (e.g., 'my-team' not 'My Team'). Spaces will be automatically converted to hyphens and text will be lowercased.

name: Add or update team project permissions
description: Adds a classic project to a team or updates the team's permission on it. This endpoint grants or updates permissions for a team on a specific classic project (not Projects V2). The authenticated user must have admin permissions for the project. Both the team and project must belong to the same organization. Requirements: - The project must be a classic project (not GitHub Projects V2) - The authenticated user must have admin permissions on the project - The team and project must be in the same organization - Requires 'admin:org' scope for the authentication token Returns HTTP 204 No Content on success, 403 if project is not an org project, or 404 if the organization, team, or project is not found.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The organization name (login) that owns both the team and the classic project. Case-insensitive.

inputs.name: team_slug
inputs.desc: The team's URL-friendly slug identifier within the organization. Found in the team's URL path.

inputs.name: permission
inputs.desc: Permission level to grant the team: 'read' (view only), 'write' (view and edit), or 'admin' (full control including settings). If omitted, uses the team's default permission.

inputs.name: project_id
inputs.desc: The unique numeric ID of the classic project. This is NOT the project number displayed in the UI.

name: Add or update team repository permissions
description: Sets or updates a team's permission level for a repository within an organization; the team must be a member of the organization.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: Organization name where the team exists (case-insensitive). This is the organization that owns the team, not necessarily the repository owner.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: Repository name, without the `.git` extension (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: Account owner of the repository (case-insensitive). This is the user or organization that owns the repository. In most cases, this should match the 'org' parameter value.

inputs.name: team_slug
inputs.desc: The team's slug (URL-friendly name).

inputs.name: permission
inputs.desc: Permission level to grant the team. Valid values: 'pull' (read-only), 'triage' (manage issues/PRs), 'push' (read/write), 'maintain' (manage repo settings plus push), 'admin' (full control). Custom repository role names are also supported if defined by the organization.

name: Add project collaborator
description: Adds a specified GitHub user as a collaborator to an existing organization project with a given permission level. Note: This endpoint is for organization projects (classic). You must be an organization owner or a project admin to add a collaborator. Classic projects are being deprecated in favor of the new Projects experience.

inputs.name: username
inputs.desc: The GitHub username of the user to be added as a collaborator.

inputs.name: permission
inputs.desc: Permission level for the collaborator on the project.

inputs.name: project_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the organization project.

name: Add a repository to an app installation
description: Adds a repository to a GitHub App installation, granting the app access; requires authenticated user to have admin rights for the repository and access to the installation.

inputs.name: repository_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the repository to add to the installation. The authenticated user must have admin permissions for this repository.

inputs.name: installation_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the GitHub App installation to which the repository will be added. This installation must be accessible to the authenticated user.

name: Add repo to org secret with selected access
description: Adds a repository to an existing organization-level GitHub Actions secret that is configured for 'selected' repository access.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The name of the GitHub organization. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: secret_name
inputs.desc: The name of the organization secret to which the repository will be added.

inputs.name: repository_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the repository to be granted access to the organization secret.
