name: Add selected repo to org secret
description: Grants an existing repository access to an existing organization-level Dependabot secret when the secret's visibility is set to 'selected'; the repository must belong to the organization, and the call succeeds without change if access already exists.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The organization's unique handle/name (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: secret_name
inputs.desc: Name of the Dependabot secret; must be alphanumeric or underscore, no spaces (lowercase auto-converts to uppercase).

inputs.name: repository_id
inputs.desc: Unique ID of the repository to be granted access to the secret.

name: Add runner labels
description: Adds and appends custom labels to a self-hosted repository runner, which must be registered and active.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: Name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. Case-insensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: Username or organization name of the repository owner. Case-insensitive.

inputs.name: labels
inputs.desc: Custom label names to add. Appended to existing labels. Names are case-insensitive. Empty list means no changes.

inputs.name: runner_id
inputs.desc: Unique ID of the self-hosted runner.

name: Add selected repository to an organization secret
description: Adds a repository to an organization secret's access list when the secret's visibility is 'selected'; this operation is idempotent.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The name of the organization. This name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: secret_name
inputs.desc: The name of the secret. Secret names can only contain alphanumeric characters ([A-Z], [a-z], [0-9]) or underscores (_). Spaces are not allowed. Secret names cannot start with GITHUB_ and must be less than 255 characters.

inputs.name: repository_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the repository to add to the secret's access list.

name: Add selected repository to an organization variable
description: Grants a repository access to an organization-level GitHub Actions variable, if that variable's visibility is set to 'selected_repositories'.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: Name of the GitHub organization (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: name
inputs.desc: Name of the existing organization-level GitHub Actions variable.

inputs.name: repository_id
inputs.desc: Unique identifier of the repository to grant access.

name: Add a selected repository to a user secret
description: Grants a specified repository access to an authenticated user's existing Codespaces secret, enabling Codespaces created for that repository to use the secret.

inputs.name: secret_name
inputs.desc: Name of the user-level Codespaces secret to which repository access is added; this secret must already exist for the authenticated user.

inputs.name: repository_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the repository to be granted access to the specified user-level Codespaces secret.

name: Add social accounts for the authenticated user
description: Adds one or more social media links (which must be valid, full URLs for platforms supported by GitHub) to the authenticated user's public GitHub profile.

inputs.name: account_urls
inputs.desc: Full URLs for social media profiles to add to the user's GitHub profile; these will be displayed publicly.

name: Add status check contexts
description: Adds status check contexts to a protected branch's required status checks. This action appends new status check contexts to the existing list of required status checks for a protected branch. The branch must already have branch protection enabled with status checks configured. Note: The 'contexts' parameter is deprecated by GitHub in favor of 'checks' array. For new implementations, consider using update_status_check_protection with the 'checks' parameter instead.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account owner of the repository. This name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: branch
inputs.desc: The name of the branch to which status check contexts will be added. Branch names cannot contain wildcard characters. To use wildcard characters in branch names, refer to the GraphQL API.

inputs.name: contexts
inputs.desc: The status check contexts to add to the protected branch. These are the names of CI/CD jobs or other checks that must pass before merging. Can be provided as a single string or a list of strings. Examples: 'ci/test', 'linter', 'continuous-integration/travis-ci'.

name: Add sub-issue to an issue
description: Tool to add a sub-issue to a parent GitHub issue using GraphQL. Use when you need to establish a parent-child relationship between issues. Requires the 'sub_issues' GraphQL feature to be enabled.

inputs.name: issue_id
inputs.desc: The global node ID of the parent issue (e.g., 'I_kwDOABCDEFGH'). This is the GraphQL global identifier returned by GitHub's API.

inputs.name: sub_issue_id
inputs.desc: The global node ID of the sub-issue to link to the parent issue. Either sub_issue_id or sub_issue_url must be provided.

inputs.name: sub_issue_url
inputs.desc: The URL of the sub-issue to link to the parent issue. Either sub_issue_id or sub_issue_url must be provided.

inputs.name: replace_parent
inputs.desc: If true, replaces the existing parent issue if the sub-issue already has one. If false or omitted, the mutation will fail if the sub-issue already has a parent.

inputs.name: client_mutation_id
inputs.desc: A unique identifier for the client performing the mutation. Used to ensure idempotency of mutations.

name: Add team access restrictions
description: Adds teams to the list of teams with push access to a protected branch. This action grants additional teams push access to a protected branch. Unlike 'Set team access restrictions' (PUT), this action adds to the existing list rather than replacing it entirely. Prerequisites: - The repository must be owned by an organization (not a personal account) - The branch must have protection rules enabled - The branch protection must have restrictions configured - The teams must exist in the organization and have repository access

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This value is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The organization name that owns the repository. Team access restrictions only work for organization-owned repositories, not personal repositories. This value is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: teams
inputs.desc: The list of team slugs to add push access to the protected branch. A team slug is the URL-friendly version of the team name (lowercase, hyphens instead of spaces). The teams must already have access to the repository. This adds teams to the existing list (does not replace the entire list).

inputs.name: branch
inputs.desc: The name of the protected branch to add team restrictions to. The branch must already have branch protection enabled with restrictions configured. Wildcard characters are not supported. For wildcard branch protection, use the GitHub GraphQL API.

name: Add user access restrictions
description: Adds users to the list of people allowed to push to a protected branch in an organization repository. Important notes: - This action only works on organization-owned repositories (not personal repos) - The branch must already have protection rules with restrictions enabled - Users must have write access to the repository to be added - The combined total of users, apps, and teams is limited to 100 items

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account owner of the repository (must be an organization for user restrictions). This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: users
inputs.desc: The list of user logins to grant push access to the protected branch. Users must have write access to the repository. An empty list will remove all user restrictions.

inputs.name: branch
inputs.desc: The name of the branch. Cannot contain wildcard characters. To use wildcard characters in branch names, refer to the GitHub GraphQL API documentation.

name: Add users to codespaces access for an organization
description: Adds organization members to the list of users granted Codespaces access billed to the organization. IMPORTANT: This endpoint requires the organization's Codespaces billing access to be set to 'selected_members' mode. If not already configured, use the GITHUB_MANAGE_ACCESS_CONTROL_FOR_ORGANIZATION_CODESPACES action first to set visibility to 'selected_members'.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: Organization's name (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: selected_usernames
inputs.desc: Usernames of organization members to be granted Codespaces access billed to the organization. These users must be members of the specified organization.

name: Get GitHub API root
description: Retrieves a map of all top-level GitHub REST API resource URLs and their templates.

name: Approve a workflow run for a fork pull request
description: Approves a workflow run from a forked repository's pull request; call this when such a run requires manual approval due to workflow configuration.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository without the `.git` extension. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: run_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the workflow run requiring approval.

name: Assign an organization role to a team
description: Assigns an existing organization-level role (identified by `role_id`) to a team (identified by `team_slug`) within a GitHub organization (`org`), provided the organization, team, and role already exist.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The name of the GitHub organization. This field is case-insensitive.

inputs.name: role_id
inputs.desc: Unique identifier of the organization role to assign to the team (obtainable by listing organization roles).

inputs.name: team_slug
inputs.desc: The slug (URL-friendly version) of the team's name.

name: Assign an organization role to a user
description: Assigns a specific organization role to a user who is a member or an outside collaborator in a GitHub organization, using a valid role ID.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The organization's name. This is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: role_id
inputs.desc: The unique integer identifier for the organization role. This ID can be obtained by listing the organization's roles.

inputs.name: username
inputs.desc: The GitHub username of the user to whom the role will be assigned.

name: List Docker Migration Conflicting Packages
description: List Docker packages with migration conflicts for the authenticated user. This endpoint lists all Docker packages owned by the authenticated user that encountered namespace conflicts during the Docker-to-GitHub Container Registry (GHCR) migration. Conflicts occur when a package with the same name exists in both the legacy Docker registry and GHCR. IMPORTANT: The Docker registry for GitHub Packages was deprecated on Feb 24, 2025. This endpoint may return a 400 error with message 'Package migration for docker is no longer supported' as the migration period has ended. In this case, the action returns an informative response instead of failing. Use case: Identifying packages that require manual migration to GHCR. Required scope: read:packages (for OAuth and personal access tokens).

name: Block a user
description: Blocks an existing individual GitHub user (not an organization or your own account), preventing them from interacting with your account and repositories.

inputs.name: username
inputs.desc: The GitHub username (handle) of the user to block. Case-insensitive.

name: Block a user from an organization
description: Blocks a GitHub user from an organization, preventing their contributions, collaboration, and forking of the organization's repositories. Requires admin or 'Blocking users' organization permission (write access).

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The organization name (case-insensitive). You must have admin or 'Blocking users' permission in this organization.

inputs.name: username
inputs.desc: The GitHub username (handle) of the account to block from the organization.

name: Cancel a GitHub Pages deployment
description: Cancels an existing, ongoing or queued GitHub Pages deployment for a repository using its `pages_deployment_id`.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: Repository name, excluding the `.git` extension. Not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: Username or organization that owns the repository. Not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: pages_deployment_id
inputs.desc: Unique numerical ID of the GitHub Pages deployment (not the commit SHA).

name: Cancel sponsorship
description: Tool to cancel an active GitHub sponsorship using GraphQL. Use when you need to terminate a sponsorship relationship between a sponsor and a sponsorable entity. Either sponsor ID/login and sponsorable ID/login must be provided.

inputs.name: sponsor_id
inputs.desc: The global node ID of the user or organization acting as sponsor (e.g., 'U_kgDOABCDEF'). Required if sponsor_login is not provided.

inputs.name: sponsor_login
inputs.desc: The username of the sponsoring user or organization. Required if sponsor_id is not provided.

inputs.name: sponsorable_id
inputs.desc: The global node ID of the user or organization receiving the sponsorship (e.g., 'U_kgDOMNOPQR'). Required if sponsorable_login is not provided.

inputs.name: sponsorable_login
inputs.desc: The username of the sponsored user or organization. Required if sponsorable_id is not provided.

inputs.name: client_mutation_id
inputs.desc: A unique identifier for the client performing the mutation. Used to ensure idempotency of mutations.

name: Cancel a workflow run
description: Cancels a workflow run in a GitHub repository if it is in a cancellable state (e.g., 'in_progress' or 'queued'). Returns 202 Accepted on success.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account owner of the repository (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: run_id
inputs.desc: Unique identifier of the workflow run.

name: Check if a gist is starred
description: Checks if a gist is starred by the authenticated user. Returns `is_starred: true` if the gist is starred (HTTP 204), or `is_starred: false` if not starred (HTTP 404). Use this to verify star status before starring/unstarring a gist.

inputs.name: gist_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier (ID) of the gist to check. Can be found in the URL of the gist (e.g., 'https://gist.github.com/{user}/{gist_id}').

name: Check if pull request merged
description: Checks if a specified GitHub pull request has been merged, indicated by a 204 HTTP status (merged) or 404 (not merged/found).

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension (not case sensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account owner of the repository (not case sensitive).

inputs.name: pull_number
inputs.desc: The number that identifies the pull request.

name: Check if a user can be assigned
description: Verifies if a GitHub user can be assigned to issues in a repository; assignability is confirmed by an HTTP 204 (No Content) response, resulting in an empty 'data' field in the response.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account owner of the repository (e.g., a GitHub username or organization name). This name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: assignee
inputs.desc: The GitHub username of the user to check for assignability. This name is not case sensitive.

name: Check if a user can be assigned to an issue
description: Checks if a specified GitHub user can be assigned to a given issue within a repository.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The username of the account owning the repository. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: assignee
inputs.desc: The GitHub username of the user to check for assignment permissions.

inputs.name: issue_number
inputs.desc: The unique number identifying the issue within the repository.

name: Check if a user follows another user
description: Checks if a GitHub user `username` follows `target_user`; returns a 204 HTTP status if true, 404 if not or if users are invalid.

inputs.name: username
inputs.desc: The GitHub username of the user to check.

inputs.name: target_user
inputs.desc: The GitHub username of the user to verify if they are followed by the `username`.

name: Check if a user is blocked by the authenticated user
description: Checks if the specified GitHub user is blocked by the authenticated user; a 204 No Content response indicates the user is blocked, while a 404 Not Found indicates they are not.

inputs.name: username
inputs.desc: The GitHub username (handle) of the user.

name: Check if a user is blocked by an organization
description: Checks if a GitHub user is blocked by an organization. Returns is_blocked=True if the user is blocked (HTTP 204), or is_blocked=False if not blocked (HTTP 404). Requires 'Blocking users' organization permission (read access).

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The organization name (case-insensitive). Must be an organization where you have 'Blocking users' permission (read access).

inputs.name: username
inputs.desc: The GitHub username (handle) to check if blocked by the organization.

name: Check if a user is a repository collaborator
description: Checks if a user is a collaborator on a specified GitHub repository, returning a 204 status if they are, or a 404 status if they are not or if the repository/user does not exist.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account owner of the repository, typically a GitHub username or an organization name. This name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: username
inputs.desc: The GitHub username of the user whose collaborator status is being checked. This name is not case sensitive.

name: Check if person is followed by authenticated user
description: Checks if the authenticated GitHub user follows a target GitHub user. Returns a structured response with is_following=True when following (HTTP 204), or is_following=False when not following (HTTP 404).

inputs.name: username
inputs.desc: The GitHub username (e.g., 'octocat') of the person to check. This is the user's handle on GitHub.

name: Check private vulnerability reporting status
description: Checks if private vulnerability reporting is enabled for the specified repository.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The username or organization name that owns the repository. This field is not case-sensitive.

name: Check public organization membership
description: Tool to check if a user is a public member of an organization. Use when you need to verify public organization membership status.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: username
inputs.desc: The handle for the GitHub user account.

name: Check team permissions for a project
description: Checks if a team has 'read', 'write', or 'admin' permissions for an organization's specific classic project, returning the project's details if access is confirmed.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The name of the organization. This is case-insensitive.

inputs.name: team_slug
inputs.desc: The slug (short name) of the team.

inputs.name: project_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the project (classic).

name: Check team permissions for a repository
description: Checks a team's permissions for a specific repository within an organization, including permissions inherited from parent teams.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The name of the organization. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The organization that owns the repository. For this GitHub API endpoint, this value must be the same as the `org` parameter. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: team_slug
inputs.desc: The URL-friendly slug for the team name. It is unique within the organization and typically all lowercase with hyphens instead of spaces.

name: Check a token
description: Checks if a GitHub App or OAuth access_token is valid for the specified client_id and retrieves its details, typically to verify its active status and grants. NOTE: This endpoint requires Basic Authentication using the OAuth App's client_id as username and client_secret as password. The access_token parameter must be an OAuth token issued by the specified OAuth App.

inputs.name: client_id
inputs.desc: The unique client ID of your GitHub OAuth App (must match the app that issued the token being checked).

inputs.name: access_token
inputs.desc: The OAuth access token issued by your OAuth App that you want to validate. Returns 404 if the token is invalid or not issued by this client_id.

name: Clear Project V2 Item Field Value
description: Tool to clear the value of a field for an item in a GitHub Project V2. Use when you need to remove or reset a field value (text, number, date, assignees, labels, single-select, iteration, or milestone fields).

inputs.name: itemId
inputs.desc: The global node ID of the project item to update (e.g., 'PVTI_lAHOCNqc1s4BN3oqzgldG_w'). This identifies the specific item within the project whose field value will be cleared.

inputs.name: fieldId
inputs.desc: The global node ID of the field to clear (e.g., 'PVTSSF_lAHOCNqc1s4BN3oqzg8vTnA'). Currently supports text, number, date, assignees, labels, single-select, iteration and milestone fields.

inputs.name: projectId
inputs.desc: The global node ID of the Project (e.g., 'PVT_kwHOCNqc1s4BN3oq'). This identifies the project containing the item.

name: Clear repository cache by key
description: Deletes GitHub Actions caches from a repository matching a specific `key` and an optional Git `ref`, used to manage storage or clear outdated/corrupted caches; the action succeeds even if no matching caches are found to delete.

inputs.name: key
inputs.desc: The exact cache key used to identify specific caches for deletion. This key is often dynamically generated in workflows using expressions (e.g., `${{ runner.os }}-npm-${{ hashFiles('**/package-lock.json') }}`).

inputs.name: ref
inputs.desc: The full Git reference for narrowing down the cache deletion. For example, to target caches for a specific branch, use `refs/heads/<branch_name>`. To target caches for a pull request, use `refs/pull/<pull_number>/merge`. If omitted, caches matching the key across all refs are considered.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository without the `.git` extension. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

name: Clear self-hosted runner org labels
description: Removes all custom labels from a self-hosted runner for an organization; default labels (e.g., 'self-hosted', 'linux', 'x64') will remain.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The name of the GitHub organization that owns the self-hosted runner (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: runner_id
inputs.desc: Unique numeric identifier of the self-hosted runner. Can be obtained from listing runners for the organization.

name: Commit Multiple Files
description: Tool to atomically create, update, or delete multiple files in a GitHub repository as a single commit. Uses Git Data APIs to avoid SHA mismatch conflicts that occur with the Contents API when multiple files are modified in parallel. Use when you need to make multi-file changes reliably. BRANCH CREATION: When committing to a new branch (e.g., 'fix/my-fix' or 'feature/new-feature'), you MUST provide 'base_branch' (typically 'main' or 'master') to create the branch from. If the branch already exists, base_branch is not needed. This action handles race conditions automatically: if the branch is updated by another commit between fetching the HEAD and updating the reference (resulting in a 422 'not a fast forward' error), the action will retry by refetching the HEAD and rebasing changes. Use max_retries to control this behavior.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: Repository name (without .git extension)

inputs.name: force
inputs.desc: Force update the branch reference. WARNING: Use with caution as this can overwrite commits. Only use this when you intentionally want to overwrite the branch history.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: Repository owner (username or organization name)

inputs.name: author
inputs.desc: Git author or committer information.

inputs.name: branch
inputs.desc: Target branch name to commit to. If this branch doesn't exist, you MUST provide 'base_branch' to create it. For new feature branches (e.g., 'fix/my-fix', 'feature/new-feature'), always set base_branch='main' (or your default branch).

inputs.name: deletes
inputs.desc: List of file paths to delete from the repository. Files must exist in the repository; attempting to delete non-existent files will result in an error. Ensure paths are exact with no leading/trailing whitespace.

inputs.name: message
inputs.desc: Commit message describing the changes

inputs.name: upserts
inputs.desc: List of files to create or update. Each entry requires path, content, and optional encoding (utf-8 or base64)

inputs.name: committer
inputs.desc: Git author or committer information.

inputs.name: base_branch
inputs.desc: REQUIRED when committing to a new branch that doesn't exist yet. Specifies the existing branch to create the new branch from (e.g., 'main' or 'master'). Not needed if 'branch' already exists in the repository.

inputs.name: max_retries
inputs.desc: Maximum number of retries when encountering race conditions (422 'not a fast forward' errors). The action will refetch the HEAD and rebase changes on each retry. Set to 0 to disable retries.

name: Compare two commits
description: Compares two commit points (commits, branches, tags, or SHAs) within a repository or across forks, using `BASE...HEAD` or `OWNER:REF...OWNER:REF` format for the `basehead` parameter.

inputs.name: page
inputs.desc: Page number of the results to fetch, for pagination when the number of differences (commits or files) is large.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The username or organization name that owns the repository. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: basehead
inputs.desc: A string specifying the base and head references to compare, in the format `BASE...HEAD`. `BASE` and `HEAD` can be branch names (e.g., `main...develop`), tag names (e.g., `v1.0.0...v1.1.0`), or commit SHAs (abbreviated or full 40-character format). Both abbreviated and full SHAs are accepted; full 40-character SHAs are recommended for reliability in large repositories. Both references must exist in the specified repository. To compare branches across forks in the same network, use the format `OWNER:REF...OWNER:REF` (e.g., `octocat:main...another-user:main`). Do NOT pass GitHub URLs - only plain reference strings like 'main...develop' are accepted.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.desc: Number of results (e.g., files or commits) to return per page, with a maximum of 100, for pagination.

name: Configure JIT runner for an org
description: Generates a JIT configuration for a GitHub organization's new self-hosted runner to run a single job then unregister; requires admin:org scope and the runner_group_id must exist in the organization.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: Name of the GitHub organization (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: name
inputs.desc: Desired name for the new self-hosted runner, visible in the GitHub UI.

inputs.name: labels
inputs.desc: Custom labels to assign to the new self-hosted runner, used by GitHub Actions to route jobs. **Minimum items**: 1. **Maximum items**: 100.

inputs.name: work_folder
inputs.desc: Path to the working directory on the runner machine where jobs will execute, relative to the runner's installation directory.

inputs.name: runner_group_id
inputs.desc: ID of the runner group to assign the new self-hosted runner. Must exist in the organization. You can get runner group IDs by calling the 'List self-hosted runner groups for an organization' endpoint (GET /orgs/{org}/actions/runner-groups).

name: Configure OIDC subject claim template
description: Sets or updates the OIDC subject claim customization template for an existing GitHub organization by specifying which claims (e.g., 'repo', 'actor') form the OIDC token's subject (`sub`). This action customizes which claim keys are included in the OIDC subject claim for the specified organization. It allows for fine-tuning the content of the subject claim based on organizational needs.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The GitHub organization name (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: include_claim_keys
inputs.desc: Array of unique strings, each a valid claim key (e.g., 'repo', 'actor'; alphanumeric/underscores only), to include in the OIDC subject claim (`sub`).

name: Convert an organization member to outside collaborator
description: Converts an existing organization member, who is not an owner, to an outside collaborator, restricting their access to explicitly granted repositories.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The organization name (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: async
inputs.desc: If true, performs the conversion asynchronously (API returns a 202 status when the job is successfully queued).

inputs.name: username
inputs.desc: The GitHub username of the member to convert.

name: Create a blob
description: Creates a Git blob in a repository, requiring content and encoding ('utf-8' or 'base64'). Requires write access to the repository.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the '.git' extension. This field is not case-sensitive. The repository must exist and you must have write access to it.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The username or organization name that owns the repository. This field is not case-sensitive. You must have write access to the repository.

inputs.name: content
inputs.desc: The content to be stored in the new blob.

inputs.name: encoding
inputs.desc: The encoding for the 'content'; "utf-8" or "base64".

name: Create a check run
description: Creates a new check run for a specific commit in a repository, used by external services to report status, detailed feedback, annotations, and images directly within the GitHub UI. NOTE: This endpoint requires GitHub App authentication - OAuth tokens and personal access tokens cannot create check runs.

inputs.name: name
inputs.desc: The name of the check to be displayed in the GitHub UI. For example, "Code Coverage" or "Linter".

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account owner of the repository. This name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: status
inputs.desc: The current status of the check run. Only GitHub Actions can set `status` to `waiting`, `pending`, or `requested`.

inputs.name: actions
inputs.desc: A list of up to three action objects defining interactive buttons displayed on GitHub after the check run completes. Each object needs a `label` (button text), `identifier` (unique ID for the action), and `description` (tooltip). Clicking a button sends a `check_run.requested_action` webhook to your app.

inputs.name: head_sha
inputs.desc: The SHA of the commit for which the check run is created.

inputs.name: conclusion
inputs.desc: **Required if `completed_at` is provided or if `status` is `completed`**. Specifies the final outcome of the check (e.g., 'success', 'failure'). Note: Setting `conclusion` automatically sets `status` to `completed`. GitHub alone can set a conclusion to `stale`.

inputs.name: started_at
inputs.desc: The ISO 8601 timestamp (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ) indicating when the check run began.

inputs.name: details_url
inputs.desc: The URL of the integrator's site providing full details of the check. If omitted, the GitHub App's homepage is used. This URL is available from the check's UI.

inputs.name: external_id
inputs.desc: A unique identifier for the check run on the integrator's system. This helps in correlating check runs between GitHub and the external system.

inputs.name: completed_at
inputs.desc: The ISO 8601 timestamp (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ) indicating when the check run finished. **Required if `status` is `completed`**.

inputs.name: output__text
inputs.desc: Detailed textual information about the check run's output. Supports Markdown. **Maximum length**: 65,535 characters.

inputs.name: output__title
inputs.desc: A descriptive title for the output of the check run, displayed in the GitHub UI. For example, 'Linter Scan Summary'.

inputs.name: output__images
inputs.desc: A list of image objects to display in the check run's output in the GitHub UI. Each object requires `alt` (alternative text), `image_url` (URL of the image), and an optional `caption`.

inputs.name: output__summary
inputs.desc: A summary of the check run's findings. Supports Markdown. **Maximum length**: 65,535 characters.

inputs.name: output__annotations
inputs.desc: A list of annotation objects detailing issues at specific code locations. Annotations are visible in the 'Checks' and 'Files changed' tabs of a pull request. Each annotation object should specify `path`, `start_line`, `end_line`, `annotation_level` ('notice', 'warning', or 'failure'), and `message`. Optional fields include `start_column`, `end_column`, `title`, and `raw_details`. Up to 50 annotations per API request; for more, use subsequent 'Update a check run' calls. GitHub Actions limits are 10 warnings and 10 errors per step.

name: Create a check suite
description: Creates a new check suite for a specific commit (`head_sha`) in an original repository (not a fork). IMPORTANT: This endpoint requires a GitHub App installation access token - OAuth tokens and classic personal access tokens cannot use this endpoint. GitHub dispatches a `check_suite` webhook event with the `requested` action upon success.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: Repository name, without the `.git` extension (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: Account owner of the repository (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: head_sha
inputs.desc: SHA of the commit for which to create the check suite, typically the head commit of a branch.

name: Create a codespace for the authenticated user
description: Creates a GitHub Codespace for the authenticated user, requiring a JSON request body with either `repository_id` (integer) or a `pull_request` object (containing `pull_request_number` (integer) and `repository_id` (integer)). This action allows users to set up a development environment quickly by creating a codespace from a specified repository or pull request.

inputs.name: ref
inputs.desc: Git ref (branch, tag, or commit SHA) to use for the codespace.

inputs.name: pull_request
inputs.desc: PullRequestPayload

inputs.name: repository_id
inputs.desc: ID of the repository to create the codespace from.

name: Create a codespace from a pull request
description: Creates a GitHub Codespace for an open pull request in a Codespaces-enabled repository, with options to customize its configuration.

inputs.name: geo
inputs.desc: Geographic area for this codespace (e.g., `UsEast`, `EuropeWest`); supersedes `location`. If unspecified, typically IP-derived.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This is case-insensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The username of the account that owns the repository. This is case-insensitive.

inputs.name: machine
inputs.desc: Machine type for this codespace (e.g., `standardLinux`). Varies by CPU, RAM, storage. Uses default if unspecified.

inputs.name: location
inputs.desc: Requested geographic location (e.g., `EastUs`, `WestEurope`). This parameter is deprecated; use `geo`. If unspecified, location may be IP-derived.

inputs.name: client_ip
inputs.desc: Client's public IP address for location auto-detection if proxied and `geo` or `location` are not specified.

inputs.name: pull_number
inputs.desc: The unique number identifying the pull request.

inputs.name: display_name
inputs.desc: Custom name for the codespace.

inputs.name: devcontainer_path
inputs.desc: Path to a `devcontainer.json` file in the repository that defines the development environment.

inputs.name: working_directory
inputs.desc: Working directory for the codespace upon opening. Defaults to the repository root if unspecified.

inputs.name: idle_timeout_minutes
inputs.desc: Minutes of inactivity after which the codespace is automatically stopped. A default timeout applies if unspecified.

inputs.name: retention_period_minutes
inputs.desc: Retention period in minutes (0-43200, i.e., up to 30 days) for a stopped codespace. 0 means immediate deletion.

inputs.name: multi_repo_permissions_opt_out
inputs.desc: Set to `true` to prevent GitHub from requesting additional repository permissions defined in `devcontainer.json`. Defaults to `false` (permissions will be requested).

name: Create a codespace in a repository
description: Creates a GitHub Codespace for the authenticated user in a specified repository, which must be accessible and use a valid `devcontainer.json` if `devcontainer_path` is specified.

inputs.name: geo
inputs.desc: Geographic area for the codespace, replacing `location`; determined by user IP if not specified.

inputs.name: ref
inputs.desc: Git reference (e.g., branch name) for the codespace; uses repository's default branch if not provided.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: Name of the repository, without the `.git` extension; not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: Username or organization name owning the repository; not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: machine
inputs.desc: Machine type (CPU, RAM, storage). See 'List available machine types for a repository' action for options; uses a default type if not specified.

inputs.name: location
inputs.desc: Requested AWS region (best-effort, may differ); determined by user IP if not provided. Deprecated: use `geo`.

inputs.name: client_ip
inputs.desc: IP address to infer preferred region if `location` and `geo` are unspecified; useful for proxied requests.

inputs.name: display_name
inputs.desc: Custom display name for the codespace; a default name is generated if not provided.

inputs.name: devcontainer_path
inputs.desc: Path to a `devcontainer.json` in the repository; uses `.devcontainer/devcontainer.json` or `devcontainer.json` at root if not specified.

inputs.name: working_directory
inputs.desc: Directory to use as default when codespace opens; uses repository root if not specified.

inputs.name: idle_timeout_minutes
inputs.desc: Minutes of inactivity before codespace auto-stops; uses a default timeout if not specified.

inputs.name: retention_period_minutes
inputs.desc: Minutes an idle (stopped) codespace is retained before auto-deletion (0-43200, i.e., up to 30 days); uses a default period if not specified.

inputs.name: multi_repo_permissions_opt_out
inputs.desc: If `true`, opts out of granting multi-repository permissions from `devcontainer.json`. If unspecified or `false`, permissions are granted if requested.

name: Create a commit
description: Creates a new commit in a GitHub repository; the `tree` SHA and any `parents` SHAs must already exist in the repository.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: Name of the repository, without the `.git` extension (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: tree
inputs.desc: SHA of the tree object for this commit; must exist in the repository.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: Account owner of the repository (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: message
inputs.desc: The commit message.

inputs.name: parents
inputs.desc: SHAs of parent commits; all must exist in the repository. Omit or provide an empty list for a root commit.

inputs.name: signature
inputs.desc: ASCII-armored detached PGP signature over the commit data. GitHub adds this to the `gpgsig` header if provided.

inputs.name: author__date
inputs.desc: Timestamp (ISO 8601: `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`) when the commit was authored.

inputs.name: author__name
inputs.desc: Name of the commit's author. Defaults to authenticated user if not provided.

inputs.name: author__email
inputs.desc: Email of the commit's author. Defaults to authenticated user if not provided.

inputs.name: committer__date
inputs.desc: Timestamp (ISO 8601: `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`) when the commit was committed. Defaults to `author_date` or current time.

inputs.name: committer__name
inputs.desc: Name of the committer. Defaults to author's name or authenticated user if not provided.

inputs.name: committer__email
inputs.desc: Email of the committer. Defaults to author's email or authenticated user if not provided.

name: Create a commit comment
description: Creates a comment on a specific commit, or on a specific line if `path` and `position` are provided.

inputs.name: body
inputs.desc: The text content of the comment.

inputs.name: line
inputs.desc: **Deprecated:** Use `position` instead. Line number in the file to comment on. `position` takes precedence if both are provided.

inputs.name: path
inputs.desc: Relative path of the file to comment on. If specified, `position` is also required.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The username or organization name that owns the repository.

inputs.name: position
inputs.desc: Line number in the diff's patch to comment on, relative to the commit's changes. Required if `path` is specified.

inputs.name: commit_sha
inputs.desc: The SHA identifier of the commit to comment on.

name: Create a commit status
description: Sets a commit's status (e.g., error, failure, pending, success from CI/CD) for a given SHA; max 1000 statuses per SHA/context.

inputs.name: sha
inputs.desc: SHA hash of the commit.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: Repository name, without the `.git` extension (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: Username or organization name of the repository owner (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: state
inputs.desc: State of the commit status, indicating check outcome or phase.

inputs.name: context
inputs.desc: Label to differentiate this status from other systems (e.g., 'ci/jenkins'); case-insensitive.

inputs.name: target_url
inputs.desc: URL linking to the source of the status (e.g., CI build log), displayed in GitHub UI.

inputs.name: description
inputs.desc: Human-readable description of the status, displayed in GitHub UI.

name: Create a custom organization role
description: Creates a custom role with defined permissions within a GitHub organization.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: Name of the GitHub organization (case-insensitive) where the custom role will be created.

inputs.name: name
inputs.desc: Unique name for the custom role.

inputs.name: description
inputs.desc: Optional short description of the custom role's purpose or permissions.

inputs.name: permissions
inputs.desc: List of fine-grained permission strings defining actions granted by this role. Use the List Organization Fine-Grained Permissions action to get available permissions for your organization.

name: Create a deploy key
description: Creates a deploy key for a repository; the repository must exist and be accessible, and the provided key must be a valid public SSH key.

inputs.name: key
inputs.desc: The full public SSH key. Ensure this is the public key, not the private key.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The username of the account that owns the repository. This is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: title
inputs.desc: A descriptive name for the new deploy key. If not provided, a default name will be generated.

inputs.name: read_only
inputs.desc: If `true`, the deploy key will only have read access. If `false` (default), the key will have read and write access. Write access grants significant privileges.

name: Create a deployment
description: Creates a GitHub deployment for an existing repository, targeting a specific ref (branch, tag, or SHA) that must also exist within the repository.

inputs.name: ref
inputs.desc: The ref to deploy. This can be a branch name, a tag name, or a commit SHA.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case-sensitive. The repository must exist and the authenticated user must have write access to it.

inputs.name: task
inputs.desc: Specifies a task to execute, e.g., `deploy` or `deploy:migrations`.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account owner of the repository. This name is not case-sensitive. The repository must exist and the authenticated user must have access to it.

inputs.name: payload
inputs.desc: A JSON string payload providing additional information that a deployment system might need, customizable based on the deployment process.

inputs.name: auto_merge
inputs.desc: Attempts to automatically merge the default branch into the requested ref if the ref is behind the default branch. Set to `false` to disable.

inputs.name: description
inputs.desc: A short description of the deployment. Maximum length of 1000 characters.

inputs.name: environment
inputs.desc: Name for the target deployment environment (e.g., `production`, `staging`, `qa`).

inputs.name: required_contexts
inputs.desc: A list of status check contexts to verify against commit status checks before creating the deployment. If omitted or `null`, GitHub verifies all unique contexts. Pass an empty list `[]` to bypass all checks.

inputs.name: transient_environment
inputs.desc: Specifies if the given environment is specific to this deployment and will no longer exist at some point in the future (e.g., a staging environment for a pull request). Set to `true` if the environment is temporary.

inputs.name: production_environment
inputs.desc: Specifies if the given environment is one that end-users directly interact with.

name: Create a deployment branch policy
description: Creates a deployment branch or tag policy for an existing environment in a GitHub repository, using a Ruby File.fnmatch pattern (where `*` doesn't match `/`) to specify which branches or tags are deployable.

inputs.name: name
inputs.desc: Name pattern for deployable branches/tags (Ruby File.fnmatch syntax; `*` won't match `/`, e.g., `release/*/*` for `release/feature/login`). Refer to [Ruby File.fnmatch documentation](https://ruby-doc.org/core-2.5.1/File.html#method-c-fnmatch).

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: Name of the repository, without the `.git` extension (not case sensitive).

inputs.name: type
inputs.desc: Whether the policy targets a Git branch or a tag.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: Account owner of the repository (not case sensitive).

inputs.name: environment_name
inputs.desc: Name of the target environment; URL-encode special characters (e.g., `/` as `%2F`).

name: Create a deployment status
description: Creates a status for an existing deployment, updating its operational state, associated URLs, and description.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository without the `.git` extension. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: state
inputs.desc: State of the status. Setting a transient deployment to `inactive` shows it as `destroyed` in GitHub.

inputs.name: log_url
inputs.desc: URL for deployment output. Preferred over `target_url`; setting this also sets `target_url` to the same value.

inputs.name: target_url
inputs.desc: URL for deployment status updates or output. `log_url` is generally preferred.

inputs.name: description
inputs.desc: Short description of the status (max 140 characters).

inputs.name: environment
inputs.desc: Name for the target deployment environment (e.g., `production`, `staging`). If unset, uses environment from previous status or deployment.

inputs.name: auto_inactive
inputs.desc: If true, sets prior successful, non-transient, non-production deployments in the same repository and environment to `inactive`.

inputs.name: deployment_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the deployment for which to create a status.

inputs.name: environment_url
inputs.desc: URL for accessing the target deployment environment.

name: Create a discussion
description: Creates a new discussion post on a specific team's page within an organization.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: Name of the organization (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: body
inputs.desc: Main content of the discussion post, typically in markdown.

inputs.name: title
inputs.desc: Title of the discussion post.

inputs.name: private
inputs.desc: If `true`, post is private (visible only to team members, org owners, and team maintainers); `false` makes it public to all organization members.

inputs.name: team_slug
inputs.desc: URL-friendly version of the team name (slug).

name: Create a discussion comment
description: Creates a new comment on an existing team discussion within a GitHub organization.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The name of the organization. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: body
inputs.desc: The content of the discussion comment. Supports GitHub Flavored Markdown.

inputs.name: team_slug
inputs.desc: The slug (URL-friendly version) of the team name.

inputs.name: discussion_number
inputs.desc: The unique number identifying the discussion within the team.

name: Create a fork
description: Creates a fork of an accessible repository, optionally into a specific organization, with a new name, or copying only the default branch.

inputs.name: name
inputs.desc: The desired name for the newly created fork. If not provided, the new fork will have the same name as the original repository.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The username or organization name that owns the repository. This field is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: organization
inputs.desc: The GitHub organization name to fork the repository into. If not specified, the fork will be created in the authenticated user's account.

inputs.name: default_branch_only
inputs.desc: Specifies whether to fork only the default branch of the repository. If `True`, only the default branch is copied. If `False` or not specified, all branches are copied.

name: Create a gist
description: Creates a new gist on GitHub with provided files, an optional description, and public/secret visibility.

inputs.name: files
inputs.desc: JSON string representing content for the gist's files. Dictionary where keys are filenames and values are objects, each having a `content` key with the file's raw string data.

inputs.name: public
inputs.desc: Indicates if the gist is public (`true`) or secret (`false`).

inputs.name: description
inputs.desc: Optional description for the gist.

name: Create a gist comment
description: Creates a new comment on a specified GitHub gist.

inputs.name: body
inputs.desc: The text of the comment to be posted on the gist.

inputs.name: gist_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the gist.

name: Create a GitHub App from a manifest
description: Use this action to complete the GitHub App Manifest flow (step 3 of 3) by exchanging the temporary authorization `code` for the app's full configuration, including credentials and private key. The code is provided via redirect URL after creating the app through GitHub's web interface and must be used within 1 hour.

inputs.name: code
inputs.desc: The temporary authorization code provided by GitHub during the app manifest flow (received as a query parameter in the redirect URL). This single-use code must be exchanged within 1 hour of generation to retrieve the app's configuration including credentials and private key.

name: Create a github pages deployment
description: Creates a GitHub Pages deployment for a repository using a specified artifact and OIDC token, provided GitHub Pages is enabled and the artifact (containing static assets) is accessible.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account owner of the repository (e.g., a GitHub username or organization name). This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: oidc_token
inputs.desc: OIDC token from GitHub Actions for deployment authorization, typically from `secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN` with `id-token: write` permission.

inputs.name: artifact_id
inputs.desc: Identifier of a GitHub Actions artifact (e.g., .zip or .tar) with static assets for deployment. Artifact must belong to the repository. Either this or `artifact_url` is required.

inputs.name: environment
inputs.desc: Target environment for this GitHub Pages deployment. Defaults to `github-pages`.

inputs.name: artifact_url
inputs.desc: URL of a GitHub Actions artifact (e.g., .zip or .tar) with static assets for deployment. Artifact must belong to the repository. Either this or `artifact_id` is required.

inputs.name: pages_build_version
inputs.desc: Unique string identifying the build version for this deployment, typically the Git commit SHA. Defaults to `GITHUB_SHA`.

name: Create a github pages site
description: Configures or updates GitHub Pages for a repository, setting build type and source; ensure a Pages workflow exists for 'workflow' `build_type`, or `source_branch` exists for 'legacy' or unspecified `build_type`. This action allows users to effectively manage their GitHub Pages site settings, specifying how the site is built and where the source files are located.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the '.git' extension. This field is case-insensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The username or organization name that owns the repository. This field is case-insensitive.

inputs.name: build_type
inputs.desc: Build process for the Pages site: 'legacy' (traditional GitHub Pages build) or 'workflow' (GitHub Actions). If omitted and `source_branch` is provided, defaults to 'legacy'. When 'workflow', `source_branch` and `source_path` are ignored.

inputs.name: source__path
inputs.desc: Directory within `source_branch` for site source files (e.g., '/' for root, '/docs'). Ignored if `build_type` is 'workflow'.

inputs.name: source__branch
inputs.desc: The branch for GitHub Pages source (e.g., 'main', 'gh-pages'). Required if `build_type` is 'legacy' or not specified. It is ignored if `build_type` is 'workflow'.

name: Create a label
description: Creates a new label in a specified GitHub repository, provided the repository exists and the user has write permissions.

inputs.name: name
inputs.desc: The name of the label. Emoji can be added to label names, using either native emoji or colon-style markup. For example, typing `:strawberry:` will render the emoji ![:strawberry:](https://github.githubassets.com/images/icons/emoji/unicode/1f353.png ":strawberry:"). For a full list of available emoji and codes, see the "[Emoji cheat sheet](https://github.com/ikatyang/emoji-cheat-sheet)."

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: color
inputs.desc: The hexadecimal color code for the label (e.g., `f29513`), without the leading `#`. Required parameter.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The username of the account that owns the repository. This is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: description
inputs.desc: A short description of the label. Maximum 100 characters.

name: Create a milestone
description: Creates a milestone in a GitHub repository for tracking progress on issues or pull requests; requires repository existence and user write permissions.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: Repository name, without the `.git` extension (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: Username of the repository owner (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: state
inputs.desc: State of the milestone.

inputs.name: title
inputs.desc: Title for the new milestone.

inputs.name: due_on
inputs.desc: Due date for the milestone (ISO 8601 format: `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`).

inputs.name: description
inputs.desc: Detailed description of the milestone.

name: Create an autolink reference for a repository
description: Creates a repository autolink to automatically convert text references (e.g., 'TICKET-123') into hyperlinks, using a unique `key_prefix` and a `url_template` that includes `<num>`.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. The name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account owner of the repository. This can be a username or an organization name. The name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: key_prefix
inputs.desc: Prefix that triggers the autolink (e.g., 'JIRA-'). When followed by an identifier, it's converted into a link. Must be non-empty and not contain spaces.

inputs.name: url_template
inputs.desc: URL template for the autolink, using `<num>` as a placeholder for the reference identifier (e.g., 'https://example.atlassian.net/browse/<num>'). Behavior of `<num>` is determined by `is_alphanumeric`.

inputs.name: is_alphanumeric
inputs.desc: Specifies if the `<num>` placeholder in `url_template` matches alphanumeric characters (`A-Z` (case-insensitive), `0-9`, and `-`) or only numeric characters (`0-9`).

name: Create an environment variable
description: Creates an encrypted environment variable for a pre-existing environment within a GitHub repository; will fail if the variable name already exists.

inputs.name: name
inputs.desc: The name of the environment variable to create (e.g., `API_KEY`).

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The username or organization name that owns the repository. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: value
inputs.desc: The value of the environment variable (e.g., `your_secret_token`). This value will be encrypted.

inputs.name: environment_name
inputs.desc: The name of the environment for which to create the variable. The name must be URL encoded if it contains special characters (e.g., slashes `/` must be replaced with `%2F`).

name: Create an issue
description: Creates a new issue in a GitHub repository, requiring the repository to exist and have issues enabled; specific fields like assignees, milestone, or labels may require push access.

inputs.name: body
inputs.desc: The detailed textual contents of the new issue.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension (case-insensitive). The repository must exist, be accessible, and have issues enabled.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The GitHub account owner of the repository (case-insensitive). The repository must exist and be accessible to the authenticated user.

inputs.name: title
inputs.desc: The title for the new issue.

inputs.name: labels
inputs.desc: Array of label names to associate with this issue (generally case-insensitive). NOTE: Only users with push access can set labels; they are silently dropped otherwise. Pass an empty list to clear all labels.

inputs.name: assignee
inputs.desc: Login for the user to whom this issue should be assigned. NOTE: Only users with push access can set the assignee; it is silently dropped otherwise. **This field is deprecated in favor of `assignees`.**

inputs.name: assignees
inputs.desc: GitHub login names for users to assign to this issue. NOTE: Only users with push access can set assignees; they are silently dropped otherwise.

inputs.name: milestone
inputs.desc: The ID of the milestone to associate this issue with (e.g., "5"). NOTE: Only users with push access can set the milestone; it is silently dropped otherwise.

name: Create an issue comment
description: Creates a new comment on an existing GitHub issue or pull request within the specified repository.

inputs.name: body
inputs.desc: Comment content in GitHub Flavored Markdown.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: Name of the repository (without `.git` extension); not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: Account owner of the repository (username or organization); not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: issue_number
inputs.desc: Number identifying the issue or pull request for the comment.

name: Create an organization project
description: Creates a new classic project board within a specified GitHub organization. Note: This action uses GitHub's Projects (classic) REST API. The classic projects feature may be disabled in some organizations, and GitHub recommends migrating to Projects V2 (accessible via GraphQL API) for new projects. Requirements: - The authenticated user must be an organization member with project creation permissions - Classic projects must be enabled for the organization - Requires 'repo' and 'admin:org' or 'write:org' scopes

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: Name of the organization (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: body
inputs.desc: Body or description for the new project.

inputs.name: name
inputs.desc: Name for the new project.

name: Create an organization repository
description: Creates a new repository within a specified GitHub organization, with options for detailed configuration including visibility, features, merge strategies, initial commit, and templates.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: Name of the organization where the repository will be created (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: name
inputs.desc: Name of the new repository.

inputs.name: private
inputs.desc: Whether the repository is private. `visibility` takes precedence if both are set.

inputs.name: team_id
inputs.desc: ID of the team to grant access to this repository within the organization.

inputs.name: has_wiki
inputs.desc: Enable wiki for this repository.

inputs.name: homepage
inputs.desc: URL for the repository's homepage.

inputs.name: auto_init
inputs.desc: Create an initial commit with an empty README.

inputs.name: has_issues
inputs.desc: Enable issues for this repository.

inputs.name: visibility
inputs.desc: Repository visibility: 'public' (visible to everyone) or 'private' (visible to collaborators).

inputs.name: description
inputs.desc: Short description of the repository.

inputs.name: is_template
inputs.desc: Make this repository a template repository.

inputs.name: has_projects
inputs.desc: Enable projects for this repository. Fails if organization has disabled repository projects and this is true.

inputs.name: has_downloads
inputs.desc: Enable downloads for this repository (deprecated).

inputs.name: allow_auto_merge
inputs.desc: Allow auto-merge on pull requests.

inputs.name: license_template
inputs.desc: License template keyword (e.g., "mit", "apache-2.0"). See [GitHub license documentation](https://docs.github.com/articles/licensing-a-repository/#searching-github-by-license-type) for options.

inputs.name: custom_properties
inputs.desc: JSON string containing custom properties for the repository as a key-value object. Example: '{"project_lead": "octocat", "status": "alpha"}'

inputs.name: allow_merge_commit
inputs.desc: Allow merging pull requests with a merge commit.

inputs.name: allow_rebase_merge
inputs.desc: Allow rebase-merging pull requests.

inputs.name: allow_squash_merge
inputs.desc: Allow squash-merging pull requests.

inputs.name: gitignore_template
inputs.desc: Name of the .gitignore template to apply (e.g., "Python", "Node"). Refer to the [GitHub gitignore template list](https://github.com/github/gitignore).

inputs.name: merge_commit_title
inputs.desc: Default title for merge commits: 'PR_TITLE' or 'MERGE_MESSAGE'.

inputs.name: merge_commit_message
inputs.desc: Default message for merge commits: 'PR_TITLE', 'PR_BODY', or 'BLANK'.

inputs.name: delete_branch_on_merge
inputs.desc: Automatically delete head branches when pull requests are merged. Requires organization owner privileges if true.

inputs.name: squash_merge_commit_title
inputs.desc: Default title for squash merge commits: 'PR_TITLE' or 'COMMIT_OR_PR_TITLE'.

inputs.name: squash_merge_commit_message
inputs.desc: Default message for squash merge commits: 'PR_BODY', 'COMMIT_MESSAGES', or 'BLANK'.

inputs.name: use_squash_pr_title_as_default
inputs.desc: DEPRECATED: Use `squash_merge_commit_title`. Default to pull request title for squash-merge commits.

name: Create an organization variable
description: Creates a new, uniquely named GitHub Actions variable for an organization, with configurable repository access visibility (all, private, or selected).

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The name of the GitHub organization. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: name
inputs.desc: The name of the organization variable. Must be unique at the organization level.

inputs.name: value
inputs.desc: The value of the organization variable.

inputs.name: visibility
inputs.desc: Controls which repositories in the organization can access the variable. Accepted values are 'all' (all repositories), 'private' (only private repositories), or 'selected' (specific repositories designated by `selected_repository_ids`).

inputs.name: selected_repository_ids
inputs.desc: A list of repository integer IDs that can access this organization variable. This field is required and only applicable when `visibility` is set to 'selected'.

name: Create an organization webhook
description: Creates a webhook for a GitHub organization to deliver event notifications to a configured URL.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The name of the GitHub organization. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: name
inputs.desc: The name of the webhook. Must be "web" for organization webhooks. Defaults to "web".

inputs.name: active
inputs.desc: Boolean indicating if the webhook is active. `true` sends notifications, `false` does not. Defaults to `true`.

inputs.name: events
inputs.desc: List of event names to trigger the webhook. Use `['*']` for all events. Defaults to `['push']` if not specified. See GitHub docs for event types.

inputs.name: config__url
inputs.desc: Required. The target URL for webhook payload delivery. This URL is a mandatory part of the webhook's `config` object and will receive POST requests for subscribed events.

inputs.name: config__secret
inputs.desc: Optional secret for generating an HMAC hex digest for payload verification (X-Hub-Signature-256 header). If provided, this is used as the key.

inputs.name: config__password
inputs.desc: Password for basic authentication if the webhook endpoint (`config_url`) requires it, corresponding to `config_username`.

inputs.name: config__username
inputs.desc: Username for basic authentication if the webhook endpoint (`config_url`) requires it. Use only if your endpoint is protected by basic auth.

inputs.name: config__content__type
inputs.desc: The media type for serializing webhook payloads. Supported: `json`, `form`. GitHub API defaults to `form` if this field is not set or is `None`.

inputs.name: config__insecure__ssl
inputs.desc: Controls SSL certificate verification for `config_url`. '1' disables verification (not recommended), '0' enables. GitHub API defaults to '0' (verification enabled) if this field is not set or is `None`.

name: Create a project card
description: Creates a project card in a GitHub project column (classic projects only). DEPRECATION NOTICE: GitHub Projects (classic) was sunset on August 23, 2024. This API only works with existing classic project columns. New classic projects cannot be created. Consider using GitHub Projects V2 (GraphQL API) instead. The request body must contain either: - `note`: A text note for the card - OR `content_id` AND `content_type`: To associate an issue or pull request

inputs.name: note
inputs.desc: The text content for the card. Use this to create a note-only card. Either 'note' OR both 'content_id' and 'content_type' must be provided.

inputs.name: column_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the project column where the card will be created. This value is used as a path parameter in the API request URL (e.g., `/projects/columns/{column_id}/cards`).

inputs.name: content_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the issue or pull request to associate with this card. Required when content_type is specified. Get this from the issue/PR details (it's the numeric ID, not the issue number).

inputs.name: content_type
inputs.desc: Type of content to associate with the card.

name: Create a project column (Classic - Deprecated)
description: Creates a new column in a GitHub project (classic). DEPRECATION NOTICE: GitHub Classic Projects (V1) and its REST API were sunset on April 1, 2025. This action will return a 404 error on GitHub.com. For new projects, use GitHub Projects V2 which uses the GraphQL API instead. See: https://github.blog/changelog/2024-05-23-sunset-notice-projects-classic/

inputs.name: name
inputs.desc: The name for the new project column.

inputs.name: project_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the target GitHub project (classic) where the new column will be created.

name: Create a pull request
description: Creates a pull request in a GitHub repository, requiring existing `base` and `head` branches; `title` or `issue` must be provided.

inputs.name: base
inputs.desc: The name of the branch you want the changes pulled into. This must be an existing branch on the current (target) repository. You cannot submit a pull request to one repository that requests a merge to a base branch of another repository.

inputs.name: body
inputs.desc: The detailed description or contents of the pull request.

inputs.name: head
inputs.desc: The name of the branch where your changes are implemented. IMPORTANT: This branch must already exist in the repository (or fork) before calling this action. You must first create the branch and push commits to it (using git or the 'Create a reference' action), then create the PR. For cross-repository pull requests, namespace `head` with the source owner and branch, like `username:branch`.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: draft
inputs.desc: Indicates whether the pull request should be created as a draft. Draft pull requests cannot be merged until marked as ready for review.

inputs.name: issue
inputs.desc: The number of an existing issue in the repository to convert into a pull request. If provided, the issue's title and body may be used for the pull request. Required if `title` is not specified.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account owner of the repository. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: title
inputs.desc: The title of the new pull request. Required unless `issue` is specified.

inputs.name: head_repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository (e.g., 'octocat/Hello-World') where the changes in the pull request were made. This field is required for cross-repository pull requests if both the source and target repositories are owned by the same organization but are different repositories.

inputs.name: maintainer_can_modify
inputs.desc: Indicates whether maintainers of the upstream repository can modify the pull request. This is primarily relevant for pull requests originating from forks.

name: Create a reference
description: Creates a NEW Git reference (branch or tag) in a repository. IMPORTANT: This action ONLY creates NEW references - if the reference already exists, it will fail with a 422 'Reference already exists' error. To update an existing reference, use the 'Update a reference' (GITHUB_UPDATE_A_REFERENCE) action instead. The repository must not be empty prior to this operation.

inputs.name: ref
inputs.desc: Fully qualified reference to create (e.g., `refs/heads/master`, `refs/tags/v1.0.0`). Must start with `refs/` and contain at least two slashes.

inputs.name: sha
inputs.desc: SHA-1 hash of an existing commit in the repository that the new reference will point to.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository without the `.git` extension. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

name: Create a registration token for an organization
description: Generates a temporary (one-hour) registration token to add a new self-hosted runner to an organization for GitHub Actions.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The name of the organization. This name is not case-sensitive.

name: Create a registration token for a repository
description: Generates a time-limited token required to register a new self-hosted runner with a specific repository.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The username of the account or the organization name that owns the repository. This field is not case-sensitive.

name: Create a release
description: Creates a release in a GitHub repository for a specified tag; the tag must be unique for published releases, and if a `discussion_category_name` is given, it must already exist.

inputs.name: body
inputs.desc: A detailed description of the release. Markdown formatting is supported.

inputs.name: name
inputs.desc: The title of the release. If omitted and `generate_release_notes` is false, it defaults to the `tag_name`.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the .git extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: draft
inputs.desc: Set to `true` to create an unpublished (draft) release. Defaults to `false`.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account owner of the repository (username or organization name). This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: tag_name
inputs.desc: The name of the tag for this release. This tag must be unique for published releases.

inputs.name: prerelease
inputs.desc: Set to `true` to identify this release as a pre-release. Defaults to `false`.

inputs.name: make_latest
inputs.desc: Specifies if this release should be marked as the latest for the repository. Draft releases and prereleases cannot be set as 'latest'.

inputs.name: target_commitish
inputs.desc: Specifies the commitish value (e.g., branch name, tag name, or commit SHA) from which the Git tag is created. Defaults to the repository's default branch if the tag specified in `tag_name` doesn't exist yet.

inputs.name: generate_release_notes
inputs.desc: Set to `true` to automatically generate the release title (`name`) and description (`body`) from the commit history since the last release. Defaults to `false`.

inputs.name: discussion_category_name
inputs.desc: If specified, a new discussion will be created in this category and linked to the release. The category must already exist in the repository's discussions.

name: Create a remove token for an organization
description: Generates a token, valid for one hour, to authenticate removing a self-hosted runner from an organization.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The unique identifier or name of the GitHub organization. This name is not case-sensitive. For example, if your organization's URL is github.com/my-org, 'my-org' is the value.

name: Create a remove token for a repository
description: Generates a temporary (one-hour validity) token required to unregister and remove a self-hosted runner from a repository.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: Name of the repository, without the `.git` extension (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: Username of the account owning the repository (not case-sensitive).

name: Create a reply for a review comment
description: Posts a reply to a specific review comment on a GitHub pull request, requiring the repository, pull request, and original comment to exist, and a non-empty reply body.

inputs.name: body
inputs.desc: The text of the reply to the review comment.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository without the `.git` extension. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: comment_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the top-level review comment to which the reply is being made. This must be the ID of a top-level review comment, not a reply to that comment. Replies to replies are not supported.

inputs.name: pull_number
inputs.desc: The number that identifies the pull request.

name: Create a repository dispatch event
description: Triggers a GitHub Actions workflow or a webhook on a repository by creating a repository dispatch event, allowing programmatic triggering of workflows based on events outside of GitHub.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account owner of the repository (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: event_type
inputs.desc: Custom webhook event name (100 characters or fewer).

inputs.name: client_payload
inputs.desc: JSON string containing extra information for the webhook event (max 10 top-level properties), passed to the triggered workflow or action. Example: '{"ref": "main", "sha": "abc123"}'

name: Create a repository for the authenticated user
description: Creates a new repository for the authenticated user, optionally within an organization if `team_id` is specified.

inputs.name: name
inputs.desc: The name of the repository. Must be unique within your account. Will be used in the repository URL (github.com/username/repo-name). Can only contain alphanumeric characters, hyphens, underscores, and periods.

inputs.name: private
inputs.desc: Whether the repository is private (true) or public (false).

inputs.name: team_id
inputs.desc: The ID of the team to be granted access. Only valid if creating in an organization.

inputs.name: has_wiki
inputs.desc: Whether the wiki is enabled.

inputs.name: homepage
inputs.desc: A URL with more information about the repository.

inputs.name: auto_init
inputs.desc: Whether to initialize the repository with a README.

inputs.name: has_issues
inputs.desc: Whether issues are enabled.

inputs.name: description
inputs.desc: A short description of the repository.

inputs.name: is_template
inputs.desc: Whether this repository is a template repository.

inputs.name: has_projects
inputs.desc: Whether projects are enabled.

inputs.name: has_downloads
inputs.desc: Whether downloads are enabled (deprecated by GitHub and may not be configurable).

inputs.name: has_discussions
inputs.desc: Whether discussions are enabled.

inputs.name: allow_auto_merge
inputs.desc: Whether to allow auto-merge on pull requests.

inputs.name: license_template
