description: Deletes a custom organization role (which should not be actively assigned) by its ID; a 204 No Content response indicates success.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The organization name (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: role_id
inputs.desc: Unique ID of the custom role to delete.

name: Delete dependabot secret by name
description: Deletes a specific Dependabot secret, identified by its name, from a given repository if both the repository and secret exist.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account owner of the repository (e.g., username or organization name). This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: secret_name
inputs.desc: The name of the Dependabot secret to delete. Secret names are case-sensitive and can only contain alphanumeric characters ([A-Z], [a-z], [0-9]) or underscores (_); spaces are not allowed.

name: Delete a deploy key
description: Deletes a specific deploy key from a repository; to change a key's properties or access scope, the existing key must be deleted and a new one created.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account owner of the repository. This name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: key_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier (ID) of the deploy key to be deleted.

name: Delete a deployment
description: Permanently deletes a specified *inactive* deployment from a repository.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: Repository name, without the `.git` extension (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: Username of the account owning the repository (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: deployment_id
inputs.desc: Unique identifier of the deployment to delete.

name: Delete a deployment branch policy
description: Deletes a specific deployment branch or tag policy, identified by its ID, from a given environment within a repository.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository without the `.git` extension. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: branch_policy_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the branch policy.

inputs.name: environment_name
inputs.desc: The name of the environment. The name must be URL encoded. For example, any slashes in the name must be replaced with `%2F`.

name: Delete a discussion
description: Deletes a specific team discussion, identified by its number, from an organization's team.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: Case-insensitive name of the organization.

inputs.name: team_slug
inputs.desc: URL-friendly version (slug) of the team name.

inputs.name: discussion_number
inputs.desc: The unique number identifying the discussion to be deleted.

name: Delete a discussion comment
description: Deletes a specific comment from an existing team discussion within an organization, provided the specified organization, team, discussion, and comment all exist.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The organization name (not case sensitive).

inputs.name: team_slug
inputs.desc: The slug of the team name (URL-friendly version).

inputs.name: comment_number
inputs.desc: Unique number identifying the comment.

inputs.name: discussion_number
inputs.desc: Unique number identifying the discussion.

name: Delete authenticated user email address
description: Sends an empty request body to `DELETE /user/emails` to attempt deletion of user email addresses; the API typically requires specific emails, so outcome may vary.

inputs.name: emails
inputs.desc: Array of email addresses to delete from the authenticated user account.

name: Delete an environment
description: Deletes an existing deployment environment from an existing repository.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The username or organization name that owns the repository. This is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: environment_name
inputs.desc: The name of the environment to delete. The name must be URL encoded; for example, slashes (`/`) should be replaced with `%2F`.

name: Delete an environment secret
description: Deletes an existing and accessible secret from a specified environment within a GitHub repository, returning an empty dictionary on success or error details otherwise.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The username or organization name that owns the repository. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: secret_name
inputs.desc: The name of the secret to be deleted. GitHub stores and processes secret names as uppercase (e.g., `my_secret` becomes `MY_SECRET`).

inputs.name: environment_name
inputs.desc: The name of the environment from which the secret will be deleted. This name must be URL-encoded if it contains special characters (e.g., slashes `/` should be replaced with `%2F`).

name: Delete an environment variable
description: Deletes a named environment variable from a specified, existing environment within a GitHub repository.

inputs.name: name
inputs.desc: The name of the environment variable to delete.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository without the `.git` extension. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: environment_name
inputs.desc: The name of the environment. Must be URL-encoded if it contains special characters (e.g., slashes `/` should be `%2F`).

name: Delete a gist
description: Permanently deletes an existing GitHub gist, specified by its `gist_id`; this action is destructive and cannot be undone.

inputs.name: gist_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the gist to be deleted.

name: Delete a gist comment
description: Deletes a specific comment from a GitHub Gist using its `gist_id` and `comment_id`.

inputs.name: gist_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the Gist from which the comment will be deleted.

inputs.name: comment_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the comment to be deleted.

name: Delete GitHub Actions cache by ID
description: Deletes a specific GitHub Actions cache from a repository using its unique `cache_id`.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The username or organization name that owns the repository.

inputs.name: cache_id
inputs.desc: The unique ID of the GitHub Actions cache to delete.

name: Delete a GitHub Pages site
description: Deletes the GitHub Pages site for the specified repository; completes without error if no site is currently enabled.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account owner of the repository (e.g., a user or organization). This name is not case-sensitive.

name: Delete an issue comment
description: Permanently deletes a specific comment by its ID from an issue or pull request, if the repository exists and the comment ID is valid.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: Name of the repository, without the `.git` extension (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: Username of the account owning the repository (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: comment_id
inputs.desc: Unique identifier of the comment to delete.

name: Delete an issue comment reaction
description: Deletes a reaction from an issue comment in a repository; the repository, comment, and reaction must exist.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The username of the account that owns the repository. This is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: comment_id
inputs.desc: The unique numeric identifier for the issue comment from which the reaction will be deleted.

inputs.name: reaction_id
inputs.desc: The unique numeric identifier for the reaction to be deleted.

name: Delete an issue reaction
description: Permanently removes a specific reaction from an issue in a GitHub repository.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository without the `.git` extension. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: reaction_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the reaction to be deleted.

inputs.name: issue_number
inputs.desc: The unique number identifying the issue from which the reaction will be deleted.

name: Delete a label
description: Permanently removes an existing label from a repository.

inputs.name: name
inputs.desc: The name of the label to delete. Label names are case-insensitive. If the label name contains spaces or special characters, ensure it is correctly URL-encoded (e.g., 'help wanted' becomes 'help%20wanted').

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: REQUIRED. The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. Must be provided together with 'owner' to identify the repository (e.g., for 'github.com/octocat/Hello-World', repo is 'Hello-World'). This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: REQUIRED. The username or organization name that owns the repository. GitHub repositories are identified by owner/repo (e.g., 'octocat/Hello-World'), so both 'owner' and 'repo' must be provided together. This field is not case-sensitive.

name: Delete a milestone
description: Deletes the specified milestone if it exists; this operation is idempotent, typically returning a 404 if the milestone is not found or already deleted.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The username or organization name that owns the repository. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: milestone_number
inputs.desc: The unique number that identifies the milestone to be deleted.

name: Delete an organization
description: Deletes a GitHub organization and its repositories; this is a destructive action and the organization name will be unavailable for reuse for approximately 90 days.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The unique name of the organization to be deleted. This name is not case-sensitive.

name: Delete an organization secret
description: Permanently deletes a secret from a GitHub organization, making it inaccessible to GitHub Actions workflows or other tools.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The name of the organization. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: secret_name
inputs.desc: The name of the secret to be deleted.

name: Delete an organization variable
description: Deletes a named GitHub Actions variable from a specified organization.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The name of the organization. This parameter is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: name
inputs.desc: The name of the organization variable to delete. This parameter is not case-sensitive.

name: Delete an organization webhook
description: Deletes a specific webhook, identified by `hook_id`, from an existing organization.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The name of the organization. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: hook_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the webhook. This ID can be retrieved from the `X-GitHub-Hook-ID` header in a webhook delivery or by listing organization webhooks.

name: Delete a codespace from the organization
description: Permanently deletes a specific codespace belonging to a member of the specified organization.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The organization's name (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: username
inputs.desc: GitHub username of the user owning the codespace.

inputs.name: codespace_name
inputs.desc: Unique name of the codespace.

name: Delete a package for an organization
description: Deletes a specific package in an organization; cannot delete public packages with over 5,000 downloads.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: Name of the organization owning the package (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: package_name
inputs.desc: Unique name of the package to be deleted.

inputs.name: package_type
inputs.desc: Type of the package to be deleted. Packages in GitHub's Gradle registry have type `maven`. Docker images in GitHub's Container registry (`ghcr.io`) have type `container`. Use type `docker` for images previously in GitHub's Docker registry (`docker.pkg.github.com`), even if migrated to the Container registry.

name: Delete a package for the authenticated user
description: Permanently deletes a specific package owned by the authenticated user; public packages downloaded over 5,000 times cannot be deleted via this API.

inputs.name: package_name
inputs.desc: The unique name of the package to delete. For npm: supports scoped packages like '@scope/name'. For maven: use format 'com.example.package'. For container/docker: use image name like 'my-app'. The name must match exactly as it appears in the GitHub Packages registry.

inputs.name: package_type
inputs.desc: The type of package to delete. Supported types: 'npm' (Node.js packages), 'maven' (Java/Gradle packages), 'rubygems' (Ruby gems), 'docker' (legacy Docker images from docker.pkg.github.com), 'nuget' (.NET packages), 'container' (Docker images from ghcr.io). Note: GitHub Gradle packages use 'maven' type; ghcr.io images use 'container' type.

name: Delete package version
description: Tool to delete a package version using GitHub GraphQL API. Use when you need to remove a specific version of a package by its global node ID.

inputs.name: client_mutation_id
inputs.desc: A unique identifier for the client performing the mutation. Used to ensure idempotency of mutations.

inputs.name: package_version_id
inputs.desc: The global node ID of the package version to delete (e.g., 'MDEyOlBhY2thZ2VWZXJzaW9uMQ=='). This is the GraphQL ID, not the numeric REST API ID.

name: Delete package version for an organization
description: Deletes a specific package version within an organization; requires admin permissions for packages with over 5,000 downloads.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: Organization name (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: package_name
inputs.desc: Name of the package.

inputs.name: package_type
inputs.desc:
Package ecosystem. Notes:
- `maven`: For GitHub's Gradle registry packages.
- `container`: For Docker images in GitHub's Container registry (ghcr.io).
- `docker`: For images originally in GitHub's Docker registry (docker.pkg.github.com), including migrated ones.

inputs.name: package_version_id
inputs.desc: Unique ID of the package version to delete.

name: Delete package version for a user
description: Permanently and irreversibly deletes a specific version of a package owned by the specified user.

inputs.name: username
inputs.desc: The GitHub username of the account that owns the package.

inputs.name: package_name
inputs.desc: The name of the package to which the version belongs.

inputs.name: package_type
inputs.desc: The type of the package. Supported types include npm, maven, rubygems, docker, nuget, and container. Packages in GitHub's Gradle registry use the `maven` type. Docker images in GitHub's Container registry (ghcr.io) use the `container` type. The `docker` type can be used for images previously in GitHub's Docker registry (docker.pkg.github.com), even if migrated.

inputs.name: package_version_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the package version to be deleted.

name: Delete a pending review for a pull request
description: Deletes a pending (unsubmitted) review from a pull request; this is only possible if the review has not yet been submitted.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account owner of the repository. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: review_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the pending review to be deleted.

inputs.name: pull_number
inputs.desc: The number that identifies the pull request.

name: Delete a project
description: Deletes the specified GitHub project (Projects V2) using the GraphQL API. Note: This action uses GitHub's Projects V2 GraphQL API. The legacy Projects (classic) REST API was sunset on April 1, 2025 and is no longer available. The project is permanently deleted and cannot be recovered. The user must have admin permissions for the project to delete it.

inputs.name: project_id
inputs.desc: The unique GraphQL node ID of the project to be deleted (e.g., 'PVT_kwDOBcMqx84ABdPx'). This is returned from project creation or listing operations.

name: Delete a project card (Classic - Deprecated)
description: Deletes a project card from a GitHub 'Project (classic)'; this operation is idempotent. DEPRECATION NOTICE: GitHub Classic Projects (V1) and its REST API were sunset on April 1, 2025. This action may return a 404 error on GitHub.com. For new projects, use GitHub Projects V2 which uses the GraphQL API instead. See: https://github.blog/changelog/2024-05-23-sunset-notice-projects-classic/

inputs.name: card_id
inputs.desc: The unique numerical identifier of the project card to be deleted.

name: Delete a project field (column)
description: Deletes a project field (column) from a GitHub Projects V2 project using the GraphQL API. IMPORTANT: GitHub Classic Projects REST API was sunset on April 1, 2025. This action now uses the GitHub Projects V2 GraphQL API with the deleteProjectV2Field mutation. Note: The built-in Status field cannot be deleted as it is a core field in GitHub Projects V2. Only custom fields can be deleted.

inputs.name: field_id
inputs.desc: The node ID of the project field to be deleted. This is a GraphQL node ID (e.g., 'PVTF_...').

name: Delete project item for user
description: Tool to delete a project item for a user in GitHub Projects V2. Use when you need to remove an item from a user's project. Requires the project item's GraphQL node ID (starts with 'PVTI_'). Uses the GitHub GraphQL API deleteProjectV2Item mutation.

inputs.name: item_id
inputs.desc: The GraphQL node ID of the project item to delete (e.g. 'PVTI_lADOBCxhzs4A...').

inputs.name: username
inputs.desc: The handle for the GitHub user account.

inputs.name: project_number
inputs.desc: The project's number.

name: Delete a pull request comment reaction
description: Deletes a specific reaction from a pull request review comment, provided the comment and reaction exist on that comment within the specified repository.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: Name of the repository, without the `.git` extension; not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: Account owner (username or organization) of the repository; not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: comment_id
inputs.desc: Unique ID of the pull request review comment.

inputs.name: reaction_id
inputs.desc: Unique ID of the reaction to delete.

name: Delete pull request review protection
description: Disables the requirement for pull request reviews before merging for a specific, existing branch in an existing repository; this action is idempotent and will succeed even if the protection is not currently enabled.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: Name of the repository, without the `.git` extension (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: Username or organization name of the repository owner (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: branch
inputs.desc: Name of the branch to remove pull request review protection from (wildcards not supported; use GraphQL API for wildcards).

name: Delete repo codespace secret by name
description: Deletes a specific Codespace secret from a repository by its name; this action is idempotent and will succeed even if the secret doesn't exist.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The username of the account or the name of the organization that owns the repository. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: secret_name
inputs.desc: The name of the Codespace secret to be deleted.

name: Delete social accounts for the authenticated user
description: Deletes currently linked social media account URLs from the authenticated user's GitHub profile.

inputs.name: account_urls
inputs.desc: Absolute URLs of the social media profiles to be unlinked from the GitHub profile.

name: Delete team discussion comment reaction
description: Deletes a reaction from a team discussion comment, given the organization name, team slug, discussion number, comment number, and reaction ID.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: team_slug
inputs.desc: The slug of the team name (URL-friendly version).

inputs.name: reaction_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the reaction to be deleted.

inputs.name: comment_number
inputs.desc: The number that uniquely identifies the comment within the discussion.

inputs.name: discussion_number
inputs.desc: The number that uniquely identifies the discussion.

name: Delete team discussion reaction
description: Permanently deletes a specific reaction from a team discussion within an organization.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The organization name. This name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: team_slug
inputs.desc: The slug of the team name, which is a URL-friendly version of the team name.

inputs.name: reaction_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the reaction to be deleted.

inputs.name: discussion_number
inputs.desc: The unique number identifying the specific discussion within the team.

name: Delete User List
description: Tool to delete a GitHub user list using GraphQL. Use when you need to remove a user list from a GitHub account.

inputs.name: listId
inputs.desc: The ID of the user list to delete. This is a GitHub global node ID for the list.

inputs.name: clientMutationId
inputs.desc: A unique identifier for the client performing the mutation. Used to track requests.

name: Delete a package for a user
description: Deletes a package owned by the specified user, requiring admin permissions for the authenticated user; deletion of public packages with over 5,000 downloads may require GitHub support.

inputs.name: username
inputs.desc: The GitHub username of the account that owns the package.

inputs.name: package_name
inputs.desc: The unique name of the package to be deleted.

inputs.name: package_type
inputs.desc: Ecosystem of the package. Packages in GitHub's Gradle registry use `maven`. Docker images pushed to GitHub's Container registry (`ghcr.io`) use `container`. The `docker` type can find images from the old Docker registry (`docker.pkg.github.com`), even if migrated.

name: Delete workflow run logs
description: Deletes all logs for a specific workflow run in a GitHub repository, provided the repository and run exist.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository without the `.git` extension. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: run_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the workflow run.

name: Disable a custom protection rule for an environment
description: Disables a specific, currently active custom deployment protection rule for an existing environment within a GitHub repository.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository (without the `.git` extension). This name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account owner of the repository (e.g., a user or organization). This name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: environment_name
inputs.desc: The name of the environment. This name must be URL encoded; for example, any slashes (`/`) in the name must be replaced with `%2F`.

inputs.name: protection_rule_id
inputs.desc: The unique integer identifier of the custom deployment protection rule to be disabled.

name: Disable a workflow
description: Disables a specified workflow (by ID or filename) in a given GitHub repository, preventing new automatic triggers; any in-progress runs will continue.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account owner of the repository (username or organization name). This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: workflow_id
inputs.desc: The ID of the workflow or the workflow's file name (e.g., `main.yml` or `ci.yaml`). You can obtain the workflow ID by listing workflows in a repository.

name: Disable private vulnerability reporting for a repository
description: Disables private vulnerability reporting for an existing GitHub repository, preventing direct private vulnerability reports to maintainers via GitHub's interface for this repository.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account owner of the repository (e.g., a GitHub username or organization name). This field is not case-sensitive.

name: Disable Repository Actions In Org
description: Removes a repository from the list of selected repositories enabled for GitHub Actions in an organization. Only works when the organization's `enabled_repositories` policy is set to `selected`. Requires admin:org scope.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The GitHub organization name (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: repository_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the repository.

name: Dismiss a review for a pull request
description: Dismisses an APPROVED or CHANGES_REQUESTED review on a pull request with a mandatory explanatory message. IMPORTANT: Only reviews in APPROVED or CHANGES_REQUESTED state can be dismissed. Reviews in COMMENTED, PENDING, or already DISMISSED state will return a 422 error. To dismiss a review on a protected branch, you must be a repository admin or be authorized to dismiss pull request reviews.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: event
inputs.desc: The event type for dismissing the review. Must be set to DISMISS.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The username or organization name that owns the repository. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: message
inputs.desc: The reason for dismissing the review. This is a required field.

inputs.name: review_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier for the review to be dismissed. The review must be in APPROVED or CHANGES_REQUESTED state.

inputs.name: pull_number
inputs.desc: The unique number identifying the pull request within the repository.

name: Download an artifact
description: Downloads a GitHub Actions workflow artifact as a ZIP file. Returns the artifact as a downloadable file. Use GITHUB_LIST_ARTIFACTS_FOR_A_REPOSITORY or GITHUB_LIST_WORKFLOW_RUN_ARTIFACTS to get valid artifact IDs first.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: Repository name, without the `.git` extension (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: Username or organization name of the repository owner (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: artifact_id
inputs.desc: Unique numeric identifier of the artifact. Obtain from GITHUB_LIST_ARTIFACTS_FOR_A_REPOSITORY or GITHUB_LIST_WORKFLOW_RUN_ARTIFACTS actions.

inputs.name: archive_format
inputs.desc: Archive format for the download. GitHub API only supports 'zip'. Defaults to 'zip'.

name: Download a repository tarball
description: Downloads a repository's source code as a tarball (.tar.gz) archive for a specific Git reference, if the repository is accessible.

inputs.name: ref
inputs.desc: A Git reference (e.g., branch name, tag name, or commit SHA) that points to the specific version of the repository content to be downloaded as a tarball archive. For example, `main` for the main branch, `v1.0.0` for a tag, or a full commit SHA.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository without the `.git` extension. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

name: Download a repository archive ZIP
description: Downloads a repository's source code as a ZIP archive for a specific Git reference (branch, tag, or commit SHA). IMPORTANT SIZE LIMITATION: This action may fail with 'payload too large' errors for large repositories due to platform size restrictions. If you encounter size limit issues, consider these alternatives: - Use GITHUB_GET_REPOSITORY_CONTENT to fetch specific files or directories (also lists directory contents) - Clone the repository using git if you need the full codebase Best suited for: small to medium repositories, downloading specific tagged releases, or quick code inspection.

inputs.name: ref
inputs.desc: The Git reference for the archive. This can be a branch name, a tag name, or a commit SHA.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The username of the account that owns the repository. This is not case-sensitive.

name: Download job logs for a workflow run
description: Downloads logs for a specific job in a GitHub Actions workflow run, contingent on the repository's existence and the job ID being valid and having produced logs.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account owner of the repository (e.g., user or organization). This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: job_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the job for which to download logs. You can obtain this from the 'List jobs for a workflow run' endpoint.

name: Download workflow run attempt logs
description: Downloads a ZIP archive of logs for a specific workflow run attempt. Returns a downloadable ZIP file containing logs for all jobs and steps in the workflow run.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository without the `.git` extension. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: run_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the workflow run.

inputs.name: attempt_number
inputs.desc: The attempt number of the workflow run.

name: Download workflow run logs
description: Downloads logs for a specific GitHub Actions workflow run as a ZIP archive containing log files for each job in the workflow.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository (e.g., 'Spoon-Knife') without the `.git` extension. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account owner of the repository (e.g., 'octocat'). This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: run_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the workflow run (e.g., 12345).

name: Enable a workflow
description: Reactivates a currently disabled GitHub Actions workflow within a repository using its workflow ID or filename.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This value is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The username or organization name that owns the repository. This value is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: workflow_id
inputs.desc: The ID of the workflow to be enabled. You can provide either the numeric workflow ID (e.g., 30433642) or the workflow filename (e.g., 'ci.yaml', 'data.yml').

name: Enable GitHub actions in selected repositories
description: Sets the specific repositories that can use GitHub Actions within an organization by replacing the current list; only applies if the organization's Actions policy is 'selected repositories'.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: Name of the GitHub organization (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: selected_repository_ids
inputs.desc: List of unique repository IDs for which GitHub Actions will be enabled. This list completely replaces the current selection of repositories.

name: Enable private vulnerability reporting for a repository
description: Enables private vulnerability reporting for a repository, allowing security researchers to privately submit vulnerability reports to maintainers.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account owner of the repository. This is typically the username or organization name and is not case-sensitive.

name: Enable repo for Github Actions
description: Adds a repository to the list of selected repositories enabled for GitHub Actions in an organization. Requires the organization's 'enabled_repositories' policy to be set to 'selected'.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The name of the GitHub organization. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: repository_id
inputs.desc: The unique numerical identifier of the GitHub repository.

name: Encrypt and update dev secret
description: Creates or updates a repository's development environment secret using an `encrypted_value` and its corresponding `key_id`; the secret must be pre-encrypted with the repository's Codespaces public key.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository without the `.git` extension. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account owner of the repository. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: key_id
inputs.desc: ID of the public key (retrieved from the GitHub API's 'Get a repository public key for Codespaces' endpoint: `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/codespaces/secrets/public-key`) used to encrypt the secret's value. If not provided, it will be fetched automatically.

inputs.name: secret_name
inputs.desc: The name of the development environment secret.

inputs.name: secret_value
inputs.desc: The plaintext value for your secret. This will be automatically encrypted using the repository's Codespaces public key before being sent to GitHub. If you already have an encrypted value, use the encrypted_value parameter instead.

inputs.name: encrypted_value
inputs.desc: Value for your secret, already encrypted using the public key retrieved from the GitHub API's 'Get a repository public key for Codespaces' endpoint (`GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/codespaces/secrets/public-key`). Use this if you have pre-encrypted the value yourself.

name: Encrypt org dev env secret
description: Creates or updates an organization's GitHub Codespaces secret using an encrypted value and its corresponding public key ID.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The GitHub organization name (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: key_id
inputs.desc: ID of the public key (from GET /orgs/{org}/codespaces/secrets/public-key) used to encrypt the secret's value. If not provided, it will be fetched automatically when encrypting.

inputs.name: visibility
inputs.desc: Visibility of the secret. 'selected' requires `selected_repository_ids`.

inputs.name: secret_name
inputs.desc: Name of the secret to create or update.

inputs.name: secret_value
inputs.desc: The plaintext value for your secret. This will be automatically encrypted using the organization's Codespaces public key before being sent to GitHub. If you already have an encrypted value, use the encrypted_value parameter instead.

inputs.name: encrypted_value
inputs.desc: Encrypted secret value, using the public key from 'Get an organization public key' (GET /orgs/{org}/codespaces/secrets/public-key). Use this if you have pre-encrypted the value yourself. If not provided and secret_value is given, encryption will be done automatically.

inputs.name: selected_repository_ids
inputs.desc: Repository IDs that can access the secret; required if visibility is 'selected'.

name: Export a codespace for the authenticated user
description: Triggers an export of a user's specified codespace, automatically stopping it if active, and returns its export status and download URL.

inputs.name: codespace_name
inputs.desc: Unique name of the codespace to be exported.

name: Export an SBOM for a repository
description: Exports the software bill of materials (SBOM) in SPDX JSON format for a repository, if its dependency graph is enabled and it has at least one commit.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The username or organization name that owns the repository. This field is not case-sensitive.

name: Find Pull Requests
description: Primary tool to find and search pull requests. Supports filtering by repository, author, state, labels, and merge status, and returns structured PR data for reliable use in workflows. GitHub search results are capped at ~1000 total items; narrow filters when totals approach this limit. `created_since`/`updated_since` filter on creation/update timestamps, not `merged_at`; apply merge-date filtering client-side.

inputs.name: page
inputs.desc: Page number of results to fetch.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: Filter by specific repository. Can be provided in 'owner/repo' format (e.g., 'facebook/react'), or as just the repo name when used with the owner parameter. When owner and repo are both provided and have the same value, they are combined as 'owner/repo'. When omitted along with owner, search scope is determined by for_authenticated_user flag. The repository must exist and be accessible with your token. Prefer 'owner/repo' format; bare repo name without the `owner` parameter causes invalid repository format errors.

inputs.name: sort
inputs.desc: Field to sort results by. Set explicitly to 'updated' with `order=desc` when recency matters; default behavior may return best-match relevance ordering instead.

inputs.name: label
inputs.desc: Filter by label name.

inputs.name: order
inputs.desc: Sort order (ascending or descending).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: Filter by repository owner (user or organization). Can be used alone to search all repositories for an owner, or combined with repo parameter to specify owner and repo separately. When both owner and repo are provided, the search is scoped to 'owner/repo'. When omitted along with repo, search scope is determined by for_authenticated_user flag. The owner must exist and repositories must be accessible with your token.

inputs.name: query
inputs.desc: Optional search query for PR title, description, or commit messages. Supports GitHub search syntax including label filters (e.g., 'label:bug'), logical operators (AND, OR, NOT), and text search. If not provided, PRs will be listed based on other filter parameters. Type qualifiers (type:pr, is:pr, is:issue, type:issue) are automatically normalized. When using logical operators with qualifiers like labels, the query is automatically grouped for proper parsing. When omitting query, ensure at least one structured filter parameter is provided; passing no query and no filters may cause validation errors. Prefer dedicated parameters (`label`, `state`, `author`) over embedding qualifiers in the query string. Exact title matching is not supported; post-filter on title client-side if needed.

inputs.name: state
inputs.desc: Filter by PR state: 'open', 'closed', or 'all'.

inputs.name: author
inputs.desc: Filter by PR author username. Must be a valid GitHub username (alphanumeric characters and hyphens only, cannot start or end with hyphen). No dots or other special characters allowed.

inputs.name: assignee
inputs.desc: Filter by PR assignee username. Use '@me' for current user. Must be a valid GitHub username (alphanumeric characters and hyphens only, cannot start or end with hyphen). No dots or other special characters allowed.

inputs.name: language
inputs.desc: Filter by programming language.

inputs.name: mentions
inputs.desc: Filter by username mentioned in PR. Must be a valid GitHub username (alphanumeric characters and hyphens only, cannot start or end with hyphen). No dots or other special characters allowed.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.desc: Number of results per page (maximum 100). GitHub caps total search results at ~1000 items; iterate `page` until results fewer than `per_page` are returned, and add `repo`, `label`, or date filters when totals approach 1000.

inputs.name: is_merged
inputs.desc: Filter by merge status. True for merged PRs, False for unmerged, None for all.

inputs.name: base_branch
inputs.desc: Filter by base branch name.

inputs.name: head_branch
inputs.desc: Filter by head branch name.

inputs.name: created_since
inputs.desc: Filter PRs created after this date (ISO 8601 format). Provide as UTC ISO 8601 timestamp to avoid off-by-one-day errors at range boundaries. Does not filter on `merged_at`.

inputs.name: updated_since
inputs.desc: Filter PRs updated after this date (ISO 8601 format).

inputs.name: response_detail
inputs.desc: Level of detail in the response. 'minimal' (default) returns an optimized response for AI agents. 'full' returns the complete GitHub API response.

inputs.name: for_authenticated_user
inputs.desc: Include private repositories accessible to the authenticated user in search results. When True without repo/owner, searches PRs involving you (author, assignee, mentions, commenter) across all accessible repos. When False without repo/owner, restricts to public repos only. When repo/owner is specified, this flag has no effect on scope. Set to True when searching private repositories; False searches all of GitHub and produces large noisy result sets.

name: Find Repositories
description: AI-optimized repository search with smart filtering by language, stars, topics, and ownership. Builds intelligent search queries and returns clean, actionable repository data. Check `incomplete_results` in the response — when true, results are non-exhaustive. Search endpoints are rate-limited to ~30 requests/minute (vs. 5000/hour for general REST); apply backoff on 403/429. Total results are capped at ~1000 per query regardless of pagination.

inputs.name: page
inputs.desc: Page number of results to fetch.

inputs.name: sort
inputs.desc: Field to sort results by.

inputs.name: order
inputs.desc: Sort order (ascending or descending).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: Filter by specific user or organization. Do NOT use this parameter if your 'query' already contains 'user:', 'org:', or 'repo:' qualifiers - they will conflict. Use either 'owner' parameter OR search qualifiers in 'query', not both.

inputs.name: query
inputs.desc: Search query for repository name, description, or README content. Accepts both 'query' and 'q' as parameter names. Supports OR operator for alternatives (e.g., 'bug OR error'). Multi-word phrases in OR expressions are automatically quoted. GitHub limits OR expressions to 6 terms maximum; queries exceeding this limit are truncated. If using GitHub qualifiers like 'repo:', 'user:', or 'org:', they must be properly formatted (e.g., 'repo:owner/name', 'user:username', 'org:orgname'). Note: 'owner:' is not a valid GitHub qualifier; if used, it will be automatically transformed to 'user:'. Must be non-empty; blank strings trigger a 'Search query cannot be empty' validation error.

inputs.name: topic
inputs.desc: Filter by repository topic/tag.

inputs.name: archived
inputs.desc: Filter by archived status. None means include both.

inputs.name: language
inputs.desc: Filter by programming language.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.desc: Number of results per page (maximum 100). GitHub caps total results at ~1000 per query; for exhaustive searches, split by `language`, `topic`, `min_stars`/`max_stars`, or `owner`.

inputs.name: max_stars
inputs.desc: Maximum number of stars the repository should have.

inputs.name: min_forks
inputs.desc: Minimum number of forks the repository should have.

inputs.name: min_stars
inputs.desc: Minimum number of stars the repository should have.

inputs.name: fork_filter
inputs.desc: How to handle forks: 'include' (include forks), 'exclude' (exclude forks), 'only' (only forks).

inputs.name: response_detail
inputs.desc: Level of detail in the response. 'minimal' (default) returns an optimized response for AI agents. 'full' returns the complete GitHub API response.

inputs.name: for_authenticated_user
inputs.desc: Search only in repositories accessible to the authenticated user (including private repos). Requires token scopes `repo`/`read:org`; sparse results compared to GitHub UI typically indicate insufficient token scopes.

name: Follow Organization (GraphQL)
description: Tool to follow a GitHub organization using GraphQL. Use when you need to make the authenticated user follow an organization.

inputs.name: organizationId
inputs.desc: The global GraphQL node ID of the organization to follow. Must be a valid organization node ID.

inputs.name: clientMutationId
inputs.desc: A unique identifier for the client performing the mutation. Used to track requests.

name: Follow a user
description: Allows the authenticated user to follow the GitHub user specified by `username`; this action is idempotent and the user cannot follow themselves.

inputs.name: username
inputs.desc: The GitHub username (handle) of the user to be followed by the authenticated user.

name: Follow User (GraphQL)
description: Tool to follow a GitHub user using GraphQL. Use when you need to make the authenticated user follow another user.

inputs.name: userId
inputs.desc: The global Node ID of the user to follow. Must be a valid GitHub user ID in the format 'MDQ6VXNlcjU4MzIzMQ=='.

inputs.name: clientMutationId
inputs.desc: A unique identifier for the client performing the mutation. Used to track requests.

name: Force cancel a workflow run
description: Forcefully cancels a queued or in-progress GitHub Actions workflow run, bypassing conditions like always() that would otherwise continue execution. Only use when the standard cancel endpoint fails. Cannot cancel completed workflow runs. Requires write permissions to the repository.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository without the `.git` extension. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: run_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the workflow run. Note: Only workflow runs with status 'queued' or 'in_progress' can be canceled. Completed workflow runs cannot be canceled.

name: Fork a gist
description: Forks a specified public gist, creating a copy under the authenticated user's account.

inputs.name: gist_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the gist to be forked.

name: Generate release notes content for a release
description: Generates Markdown release notes content (listing changes, pull requests, and contributors) for a GitHub repository release, customizable via tags and a configuration file.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The username or organization name that owns the repository. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: tag_name
inputs.desc: The tag name for which the release notes are to be generated. This can be an existing tag or a new tag name.

inputs.name: target_commitish
inputs.desc: Specifies the commitish value (e.g., a branch name, tag name, or commit SHA) that will be the target for the release's tag. This is required if the `tag_name` does not reference an existing tag. It is ignored if `tag_name` already exists.

inputs.name: previous_tag_name
inputs.desc: The name of the previous tag to use as the starting point for the release notes. This manually specifies the range of changes to be included. If omitted, GitHub will attempt to automatically determine the previous release.

inputs.name: configuration_file_path
inputs.desc: Specifies a path to a configuration file in the repository (e.g., `.github/release.yml`) containing settings for generating release notes. If unspecified, GitHub looks for a configuration file at ".github/release.yml" or ".github/release.yaml". If no such file is found, default settings are used.

name: Get a blob
description: Retrieves the raw, typically Base64-encoded, content of a file (blob) from a GitHub repository using its SHA hash, if the repository and blob SHA exist.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the '.git' extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The username or organization name that owns the repository. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: file_sha
inputs.desc: The SHA-1 hash of the file (blob) to retrieve.

name: Get a branch
description: Retrieves detailed information for a specified branch within a GitHub repository.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository without the `.git` extension. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: branch
inputs.desc: The name of the branch. Cannot contain wildcard characters. To use wildcard characters in branch names, use [the GraphQL API](https://docs.github.com/graphql). If 'HEAD' is provided, it will be resolved to the repository's default branch.

name: Get access restrictions
description: Lists users, teams, and GitHub Apps with push access to a branch; this branch must be protected in repository settings for detailed restrictions, otherwise expect a 404 or empty response.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account owner of the repository (e.g., username or organization name). This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: branch
inputs.desc: The name of the branch. Wildcard characters are not supported in this field. To manage branch protections with wildcard patterns, please refer to the GitHub GraphQL API documentation.

name: Get a commit
description: Retrieves a specific commit from a repository by its owner, name, and a valid commit reference (SHA, branch, or tag), supporting pagination for large diffs.

inputs.name: ref
inputs.desc: The commit reference. Can be a commit SHA (e.g., '`sha`'), a branch name (e.g., 'heads/`BRANCH_NAME`' or simply '`BRANCH_NAME`'), or a tag name (e.g., 'tags/`TAG_NAME`'). For more information, see "[Git References](https://git-scm.com/book/en/v2/Git-Internals-Git-References)" in the Git documentation.

inputs.name: page
inputs.desc: Page number for paginating the commit's diff if it's too large. For more information, see "[Using pagination in the REST API](https://docs.github.com/rest/using-the-rest-api/using-pagination-in-the-rest-api)."

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The username or organization name that owns the repository. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.desc: Number of results per page when paginating the commit's diff (max 100). For more information, see "[Using pagination in the REST API](https://docs.github.com/rest/using-the-rest-api/using-pagination-in-the-rest-api)."

name: Get admin branch protection
description: Checks if repository administrators are subject to the branch protection rules on a specific branch.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: Name of the repository, without the `.git` extension (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: Account owner of the repository (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: branch
inputs.desc: Name of the branch (wildcard characters not allowed; use GraphQL API for wildcard support).

name: Get all API versions
description: Retrieves all officially supported, date-based (e.g., "2022-11-28") versions of the GitHub REST API from the /versions endpoint.

name: Get all contributor commit activity
description: Retrieves commit activity (total commits, weekly additions/deletions/commits) for all contributors to a repository; may require a retry if GitHub returns 202 while preparing data.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: Repository name, excluding `.git` (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: Username of the repository owner (case-insensitive).

name: Get all deployment protection rules for an environment
description: Lists all enabled custom deployment protection rules for a specific environment in a repository; the environment must exist and be configured for deployments.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The username or organization name that owns the repository. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: environment_name
inputs.desc: The name of the environment. This name must be URL-encoded. For instance, if the environment name contains slashes (`/`), they should be replaced with `%2F`.

name: Get a milestone
description: Retrieves detailed information for a specific milestone within a GitHub repository by its number.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The username of the account that owns the repository. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: milestone_number
inputs.desc: The unique sequential number identifying the milestone within the repository.

name: Get an app
description: Retrieves publicly available information for an existing GitHub App, identified by its unique URL-friendly `app_slug`.

inputs.name: app_slug
inputs.desc: The app's unique, URL-friendly, lowercase, hyphenated identifier, derived from its name (e.g., 'my-awesome-app').

name: Get an artifact
description: Gets a specific artifact for a repository by `artifact_id`. This action retrieves the details of a specific artifact from a GitHub repository using the artifact's unique identifier. It allows users to access information about artifacts stored in the repository.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner

inputs.name: environment_name
inputs.desc: The name of the environment. The name must be URL encoded. For example, any slashes (`/`) in the name must be replaced with `%2F`.

name: Get a package for a user

inputs.name: username
inputs.desc: GitHub username of the package owner.

inputs.desc: Unique name of the package on GitHub Packages.

inputs.name: package_type
inputs.desc: Specifies the package's type. Note: Gradle packages use 'maven'; ghcr.io images use 'container'; 'docker' can find images migrated from docker.pkg.github.com to the Container registry.

name: Get a package for the authenticated user

inputs.desc: The unique name of the package as it appears in the registry.

inputs.name: package_type
inputs.desc: Type of the package. 'maven' for GitHub's Gradle registry; 'container' for Docker images in GitHub's Container registry (`ghcr.io`); 'docker' for images from GitHub's Docker registry (`docker.pkg.github.com`), including those migrated to the Container registry.

name: Get a package version for an organization
description: Retrieves detailed information for a specific version of a package within an organization.

inputs.desc: Unique package name.

inputs.name: package_type
inputs.desc: Package type. `maven` for GitHub Gradle registry. `container` for GitHub Container registry (`ghcr.io`) images. `docker` for images from GitHub Docker registry (`docker.pkg.github.com`), including migrated ones.

inputs.name: package_version_id
inputs.desc: Unique ID of the package version.

name: Get a package version for a user
description: Retrieves a specific public package version associated with a GitHub user.

inputs.name: username

inputs.name: package_name
inputs.desc: The name of the package.

inputs.name: package_type
inputs.desc: The type of package. 'maven' is used for Gradle packages. 'container' is for images in GitHub's Container registry (ghcr.io). 'docker' can be used for images previously in GitHub's Docker registry (docker.pkg.github.com).

inputs.name: package_version_id
inputs.desc: The unique numeric identifier of the specific package version. Obtain this ID by first listing package versions using the 'list_package_versions_for_a_package_owned_by_a_user' action.

name: Get authenticated user package version

inputs.name: package_version_id
inputs.desc: The unique numerical identifier (ID) of the specific package version.

name: Get a project
description: Retrieves detailed information for a specific GitHub project (V2) using its project number and owner. Note: This action uses GitHub's Projects V2 REST API. The legacy Projects (classic) REST API was sunset on April 1, 2025. The project is identified by its number (visible in the project URL) and owner (either a username or organization name). If no owner is specified, the authenticated user is assumed.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The organization that owns the project. If provided, this takes precedence over username.

inputs.name: username
inputs.desc: The GitHub username who owns the project. If not provided, defaults to the authenticated user.

inputs.name: project_number
inputs.desc: The project's sequential number (e.g., 1, 2, 3). This is displayed in the project URL as https://github.com/users/{username}/projects/{project_number}.

name: Get a project card
description: Retrieves all details of a specific project card, given its unique `card_id`. DEPRECATION NOTICE: GitHub Projects (classic) REST API was sunset on April 1, 2025. This action only works with existing classic project cards that were created before the sunset date. For new projects, use GitHub Projects V2 (GraphQL API) instead.

inputs.name: card_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the project card.

name: Get a project column (Classic - Deprecated)
description: Retrieves detailed information for a specific project column from GitHub Projects (classic). DEPRECATION NOTICE: GitHub Classic Projects (V1) and its REST API were sunset on April 1, 2025. This action will return 404 for most requests on GitHub.com. However, it may still work on GitHub Enterprise Server instances where classic projects are enabled. For new projects, use GitHub Projects V2 which uses the GraphQL API instead. See: https://github.blog/changelog/2024-05-23-sunset-notice-projects-classic/ Returns column details including: id, name, url, project_url, cards_url, node_id, created_at, and updated_at timestamps.

inputs.name: column_id
inputs.desc: The unique integer identifier of the project column to retrieve. This ID can be obtained from the 'list_project_columns' action or from the column's API URL (e.g., /projects/columns/12345).

name: Get a pull request
description: Retrieves a specific pull request from a GitHub repository using its owner, repository name, and pull request number.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the .git extension. This value is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The username of the account that owns the repository. This value is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: pull_number
inputs.desc: The number that identifies the specific pull request.

name: Get a reference
description: Retrieves a specific Git reference (e.g., a branch, tag, or fully qualified like 'heads/main') from a GitHub repository. The reference must exist in the repository; use 'list_matching_references' first if unsure what references are available.

inputs.name: ref
inputs.desc: The Git reference to retrieve. Must be formatted as `heads/<branch_name>` for branches, `tags/<tag_name>` for tags, or `pull/<number>/head` (or `pull/<number>/merge`) for pull requests. Simple branch names (e.g., `main`) are automatically converted to `heads/<name>`. The reference must exist in the repository; a 404 error is returned if not found. If unsure which references exist, use 'list_matching_references' or 'get_a_branch' first. Note: Commit SHAs are not supported by this endpoint; use commit-specific endpoints instead. Not all repositories use 'master'; many now use 'main' as the default branch.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The username or organization name that owns the repository. This field is not case-sensitive.

name: List repository files and directories
description: Browse and list all files/directories in a GitHub repository. Efficiently retrieves the complete repository structure in a single API call when used with recursive mode. Perfect for analyzing codebase structure, finding specific files, or getting an overview of repository contents without cloning.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: Repository name without owner or .git extension (e.g., for github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes, repo is 'kubernetes').

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: Repository owner username or organization name (e.g., for github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes, owner is 'kubernetes').

inputs.name: tree_sha
inputs.desc: Branch name (e.g., 'main', 'master'), tag name (e.g., 'v1.0.0'), commit SHA, or tree SHA. Directory paths are NOT supported - use a branch/tag to get the full tree, then extract the directory's SHA from results.

inputs.name: recursive
inputs.desc: Set to True (or '1'/'true') to fetch the ENTIRE repository file tree recursively (all files and directories in one call). Set to False or leave empty to fetch only the top-level files/folders. For browsing or analyzing the full repository structure, use recursive=True.

name: Get a user

inputs.name: hook_id

inputs.name: run_id

inputs.name: run_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier (ID) of the workflow run. This ID is specific to a repository and can be obtained from API calls that list workflow runs.

inputs.name: attempt_number
inputs.desc: The attempt number of the specific workflow run. Each re-run of a workflow creates a new attempt number, starting from 1.

inputs.name: exclude_pull_requests
inputs.desc: If `true`, excludes pull request data from the API response; the `pull_requests` array in the response will then be empty.

name: Get billing usage report for user
description: Retrieves the billing usage report for a specified GitHub user. Use when you need to analyze billing costs and usage patterns.

inputs.name: day
inputs.desc: If specified, only return results for a single day. The value of `day` is an integer between `1` and `31`. If no `year` or `month` is specified, the default `year` and `month` are used.

inputs.name: year
inputs.desc: If specified, only return results for a single year. The value of `year` is an integer with four digits representing a year. For example, `2025`. Default value is the current year.

inputs.name: month
inputs.desc: If specified, only return results for a single month. The value of `month` is an integer between `1` and `12`. If no year is specified the default `year` is used.

inputs.name: username
inputs.desc: The handle for the GitHub user account.

name: Get branch protection
description: Retrieves branch protection settings for a specific, existing, and accessible branch in a GitHub repository; protection feature availability varies by GitHub product plan.

inputs.name: repo

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: Account owner of the repository (username or organization name); not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: branch
inputs.desc: Name of the branch; wildcard characters are not supported (use GitHub GraphQL API for wildcard matching).

name: Get catalog models

name: Get a check run

inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

name: Get commit authors

NOTE: This endpoint has been deprecated by GitHub as of April 12, 2024, and is no longer available. The Source Imports REST API has been retired due to very low usage levels. Please use the GitHub Importer tool at https://github.com/new/import for repository imports.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner

inputs.name: since
inputs.desc: An optional author ID. If provided, only authors with an ID greater than this specified ID will be returned. This ID refers to the `id` field of an author object in the response, and can be used for paginating through the list of authors.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.desc: Number of results per page, maximum 100. Default: 30

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: Name of the repository, without '.git' extension. Not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: Username of the account owning the repository. Not case-sensitive.

inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The username or organization name that owns the repository. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.desc: The optional full path, relative to the repository root, of a specific dependency manifest file (e.g., `package-lock.json`, `pom.xml`). If provided, the diff will be scoped to this file.

inputs.desc: A string specifying the base and head Git revisions to compare, in the format `BASE_REF...HEAD_REF`. `BASE_REF` and `HEAD_REF` can be commit SHAs, branch names, or tags. For example, `main...develop` or `v1.0.0...v1.1.0`.

inputs.desc: If specified, only return results for a single day. The value of `day` is an integer between `1` and `31`. If no `year` or `month` is specified, the default `year` and `month` are used.

inputs.desc: If specified, only return results for a single year. The value of `year` is an integer with four digits representing a year. For example, `2025`. Default value is the current year.

inputs.name: sku
inputs.desc: The SKU to query for usage.

inputs.name: year
inputs.desc: If specified, only return results for a single year. The value of year is an integer with four digits representing a year (e.g., 2025). Default value is the current year.

inputs.name: month
inputs.desc: If specified, only return results for a single month. The value of month is an integer between 1 and 12. Default value is the current month. If no year is specified the default year is used.

name: Get Raw Repository Content
description: Tool to fetch raw file content from a GitHub repository. Returns raw bytes in whatever format the file uses (JSON, CSV, binary, etc.) — parsing and validation are the caller's responsibility. Ensure owner, repo, ref, and path are consistent when combining with other GitHub tools to avoid mismatched-ref errors.

inputs.name: ref
inputs.desc: The branch name, tag name, or commit SHA to fetch the file from. Defaults to the repository's default branch (usually 'main' or 'master'). Recommended to pass explicitly — omitting it fetches from the default branch, which may not match the ref used in other tool calls and can yield unexpected or missing content.

inputs.name: path
inputs.desc: The file path within the repository, relative to the repository root. Use forward slashes for directory separators (e.g., 'src/utils/helper.js'). Do not include leading slash. Do NOT pass a full GitHub URL - only the relative path within the repo. Must exactly match the file path as it exists in the repository — use GITHUB_GET_A_TREE to discover valid paths rather than guessing directory structure.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The repository name (not the full URL). This is the repository name shown in the repository URL (e.g., for github.com/torvalds/linux, the repo is 'linux'). Do not include '.git' suffix. Case-insensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The GitHub username or organization name that owns the repository. This is the account name shown in the repository URL (e.g., for github.com/torvalds/linux, the owner is 'torvalds'). Case-insensitive.

name: Get repository content
description: Retrieves a file's Base64 encoded content or lists a directory's contents from a GitHub repository.

inputs.name: ref
inputs.desc: The name of the commit, branch, or tag to read from. Use this parameter to specify which branch (e.g., 'feat/godlocal-dev', 'main', 'develop'), tag (e.g., 'v1.2.0'), or commit SHA you want to access. If not provided, the repository's default branch will be used. Note: Branch names should be passed here, NOT in the path parameter.

inputs.name: path
inputs.desc: Required. Path to file or directory in the repository. Empty string ('') retrieves root directory contents. Leading slashes auto-stripped. CRITICAL: Do NOT pass branch names here (e.g., 'feat/godlocal-dev', 'main'). Branch names belong in the `ref` parameter. Path should be file/directory path only (e.g., 'src/main.js', 'README.md'), NOT prefixed with branch name (use 'src/main.js' not 'main/src/main.js'). File paths must include extension (e.g., 'README.md', not 'README'). For common files (README, LICENSE, CHANGELOG) without extensions, action auto-tries common extensions (.md, .txt, .rst). Paths are case-sensitive. On 404 errors, list parent directory to verify exact path/casing.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: Required. The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: Required. The account owner of the repository. This name is not case sensitive. Can also accept full repository path in format 'owner/repo' - the repo part will be extracted automatically.

name: List commits
description: Lists commits for a GitHub repository. Requires 'owner' (username/org) and 'repo' (repository name) parameters. Optionally filter by SHA/branch, path, author, committer, or date range.

inputs.name: sha
inputs.desc: SHA hash or branch name to start listing commits from. If not provided, the GitHub API uses the repository's default branch (usually `main` or `master`).

inputs.name: page
inputs.desc: Page number of the results to fetch when using pagination.

inputs.name: path
inputs.desc: Only commits modifying this specific file path will be returned.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository without the `.git` extension (repository name only, not the full 'owner/repo' path). Must contain only alphanumeric characters, hyphens (-), underscores (_), and periods (.). Wildcards like '*' are not supported.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account owner of the repository (username or organization name only, not the full 'owner/repo' path). Must contain only alphanumeric characters and hyphens, cannot start/end with a hyphen, max 39 characters. Wildcards are not supported.

inputs.name: since
inputs.desc: Only commits created on or after this timestamp will be returned. This is a timestamp in ISO 8601 format: `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`.

inputs.name: until
inputs.desc: Only commits created before this timestamp will be returned. This is a timestamp in ISO 8601 format: `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`.

inputs.name: author
inputs.desc: Filter commits by the commit author's GitHub login or email address.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.desc: Number of results to return per page (max 100).

inputs.name: committer
inputs.desc: Filter commits by the commit committer's GitHub login or email address. The committer is the user who applied the patch and may differ from the original author.

inputs.name: per_page

inputs.name: pull_number

description: Lists discussions for a specific team within an organization, with options for sorting, pagination, and filtering by pinned status. Note: Team discussions are deprecated as of 2023-11-28. Consider using GitHub Discussions instead. This endpoint requires the authenticated user to be a member of the organization and have access to the team's discussions.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The organization's name (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: page
inputs.desc: Page number for pagination. Defaults to 1.

inputs.name: pinned
inputs.desc: Filter for pinned discussions. Use 'true' to show only pinned discussions. Omit to show all discussions.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.desc: Number of results per page (max 100). Defaults to 30.

inputs.name: direction
inputs.desc: Sort direction for results: 'asc' (oldest first) or 'desc' (newest first). Defaults to 'desc'.

inputs.name: team_slug
inputs.desc: The team's slug (URL-friendly name).

name: List email addresses for the authenticated user

This action retrieves the complete list of email addresses associated with the authenticated user, providing important details such as which email is primary and whether the emails are verified.

inputs.name: page
inputs.desc: Page number of the results to retrieve.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.desc: Number of results per page (max 100).

inputs.desc: Number of results per page, with a maximum of 100.

inputs.name: environment_name
inputs.desc: Name of the environment, which must be URL-encoded (e.g., slashes `/` replaced with `%2F`).

inputs.desc: Full Git reference for filtering caches (e.g., `refs/heads/<branch_name>` or `refs/pull/<pull_request_number>/merge`).

inputs.desc: Page number of results to retrieve (starts from 1).

name: List global security advisories
description: Lists GitHub's global security advisories, filterable by various attributes including ID, type, ecosystem, severity, and dates.

inputs.name: cwes
inputs.desc: Filter by Common Weakness Enumeration (CWE) identifiers. Accepts comma-separated string or multiple parameters. Example: `cwes=79,284`.

inputs.name: sort
inputs.desc: Property to sort results by (updated, published, epss_percentage, epss_percentile).

inputs.name: type
inputs.desc: Filter by advisory type (e.g., reviewed, malware). Defaults to 'reviewed'; if this default is active and no other search parameters are used, malware advisories are also excluded.

inputs.name: after
inputs.desc: Pagination cursor to retrieve results after this value.

inputs.name: before
inputs.desc: Pagination cursor to retrieve results before this value.

inputs.name: cve_id
inputs.desc: Filter by Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures (CVE) identifier.

inputs.name: affects
inputs.desc: Filter by affected 'package' or 'package@version'. Max 100 items. Example: `affects=pkg1,pkg2@1.0.0`.

inputs.name: ghsa_id
inputs.desc: Filter by GitHub Security Advisory (GHSA) identifier.

inputs.name: updated
inputs.desc: Filter by update date or date range. For syntax, see GitHub's 'understanding the search syntax' documentation.

inputs.name: modified
inputs.desc: Filter by last modification (update or publication) date or date range. For syntax, see GitHub's 'understanding the search syntax' documentation.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.desc: Number of results per page (max 100).

inputs.name: severity
inputs.desc: Filter by advisory severity (e.g., high, critical).

inputs.name: direction
inputs.desc: Sort direction for results (asc, desc).

inputs.name: ecosystem
inputs.desc: Filter by package ecosystem (e.g., npm, pip).

inputs.name: published
inputs.desc: Filter by publication date or date range. For syntax, see GitHub's 'understanding the search syntax' documentation.

inputs.name: is_withdrawn
inputs.desc: Filter for withdrawn advisories.

inputs.name: epss_percentage
inputs.desc: Filter by EPSS (Exploit Prediction Scoring System) percentage score. Use comparison operators (e.g., '>0.5', '<=0.1').

inputs.name: epss_percentile
inputs.desc: Filter by EPSS (Exploit Prediction Scoring System) percentile score. Use comparison operators (e.g., '>0.9', '>=0.5').

Returns notification threads plus polling headers (Last-Modified, ETag, X-Poll-Interval) for efficient polling. IMPORTANT: This endpoint requires a Personal Access Token (classic) with 'notifications' or 'repo' scope. It does NOT work with GitHub App tokens or fine-grained personal access tokens. If the token type is incompatible, a clear error message will be returned.

The 'role' field indicates the type of invitation: - 'direct_member': Regular organization member - 'admin': Organization administrator - 'billing_manager': Billing manager role - 'hiring_manager': Hiring manager role - 'reinstate': Reinstating a previous member Note: If the invitee is not a GitHub member, the 'login' field will be null and only the 'email' field will be populated.

For new projects, use GitHub Projects V2 which uses the GraphQL API instead.
