name: List public events for a network of repositories
description: Retrieves public events (up to 90 days old, newest first) for a GitHub repository network, including the repository and its forks.

inputs.name: page
inputs.desc: Specifies the page number for the results to be fetched. For further details, consult "[Using pagination in the REST API](https://docs.github.com/rest/using-the-rest-api/using-pagination-in-the-rest-api)."

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account owner of the repository. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.desc: Sets the number of results to return per page, with a maximum of 100. For further details, consult "[Using pagination in the REST API](https://docs.github.com/rest/using-the-rest-api/using-pagination-in-the-rest-api)."

name: List public events for a user
description: Retrieves a list of public events for a specified GitHub user, in reverse chronological order.

inputs.name: page
inputs.desc: Page number for paginated results.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.desc: Number of events to return per page (max 100).

inputs.name: username
inputs.desc: The GitHub username (e.g., 'octocat').

name: List public events received by a user
description: Lists public events for a specified GitHub user (e.g., activities in repositories they watch or are involved in); the target user's profile must be public, and if blocked by the authenticated user, a 404 error is returned.

inputs.name: page
inputs.desc: The page number for the set of results to be fetched. For more information, see "[Using pagination in the REST API](https://docs.github.com/rest/using-the-rest-api/using-pagination-in-the-rest-api)."

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.desc: The number of public events to retrieve per page. The maximum value is 100. For more information, see "[Using pagination in the REST API](https://docs.github.com/rest/using-the-rest-api/using-pagination-in-the-rest-api)."

inputs.name: username
inputs.desc: The GitHub username for the user whose received public events are to be listed.

name: List public gists
description: Lists public gists from GitHub, optionally filtering by a 'since' timestamp and supporting pagination; results are generally newest first.

inputs.name: page
inputs.desc: Page number of the results to fetch for pagination.

inputs.name: since
inputs.desc: Filters results to gists last updated at or after this ISO 8601 timestamp (e.g., `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`).

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.desc: Number of gists to return per page (maximum 100).

name: List public keys for a user
description: Lists the verified public SSH keys for a specified GitHub user.

inputs.name: page
inputs.desc: The page number of the results to fetch. For more information, see "[Using pagination in the REST API](https://docs.github.com/rest/using-the-rest-api/using-pagination-in-the-rest-api)."

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.desc: The number of results to return per page (maximum 100). For more information, see "[Using pagination in the REST API](https://docs.github.com/rest/using-the-rest-api/using-pagination-in-the-rest-api)."

inputs.name: username
inputs.desc: The GitHub username (handle) of the user whose public SSH keys are to be listed. For example, 'octocat'.

name: List public organization events
description: Lists public events for a specified GitHub organization, which must exist. This action retrieves the events that are publicly accessible for the organization specified. It returns a paginated list of events with details about each event.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The GitHub organization name (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: page
inputs.desc: Page number of results to retrieve (starts from 1).

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.desc: Number of results per page (maximum 100).

name: List public organization members
description: Lists users who have publicly declared their membership in a specified, existing GitHub organization.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The name of the GitHub organization. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: page
inputs.desc: Page number of the results to retrieve.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.desc: Number of results to return per page (maximum 100). Defaults to 30.

name: List public repositories
description: Lists all public repositories on GitHub; use the `since` parameter with a repository ID from a previous result for pagination.

inputs.name: since
inputs.desc: A repository ID. Returns only repositories with an ID greater than this value, for pagination.

name: List pull requests
description: Lists pull requests for a GitHub repository. REQUIRES 'owner' AND 'repo' parameters - both are MANDATORY and must be provided in every call. Does NOT support filtering by assignee or date range - use GITHUB_FIND_PULL_REQUESTS instead for searching PRs by assignee, author, or across multiple repositories.

inputs.name: base
inputs.desc: Filter by base branch name.

inputs.name: head
inputs.desc: Filter by head user/org and branch name, e.g., `user:ref-name`.

inputs.name: page
inputs.desc: Page number for results.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: [REQUIRED - MUST BE PROVIDED] The name of the repository (without the `.git` extension). This parameter is mandatory and cannot be omitted. Example: 'Spoon-Knife' or 'vscode'. To search PRs across multiple repos, use GITHUB_FIND_PULL_REQUESTS instead.

inputs.name: sort
inputs.desc: Sorts results. Valid values: 'created', 'updated', 'popularity', 'long-running'. 'popularity' is by comment count; 'long-running' by age and recent activity.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: [REQUIRED - MUST BE PROVIDED] The GitHub username or organization name that owns the repository. This parameter is mandatory and cannot be omitted. Example: 'octocat' or 'microsoft'. To search PRs across all repos without specifying owner, use GITHUB_FIND_PULL_REQUESTS instead.

inputs.name: state
inputs.desc: Filter by pull request state. Valid values: 'open', 'closed', 'all'.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.desc: Number of results per page (maximum 100).

inputs.name: direction
inputs.desc: Sort direction. Valid values: 'asc', 'desc'. Defaults to 'desc' if sort is 'created' or unspecified, else 'asc'.

name: List pull requests associated with a commit
description: Lists pull requests for a commit; returns merged PRs that introduced the commit if on the default branch, or open PRs including the commit if on other branches.

inputs.name: page
inputs.desc: Page number of results to retrieve.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: Repository name, without the `.git` extension (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: Account owner of the repository (GitHub username or organization name, case-insensitive).

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.desc: Number of results per page (max 100).

inputs.name: commit_sha
inputs.desc: SHA of the commit to list associated pull requests for.

name: List pull requests files
description: Lists all files (including additions, modifications, and removals) associated with a specific pull request in a GitHub repository.

inputs.name: page
inputs.desc: Page number of the results to retrieve for pagination.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account owner of the repository (e.g., a GitHub username or organization name). This field is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.desc: Number of file results to return per page (max 100).

inputs.name: pull_number
inputs.desc: The numeric identifier of the pull request whose files are to be listed.

name: List reactions for a commit comment
description: Lists reactions for a specific commit comment; this is a read-only operation.

inputs.name: page
inputs.desc: Page number for the results (1-indexed).

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: Repository name, excluding the '.git' extension (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: Owner of the repository (username or organization, case-insensitive).

inputs.name: content
inputs.desc: Filter by reaction type. If unspecified, all reaction types for the comment are returned.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.desc: Number of results per page (maximum 100).

inputs.name: comment_id
inputs.desc: Identifier of the commit comment.

name: List reactions for an issue
description: Lists reactions for a specific, existing issue within an accessible GitHub repository, optionally filtering by content type.

inputs.name: page
inputs.desc: The page number of the results to fetch for pagination.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The username of the account that owns the repository. This is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: content
inputs.desc: Filters reactions by a specific type. If omitted, all reactions to the issue are listed.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.desc: The number of results to return per page, up to a maximum of 100.

inputs.name: issue_number
inputs.desc: The unique number identifying the issue within the repository.

name: List reactions for an issue comment
description: Lists reactions for a specific issue comment in a GitHub repository, optionally filtering by content type.

inputs.name: page
inputs.desc: Page number of results to fetch.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account owner of the repository (e.g., a user or organization). This name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: content
inputs.desc: Filters reactions by their content type (e.g., '+1', 'laugh'). Omit to list all types of reactions.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.desc: Number of results to return per page (max 100).

inputs.name: comment_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the issue comment for which to list reactions.

name: List reactions for a pull request review comment
description: Lists reactions for a pull request review comment in a repository, optionally filtering by reaction type.

inputs.name: page
inputs.desc: Page number of the results to fetch.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: Name of the repository, without the `.git` extension (not case sensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: Account owner of the repository (not case sensitive).

inputs.name: content
inputs.desc: Filters reactions by a specific type; if omitted, all reactions are listed.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.desc: Number of results per page (max 100).

inputs.name: comment_id
inputs.desc: Unique identifier of the pull request review comment.

name: List reactions for a release
description: Lists all reactions, or optionally filters reactions by a specific content type, for a given GitHub release.

inputs.name: page
inputs.desc: Page number of the results to fetch. Starts from 1.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The GitHub account owner of the repository (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: content
inputs.desc: Supported reaction types for releases (note: -1 and confused are NOT supported for releases).

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.desc: Number of results per page. Range: 1-100, default: 30.

inputs.name: release_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the release. Obtain this from the List Releases API or from the release URL.

name: List reactions for a team discussion
description: Lists reactions for an existing team discussion within an organization. This endpoint allows you to retrieve all reactions (emoji responses) made to a specific team discussion. You can optionally filter by reaction type. Note: OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the `read:discussion` scope to use this endpoint.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The organization name. Account name of the organization (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: page
inputs.desc: The page number of results to fetch. Default: 1.

inputs.name: content
inputs.desc: Valid reaction content types for GitHub team discussions.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.desc: The number of results per page (max 100). Default: 30.

inputs.name: team_slug
inputs.desc: The team's slug (URL-friendly identifier). This is the lowercase, hyphenated version of the team name.

inputs.name: discussion_number
inputs.desc: The unique number that identifies the team discussion.

name: List reactions for a team discussion comment
description: Lists reactions for a specific comment in a team discussion within an organization, optionally filtering by content type.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The organization's name (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: page
inputs.desc: Page number of the results to retrieve.

inputs.name: content
inputs.desc: Filters reactions by a specific content type.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.desc: Number of results per page (max 100).

inputs.name: team_slug
inputs.desc: URL-friendly slug of the team name.

inputs.name: comment_number
inputs.desc: Unique identifier for the comment within the discussion.

inputs.name: discussion_number
inputs.desc: Unique identifier for the team discussion.

name: List release assets
description: Lists assets (e.g., compiled binaries, source code archives) for a specific GitHub release, identified by `release_id` which must be valid for an existing release in the repository.

inputs.name: page
inputs.desc: Page number of the results to fetch (starts from 1). For pagination details, see "[Using pagination in the REST API](https://docs.github.com/rest/using-the-rest-api/using-pagination-in-the-rest-api)."

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: Name of the repository, excluding the `.git` extension (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: Username or organization name of the repository owner (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.desc: Number of results per page (max 100). For pagination details, see "[Using pagination in the REST API](https://docs.github.com/rest/using-the-rest-api/using-pagination-in-the-rest-api)."

inputs.name: release_id
inputs.desc: Unique numerical identifier for the release. Obtainable by listing a repository's releases.

name: List releases
description: Retrieves a list of all releases (published, draft, and prerelease) for a specified repository.

inputs.name: page
inputs.desc: The page number of the results to fetch. For more information, see "[Using pagination in the REST API](https://docs.github.com/rest/using-the-rest-api/using-pagination-in-the-rest-api)."

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account owner of the repository. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.desc: The number of results to return per page. The maximum value is 100. For more information, see "[Using pagination in the REST API](https://docs.github.com/rest/using-the-rest-api/using-pagination-in-the-rest-api)."

name: List repo access by token
description: Lists repositories in an organization that a fine-grained personal access token has requested access to. Only GitHub Apps can use this endpoint. Requires 'Personal access token requests' organization permissions (read).

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: page
inputs.desc: The page number of the results to fetch.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.desc: The number of results per page (max 100).

inputs.name: pat_request_id
inputs.desc: Unique identifier of the request for access via fine-grained personal access token. Obtain this from the 'List PAT grant requests' endpoint.

name: List repo codespaces for authenticated user
description: Lists Codespaces the authenticated user can access within a specified, existing repository.

inputs.name: page
inputs.desc: Page number of the results to retrieve.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: Name of the repository, without the `.git` extension (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: Username or organization name of the account that owns the repository (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.desc: Number of results to return per page. Maximum: 100.

name: List repo dev container configs for user
description: Lists the `devcontainer.json` configurations available in a specified repository for use with GitHub Codespaces.

inputs.name: page
inputs.desc: Page number of the dev container configuration results to fetch.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The username or organization name that owns the repository (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.desc: Number of dev container configuration results to return per page (max 100).

name: List authenticated user's repository invitations
description: Lists all pending repository invitations for the authenticated user.

inputs.name: page
inputs.desc: Page number of the results to retrieve.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.desc: Number of results to return per page. Maximum allowed value is 100.

name: List repository notifications for the authenticated user
description: Retrieves notifications for the authenticated user from a specified repository, to which the user must have access.

inputs.name: all
inputs.desc: If `true`, shows notifications marked as read; otherwise, only unread notifications are returned.

inputs.name: page
inputs.desc: The page number of the results to retrieve.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: since
inputs.desc: Only includes notifications last updated on or after this ISO 8601 UTC timestamp (e.g., `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`).

inputs.name: before
inputs.desc: Only includes notifications last updated before this ISO 8601 UTC timestamp (e.g., `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`).

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.desc: The number of notifications to return per page. Maximum value is 100.

inputs.name: participating
inputs.desc: If `true`, shows only notifications in which the authenticated user is directly participating or was mentioned.

name: List repo secrets without values
description: Lists all Codespaces secrets available in a specific repository, without their encrypted values.

inputs.name: page
inputs.desc: Page number of the results to retrieve. Used for pagination.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The username of the account that owns the repository. This is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.desc: Number of secrets to return per page. Maximum value is 100.

name: List repos accessible to app installation
description: Lists repositories a GitHub App installation can access. IMPORTANT: This endpoint requires a GitHub App installation access token (ghs_* prefix) for authentication. OAuth/user access tokens (gho_*) or personal access tokens are not supported and will result in a 403 Forbidden error. Returns 'total_count' and 'repositories' array with pagination support.

inputs.name: page
inputs.desc: Page number of the results to fetch.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.desc: Number of results per page (max 100).

name: List repositories for a user
description: Lists public repositories for a specified GitHub user, who must have an existing account.

inputs.name: page
inputs.desc: Page number for results (starts from 1). See GitHub REST API pagination docs for more info.

inputs.name: sort
inputs.desc: Sort criterion: `created` (default), `updated`, `pushed`, or `full_name` (repository name). When not specified, GitHub API defaults to `created`.

inputs.name: type
inputs.desc: Filter for repository type: `all` (owned or member), `owner` (directly owned), `member` (user is a public member), or `public` (public repositories only). When omitted, API returns all public repositories.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.desc: Number of results per page (max 100). See GitHub REST API pagination docs for more info.

inputs.name: username
inputs.desc: GitHub username, e.g., 'octocat'.

inputs.name: direction
inputs.desc: Sort direction: `asc` (ascending) or `desc` (descending). When not specified, defaults to `asc` for `full_name` sort, and `desc` for all other sort options.

name: List repositories for the authenticated user
description: Lists repositories for the authenticated user; using 'type' with 'visibility' or 'affiliation' API parameters (not in this model) can cause a 422 error.

inputs.name: page
inputs.desc: Page number of results to fetch.

inputs.name: sort
inputs.desc: The repository property by which to sort the results.

inputs.name: type
inputs.desc: Filters the results to repositories of the specified type.

inputs.name: since
inputs.desc: Filters for repositories updated at or after this specified time. Provide as an ISO 8601 formatted timestamp.

inputs.name: before
inputs.desc: Filters for repositories updated before this specified time. Provide as an ISO 8601 formatted timestamp.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.desc: The number of results to return per page (maximum 100).

inputs.name: direction
inputs.desc: The direction of the sort. Defaults to `asc` when `sort` is 'full_name', and `desc` otherwise.

name: List repositories starred by a user
description: Lists repositories a valid and existing GitHub user has starred.

inputs.name: page
inputs.desc: Page number of the results to retrieve (starts from 1). For more information, see "[Using pagination in the REST API](https://docs.github.com/rest/using-the-rest-api/using-pagination-in-the-rest-api)."

inputs.name: sort
inputs.desc: The criteria for sorting the starred repositories. `created` sorts by the date the repository was starred. `updated` sorts by the date the repository was last pushed to.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.desc: The number of results to display per page (max 100). For more information, see "[Using pagination in the REST API](https://docs.github.com/rest/using-the-rest-api/using-pagination-in-the-rest-api)."

inputs.name: username
inputs.desc: The GitHub username of the user whose starred repositories are to be listed.

inputs.name: direction
inputs.desc: The direction for sorting the results. Can be `asc` for ascending or `desc` for descending.

name: List repositories starred by the authenticated user
description: Lists repositories the authenticated user has starred, optionally sorted and paginated, including star creation timestamps via 'application/vnd.github.star+json' media type.

inputs.name: page
inputs.desc: Page number of results (starts from 1).

inputs.name: sort
inputs.desc: Sorts starred repositories by 'created' (date starred) or 'updated' (date last pushed).

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.desc: Number of results per page (max 100). Defaults to 30.

inputs.name: direction
inputs.desc: Sort direction: 'asc' (ascending) or 'desc' (descending).

name: List repositories watched by a user
description: Lists repositories a given GitHub user is watching; the username must be a valid and existing GitHub user handle.

inputs.name: page
inputs.desc: Page number of results (starts from 1).

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.desc: Number of results per page (max 100).

inputs.name: username
inputs.desc: The GitHub username (case-sensitive).

name: List repositories watched by the authenticated user
description: Lists repositories the authenticated user is watching (subscribed to for notifications). This action retrieves a list of repositories that the authenticated user is currently watching. It allows the user to manage their watched repositories effectively.

inputs.name: page
inputs.desc: Page number of the results to retrieve.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.desc: Number of results per page (max 100).

name: List repos with ghactions cache usage
description: Retrieves a paginated list of GitHub Actions cache usage statistics for repositories within an existing GitHub organization.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The name of the GitHub organization for which to retrieve repository cache usage. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: page
inputs.desc: The page number for the set of results to fetch.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.desc: The number of repository cache usage results to return per page (maximum is 100).

name: List repository activities
description: Lists activities for a GitHub repository, ensuring the repository exists and is accessible.

inputs.name: ref
inputs.desc: The Git reference for which to list activities (e.g., a branch name like `main` or a full ref like `refs/heads/main`). If unspecified, activities from all branches are listed.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: actor
inputs.desc: The GitHub username of the person who performed the activity. Filters activities to only those performed by this user.

inputs.name: after
inputs.desc: A pagination cursor. Returns activity items that occurred after this cursor. This cursor is typically obtained from the `Link` header in a previous API response. For more details, see GitHub's REST API documentation on "Using pagination in the REST API" and "Using Link headers".

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account owner of the repository (e.g., a GitHub username or organization name). This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: before
inputs.desc: A pagination cursor. Returns activity items that occurred before this cursor. This cursor is typically obtained from the `Link` header in a previous API response. For more details, see GitHub's REST API documentation on "Using pagination in the REST API" and "Using Link headers".

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.desc: The number of activity results to return per page. Defaults to 30, maximum is 100. For more information, see the GitHub REST API guide on "Using pagination in the REST API".

inputs.name: direction
inputs.desc: The direction to sort the activity results. Use 'asc' for ascending (oldest first) or 'desc' for descending (newest first).

inputs.name: time_period
inputs.desc: Filters activities based on a predefined time period. For example, 'day' retrieves activities from the last 24 hours, and 'week' from the last 7 days (168 hours).

inputs.name: activity_type
inputs.desc: Filters activities by a specific type, such as 'push' or 'pr_merge'. If unspecified, all relevant activity types are returned.

name: Get all autolinks of a repository
description: Retrieves all autolinks (e.g., for JIRA issues) configured for a repository; requires admin permissions on the repository.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The repository name, excluding `.git`. Case-insensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account owner's username. Case-insensitive.

name: List repository collaborators
description: Lists collaborators for a specified repository, provided it exists and is accessible to the authenticated user.

inputs.name: page
inputs.desc: The page number of the results to retrieve.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This value is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The username of the account that owns the repository. This value is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.desc: The number of results to return per page. Maximum value is 100.

inputs.name: permission
inputs.desc: Filter collaborators by their repository permission level. If not specified, all collaborators are returned regardless of permission.

inputs.name: affiliation
inputs.desc: Filter collaborators by affiliation: 'outside' (collaborators of an organization-owned repository not part of the org), 'direct' (collaborators with direct permissions to an organization-owned repository), or 'all' (all collaborators visible to the authenticated user).

name: List repository contributors
description: Lists contributors to a specified repository, sorted by number of contributions in descending order; the repository must exist and be accessible.

inputs.name: anon
inputs.desc: Set to '1' or 'true' to include anonymous contributors. If omitted, anonymous contributors are not included.

inputs.name: page
inputs.desc: Page number of results to retrieve (1-based indexing).

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: Name of the repository (without `.git` extension). Not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: Username of the account owning the repository. Not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.desc: Number of contributor results per page. Maximum value is 100.

name: List repository events
description: Lists chronological events (e.g., code pushes, issue activities, PR actions, forks) for a specified, existing GitHub repository.

inputs.name: page
inputs.desc: Page number for paginating results.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: Repository name, without the `.git` extension (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: Owner's username or organization name (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.desc: Number of events to return per page (max 100).

name: List repository invitations
description: Retrieves all pending (unaccepted or undeclined) collaboration invitations for a specified GitHub repository.

inputs.name: page
inputs.desc: Page number of the results to retrieve.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the '.git' extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The username of the account that owns the repository. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.desc: Number of results to return per page (max 100).

name: List repository issues
description: Lists issues (including pull requests) from a GitHub repository. Requires BOTH 'owner' AND 'repo' parameters (e.g., owner='octocat', repo='Hello-World' for github.com/octocat/Hello-World).

inputs.name: page
inputs.desc: Page number for the set of results. Refer to GitHub's REST API documentation for pagination details.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: REQUIRED. The name of the repository (without .git extension). For 'octocat/Hello-World', repo is 'Hello-World'. Not case-sensitive. Must be provided together with 'owner'.

inputs.name: sort
inputs.desc: The field to sort the results by.

inputs.name: type
inputs.desc: Filter issues by type name. Use '*' for issues with any type, 'none' for issues without a type, or specify a type name.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: REQUIRED. The GitHub account owner (user or organization) of the repository. For 'octocat/Hello-World', owner is 'octocat'. Not case-sensitive. Must be provided together with 'repo'.

inputs.name: since
inputs.desc: Filters issues to include only those updated at or after the specified time. This should be a timestamp in ISO 8601 format (e.g., `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`).

inputs.name: state
inputs.desc: Filters issues based on their state.

inputs.name: labels
inputs.desc: A comma-separated list of label names to filter issues by. Example: 'bug,ui,@high'.

inputs.name: creator
inputs.desc: Filter issues by the username of the user who created the issue.

inputs.name: assignee
inputs.desc: Filter issues by assignee's username. Use 'none' for issues with no assignee, or '*' for issues assigned to any user.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.desc: Number of results per page (max 100). Default is 30, which matches GitHub's API default. Refer to GitHub's REST API documentation for pagination details.

inputs.name: direction
inputs.desc: The direction of the sort.

inputs.name: mentioned
inputs.desc: Filter issues by a user mentioned in the issue's description or comments. Provide the username.

inputs.name: milestone
inputs.desc: Filter issues by milestone. Provide the milestone `number` as a string, '*' for issues with any milestone, or 'none' for issues without a milestone.

name: List secret scanning alerts for a repository
description: List secret scanning alerts for a GitHub repository using first-party detections. Use this to retrieve detected secrets with filtering by state, type, resolution, and validity. Supports optional hiding of secret literals for security.

inputs.name: page
inputs.desc: Page number of results to fetch.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository without .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.desc: Sort alerts by 'created' or 'updated' timestamp.

inputs.desc: Cursor for pagination to fetch results after this cursor.

inputs.desc: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.desc: Filter alerts by state: 'open' for unresolved alerts or 'resolved' for resolved alerts.

inputs.desc: Cursor for pagination to fetch results before this cursor.

inputs.desc: Filter by token validity status: 'active' (token still valid), 'inactive' (token revoked/expired), or 'unknown' (validity cannot be determined).

inputs.desc: Hide the literal secret value from API response for security. When true, the 'secret' field will be omitted from results.

Note: This endpoint only returns results when the secret's visibility is set to 'selected'. Secrets with visibility 'all' or 'private' do not have specific repository selections.

inputs.desc: The number of results per page (max 100). For more information, see "[Using pagination in the REST API](https://docs.github.com/rest/using-the-rest-api/using-pagination-in-the-rest-api)."

inputs.name: issue_number
inputs.desc: The number that identifies the issue.

name: List marketplace subscriptions for authenticated user
description: Tool to list GitHub Marketplace subscriptions for the authenticated user. Use when you need to retrieve active marketplace app subscriptions including billing details, plan information, and trial status. Returns subscription information with account, plan, billing cycle, and pricing details.

inputs.name: page
inputs.desc: The page number of the results to fetch. For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API".

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.desc: The number of results per page (max 100). For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API".

name: List stubbed subscriptions for the authenticated user
description: Lists the authenticated user's stubbed (test/example data, not live) GitHub Marketplace subscriptions, useful for development or testing.

inputs.name: page
inputs.desc: Page number of results to fetch (starts from 1).

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.desc: Number of results per page (max 100).

name: List tag protection states for a repository
description: DEPRECATED: Lists tag protection rules for a repository. This API was sunset on August 30, 2024. Use GITHUB_GET_ALL_REPOSITORY_RULESETS instead to view tag protection rules via repository rulesets.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account owner of the repository. This name is not case sensitive.

name: List team members
description: Lists members of a specific team within an organization, including members of child teams.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: Organization name (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: page
inputs.desc: Page number for results.

inputs.name: role
inputs.desc: Filters members by their role in the team.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.desc: Number of results per page (maximum 100).

inputs.name: team_slug
inputs.desc: Team slug (URL-friendly name).

name: List team projects
description: Lists GitHub projects accessible to a specific team within an organization.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: Name of the GitHub organization (case-insensitive). E.g., 'MyOrg' for github.com/MyOrg.

inputs.name: page
inputs.desc: Page number for pagination.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.desc: Number of project results per page (max 100).

inputs.name: team_slug
inputs.desc: The team's URL-friendly slug (e.g., 'web-developers' for 'Web Developers').

name: List team repositories
description: Lists repositories accessible to a specific team within a GitHub organization. Returns a list of repositories that the specified team has access to, including the team's permission level (admin, maintain, push, triage, pull) for each repository. Requires read:org scope and membership in the organization to access team data.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The name of the GitHub organization (case-insensitive). Example: 'octo-org'.

inputs.name: page
inputs.desc: Page number of results to fetch. Default: 1.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.desc: Number of results per page (1-100). Default: 30.

inputs.name: team_slug
inputs.desc: The URL-friendly slug of the team name (lowercase with dashes). Example: 'justice-league'.

name: List teams
description: Lists teams for a specified GitHub organization.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The name of the GitHub organization. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: page
inputs.desc: The page number of the results to fetch when paginating through the list of teams.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.desc: The number of team results to return per page. Maximum value is 100.

name: List teams for the authenticated user
description: Lists all teams across all organizations to which the authenticated user belongs, supporting pagination.

inputs.name: page
inputs.desc: Page number of the results to retrieve.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.desc: Number of team results to return per page (max 100). For more information, refer to GitHub's documentation on API pagination.

name: List teams assigned to an organization role
description: Lists teams assigned to a specific role within a GitHub organization. This endpoint requires organization administrator privileges. Use this to audit which teams have been granted specific organization-level permissions through roles. Note: Organization roles is a feature for enterprise-level GitHub organizations.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: Name of the GitHub organization (case-insensitive). You must be an administrator of this organization.

inputs.name: page
inputs.desc: Page number of results to fetch (starts at 1).

inputs.name: role_id
inputs.desc: Unique numerical identifier of the organization role. Use 'Get all organization roles' to find valid role IDs.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.desc: Number of results per page (1-100). Default is 30.

name: List the people a user follows
description: Lists GitHub users that a valid GitHub `username` is following, supporting pagination.

inputs.name: page
inputs.desc: Page number for the results to retrieve (starts from 1).

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.desc: Number of results to return per page (maximum 100).

inputs.name: username
inputs.desc: GitHub username (case-sensitive) of the user.

name: List people the authenticated user follows
description: Lists people the authenticated user follows.

inputs.name: page
inputs.desc: Page number for paginated results.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.desc: Number of results per page (max 100).

name: List timeline events for an issue
description: Lists chronological events (e.g., comments, commits, label changes) for a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

inputs.name: page
inputs.desc: Page number for results.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: Name of the repository, without the `.git` extension (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: Account owner of the repository (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.desc: Number of events per page (max 100).

inputs.name: issue_number
inputs.desc: Number identifying the issue within the repository.

name: List token access repositories
description: Lists repositories in an organization that a fine-grained personal access token has access to. Requires organization admin permissions or 'Personal access tokens' organization read permission. Only GitHub Apps can use this endpoint.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The organization name (case-insensitive). You must be an organization owner or have the 'Personal access tokens' organization permission to use this endpoint.

inputs.name: page
inputs.desc: Page number of the results to fetch. Default: 1.

inputs.name: pat_id
inputs.desc: The unique numeric identifier of the fine-grained personal access token. You can obtain this ID from the 'list_org_resource_access_tokens' action.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.desc: Number of results per page (max 100). Default: 30.

name: List user projects
description: Retrieves a list of projects for a specified GitHub user, optionally filtering by state and supporting pagination; the username must be a valid GitHub handle. Note: This action uses GitHub's Projects V2 GraphQL API. The legacy Projects V1 REST API has been sunset by GitHub.

inputs.name: page
inputs.desc: Page number for pagination.

inputs.name: state
inputs.desc: Filter projects by state.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.desc: Number of results per page (max 100).

inputs.name: username
inputs.desc: GitHub username (handle).

name: List users
description: Retrieves all GitHub users (individuals and organizations) in chronological order of their sign-up date.

inputs.name: since
inputs.desc: A user ID. If provided, only users with an ID greater than this ID will be returned. This parameter facilitates pagination. If omitted, the list starts from the first user.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.desc: Specifies the number of user results to return per page. The maximum allowed value is 100.

name: List users blocked by an organization
description: Lists users blocked by a specified GitHub organization.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The organization name. This name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: page
inputs.desc: Specifies the page number of the results to fetch. Default is 1. For more information, see "[Using pagination in the REST API](https://docs.github.com/rest/using-the-rest-api/using-pagination-in-the-rest-api)."

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.desc: Specifies the number of results to return per page, with a maximum of 100. Default is 30. For more information, see "[Using pagination in the REST API](https://docs.github.com/rest/using-the-rest-api/using-pagination-in-the-rest-api)."

name: List users blocked by the authenticated user
description: Lists users blocked by the authenticated user, returning an empty list if no users are blocked.

inputs.name: page
inputs.desc: The page number of the results to retrieve.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.desc: The number of results per page (max 100).

name: List users assigned org role
description: Lists users assigned to a specific role within a GitHub organization.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The name of the GitHub organization. This is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: page
inputs.desc: Page number of results (1-indexed).

inputs.name: role_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier for the organization role. Use the 'Get all organization roles' action first to retrieve valid role IDs.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.desc: Number of results per page (maximum 100).

name: List watchers
description: Retrieves a list of users watching a specific repository; the repository must be accessible to the authenticated user.

inputs.name: page
inputs.desc: Page number of the results to retrieve.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The username of the account that owns the repository. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.desc: Number of results to return per page (max 100).

name: List workflow run artifacts
description: Lists artifacts (e.g., build outputs, test results) for a specific workflow run in a GitHub repository.

inputs.name: name
inputs.desc: If specified, filters artifacts by this exact name.

inputs.name: page
inputs.desc: Page number of results to fetch.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: Repository name, without the `.git` extension (not case sensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: Account owner of the repository (not case sensitive).

inputs.name: run_id
inputs.desc: Unique identifier of the workflow run.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.desc: Number of results per page (max 100).

name: List workflow runs for a repository
description: Lists workflow runs for a repository, allowing filtering by actor, branch, event, status, creation date, check suite ID, or head SHA; the repository must exist and be accessible.

inputs.name: page
inputs.desc: Page number of the results to fetch.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: actor
inputs.desc: Filter by the actor's login (e.g., the user who triggered the `push`).

inputs.name: event
inputs.desc: Filter by the event that triggered the workflow (e.g., `push`, `pull_request`).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account owner of the repository (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: branch
inputs.desc: Filter by branch name (e.g., `main` or the branch that triggered the `push`).

inputs.name: status
inputs.desc: Filter by `status` or `conclusion` (e.g., `success`, `in_progress`). Note: Only GitHub Actions can set `waiting`, `pending`, or `requested`.

inputs.name: created
inputs.desc: Filter by creation date or date-time range (ISO8601 format: YYYY-MM-DD or YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ). Supports operators like `>`, `<`, `>=`, `<=`, and `..` for ranges.

inputs.name: head_sha
inputs.desc: Filter to include only runs associated with the specified `head_sha` (commit SHA at the head of the branch).

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.desc: Number of results per page (maximum 100).

inputs.name: check_suite_id
inputs.desc: Filter by a specific `check_suite_id`.

inputs.name: exclude_pull_requests
inputs.desc: Set to `true` to exclude workflow runs triggered by pull request events. Omit or leave as null to include all runs.

name: List workflow runs for a workflow
description: Lists runs for a specified, existing workflow (identified by ID or filename like `main.yml`) in a GitHub repository, with filtering options.

inputs.name: page
inputs.desc: Page number of the results to retrieve.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: Repository name, without the `.git` extension (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: actor
inputs.desc: Filter by the GitHub username of the actor who initiated the run.

inputs.name: event
inputs.desc: Filter by the event that triggered the run (e.g., `push`, `pull_request`). For more event types, see GitHub's documentation on "Events that trigger workflows".

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: Account owner of the repository (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: branch
inputs.desc: Filter by branch name, typically for `push` events.

inputs.name: status
inputs.desc: Filter by status or conclusion (e.g., `success`, `in_progress`). Note: `waiting`, `pending`, or `requested` statuses are set exclusively by GitHub Actions.

inputs.name: created
inputs.desc: Filter by creation date/time range. For syntax, refer to GitHub's "Understanding the search syntax" guide for dates.

inputs.name: head_sha
inputs.desc: Filter by the head commit's SHA.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.desc: Number of results per page (max 100).

inputs.name: workflow_id
inputs.desc: The ID of the workflow (integer) or the workflow file name (string, e.g., `main.yml` or `ci.yaml`).

inputs.name: check_suite_id
inputs.desc: Filter by `check_suite_id` (identifier for a collection of check runs).

inputs.name: exclude_pull_requests
inputs.desc: If true, exclude workflow runs triggered by pull requests.

name: Lock an issue
description: Locks an existing GitHub issue's conversation, preventing further comments; an optional reason can be specified.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The username of the account that owns the repository. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: lock_reason
inputs.desc: Optional reason for locking the conversation. Valid values: 'off-topic', 'too heated', 'resolved', 'spam'. If omitted, no reason is displayed.

inputs.name: issue_number
inputs.desc: The number that uniquely identifies the issue to be locked within the repository.

name: Lookup Enterprise Administrator Invitation
description: Tool to look up a pending enterprise administrator invitation by invitee, enterprise, and role. Use when you need to check if a specific user has a pending invitation to become an enterprise administrator.

inputs.name: role
inputs.desc: The role for the enterprise administrator invitation. Must be one of: OWNER, BILLING_MANAGER, or UNAFFILIATED.

inputs.name: user_login
inputs.desc: The login (username) of the user invited to join the enterprise as an administrator.

inputs.name: enterprise_slug
inputs.desc: The slug of the enterprise the user was invited to join. This is the URL-friendly identifier for the enterprise.

name: Lookup GitHub Resource
description: Tool to lookup a GitHub resource by its URL. Use when you need to identify or retrieve information about a GitHub resource from its URL.

inputs.name: url
inputs.desc: The GitHub URL of the resource to lookup. Can be a repository, issue, pull request, or other GitHub resource URL.

name: Lookup nodes by IDs
description: Tool to lookup GitHub nodes by their global node IDs. Use when you need to retrieve multiple objects by their IDs in a single batch query.

inputs.name: ids
inputs.desc: A list of GitHub global node IDs to lookup. Each ID must be a valid base64-encoded global node ID (e.g., 'MDQ6VXNlcjU4MzIzMQ==').

name: Lookup Organization (GraphQL)
description: Tool to lookup a GitHub organization by login using GraphQL. Use when you need organization information via GraphQL API.

inputs.name: login
inputs.desc: The organization's login username to look up. Case-sensitive.

name: Look Up Topic by Name
description: Tool to look up a GitHub topic by name using GraphQL. Use when you need to find information about a specific GitHub topic including its popularity (stargazer count) and related topics.

inputs.name: name
inputs.desc: The topic's name to look up (e.g., 'python', 'javascript', 'machine-learning').

inputs.name: related_topics_count
inputs.desc: The number of related topics to fetch (between 1 and 10). Only used if include_related_topics is true.

inputs.name: include_related_topics
inputs.desc: Whether to include related topics in the response. If true, up to 3 related topics will be returned.

name: Manage access control for organization codespaces
description: Sets the Codespaces access control policy for a GitHub organization, determining which members can use them. This endpoint requires admin permissions on the organization. The API returns HTTP 204 No Content on success.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The name of the GitHub organization (case-insensitive). The authenticated user must have admin permissions on this organization.

inputs.name: visibility
inputs.desc:
Defines which users can access Codespaces within the organization.
`disabled`: Codespaces are disabled for all users in the organization.
`selected_members`: Only users specified in `selected_usernames` can access Codespaces.
`all_members`: All members of the organization can access Codespaces.
`all_members_and_outside_collaborators`: All members and outside collaborators can access Codespaces.

inputs.name: selected_usernames
inputs.desc: A list of GitHub usernames who should have access to Codespaces. Required when `visibility` is set to `selected_members`. Providing a list here will replace any existing list of selected usernames. The specified users must be members or outside collaborators of the organization.

name: Manage custom properties for org repos
description: Creates or updates values for an organization's predefined custom properties across multiple repositories (up to 30).

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The organization's name (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: properties
inputs.desc: JSON string containing an array of custom properties to set. Each must have `property_name` (predefined for the organization) and `value`. Example: '[{"property_name": "project-status", "value": "active"}]'

inputs.name: repository_names
inputs.desc: Repository names (max 30) to apply the custom property values to.

name: List selected repositories for a GitHub Actions organization secret
description: Lists repositories within an organization that have been explicitly granted access to a specific GitHub Actions organization secret. Returns all repositories that have been selected when the visibility for repository access to an Actions secret is set to 'selected'. Requires admin:org scope and admin access to the organization. Note: This endpoint only returns results when the secret's visibility is set to 'selected'. Secrets with visibility 'all' or 'private' do not have specific repository selections.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The name of the GitHub organization. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: page
inputs.desc: Page number of the results to fetch. Default is 1. See '[Using pagination in the REST API](https://docs.github.com/rest/using-the-rest-api/using-pagination-in-the-rest-api)' for more details.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.desc: Number of results to return per page (maximum 100). Default is 30. See '[Using pagination in the REST API](https://docs.github.com/rest/using-the-rest-api/using-pagination-in-the-rest-api)' for more details.

inputs.name: secret_name
inputs.desc: The name of the organization secret for which to list accessible repositories.

name: Map a commit author
description: Updates Git author information (name and/or email) for an `author_id` obtained during a repository import, to correctly attribute commits. NOTE: This endpoint has been deprecated by GitHub as of April 12, 2024, and is no longer available. The Source Imports REST API has been retired due to very low usage levels. Please use the GitHub Importer tool at https://github.com/new/import for repository imports.

inputs.name: name
inputs.desc: New Git author name for the `author_id`. At least one of `email` or `name` must be provided.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: Name of the repository, without the `.git` extension (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: email
inputs.desc: New Git author email for the `author_id`. At least one of `email` or `name` must be provided.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: Username of the account owning the repository (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: author_id
inputs.desc: Numeric ID of the author to map, identified during a repository import; this ID is specific to the import process.

name: Mark a thread as done
description: Marks the GitHub notification thread (identified by `thread_id`) as done or read for the authenticated user, effectively archiving it.

inputs.name: thread_id
inputs.desc: ID of the notification thread to mark as done. Typically obtained from the `id` field in notification list operations (e.g., `GET /notifications`).

name: Mark a thread as read
description: Marks an existing GitHub notification thread, identified by its `thread_id`, as read.

inputs.name: thread_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the notification thread. This ID is obtained from a notification's `id` field, for example, via the 'List notifications for the authenticated user' endpoint.

name: Mark notifications as read
description: Marks notifications as read or unread, optionally for those updated at or before a `last_read_at` timestamp.

inputs.name: read
inputs.desc: Indicates whether to mark targeted notifications as read (`true`) or unread (`false`). Defaults to `true` (mark as read) if not provided.

inputs.name: last_read_at
inputs.desc: If provided, only notifications updated at or before this timestamp are affected. If omitted, all current notifications are targeted.

name: Mark repository notifications as read
description: Marks notifications in a repository as read; if 'last_read_at' is specified, notifications updated after this timestamp are not marked as read.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: Name of the repository, without the `.git` extension (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: Username of the account owning the repository (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: last_read_at
inputs.desc: Timestamp (ISO 8601 format: `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`). If provided, notifications updated *after* this time will NOT be marked as read. If omitted, all notifications in the repository are marked as read. Defaults to the current time.

name: Merge a branch
description: Merges a head branch or commit SHA into a base branch in a repository; fails if there are merge conflicts requiring manual resolution.

inputs.name: base
inputs.desc: The name of the branch to merge the `head` branch into.

inputs.name: head
inputs.desc: Source branch name or full commit SHA to merge into the `base` branch.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: Repository name, without the `.git` extension. Not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: Username or organization name of the repository owner. Not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: commit_message
inputs.desc: Commit message for the merge. If omitted, GitHub uses a default message.

name: Merge a pull request
description: Merges an open and mergeable pull request in a repository. A 405 error can occur for multiple reasons: (1) The PR is still in draft mode - convert it to ready-for-review first using GITHUB_UPDATE_A_PULL_REQUEST. (2) Branch protection rules are not satisfied - check for required approving reviews, status checks, or other repository rules that must be met before merging. (3) The base branch has been modified since the PR was last synced - update the PR branch first using GITHUB_UPDATE_A_PULL_REQUEST_BRANCH.

inputs.name: sha
inputs.desc: SHA of the pull request's head commit. Must match for merge to succeed, ensuring PR hasn't changed since review.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: Repository name, without the `.git` extension. Not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: Repository owner's username. Not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: pull_number
inputs.desc: The unique number identifying the pull request. The PR must be mergeable: not a draft, and all branch protection rules (required reviews, status checks, etc.) must be satisfied.

inputs.name: commit_title
inputs.desc: Title for the merge commit message.

inputs.name: merge_method
inputs.desc: Merge strategy: 'merge', 'squash', or 'rebase'. Defaults to repository's setting if unspecified.

inputs.name: commit_message
inputs.desc: Additional details for the merge commit message.

name: Move a project card (Classic - Deprecated)
description: Moves a project card to a specified position within a column (GitHub Classic Projects). DEPRECATION NOTICE: GitHub Projects (classic) and its REST API were sunset on April 1, 2025. This action will return 404 for most requests on GitHub.com. It may still work on GitHub Enterprise Server instances where classic projects remain enabled. For new projects, use GitHub Projects V2 which uses the GraphQL API instead. See: https://github.blog/changelog/2024-05-23-sunset-notice-projects-classic/ On success, the API returns HTTP 201 with an empty body, indicating the card was moved.

inputs.name: card_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the project card to be moved. Obtain this ID from the list_project_cards or get_a_project_card actions.

inputs.name: position
inputs.desc: The target position of the card within the column. Must be one of: 'top' (place card at top of column), 'bottom' (place card at bottom of column), or 'after:<card_id>' (place card immediately after the specified card). When using 'after:<card_id>', the referenced card must exist in the target column.

inputs.name: column_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the column to move the card to. If omitted, the card stays in its current column but moves to the specified position.

name: Move a project column (Classic - Deprecated)
description: Moves a column within a GitHub project (classic) to a new position. DEPRECATION NOTICE: GitHub Classic Projects (V1) and its REST API were sunset on April 1, 2025. This action will return a 404 error on GitHub.com. For new projects, use GitHub Projects V2 which uses the GraphQL API instead. The `position` parameter can be: - 'first': Move the column to the beginning of the project - 'last': Move the column to the end of the project - 'after:<column_id>': Place the column after the specified column (e.g., 'after:67890') See: https://github.blog/changelog/2024-05-23-sunset-notice-projects-classic/

inputs.name: position
inputs.desc: The new position for the column within the project. Valid values are: 'first' (move to the beginning), 'last' (move to the end), or 'after:<column_id>' (place after the specified column, e.g., 'after:67890').

inputs.name: column_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the project column to be moved. Must be a valid column ID from a GitHub project (classic).

name: Ping an organization webhook
description: Sends a 'ping' event to a specified, existing organization webhook to test its configuration and ensure it correctly receives events.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The name of the organization. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: hook_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the webhook. This ID can be found in the `X-GitHub-Hook-ID` header of a webhook delivery.

name: Ping a repository webhook
description: Pings an existing webhook on a repository to test its configuration and reachability by GitHub.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository without the `.git` extension. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: hook_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the hook. You can find this value in the `X-GitHub-Hook-ID` header of a webhook delivery.

name: Privately report a security vulnerability
description: Privately reports a security vulnerability to a repository's maintainers. IMPORTANT: You cannot report vulnerabilities to repositories you own - this API is for third-party security researchers to report to other repositories. The target repository must have private vulnerability reporting enabled. Specify EITHER `severity` OR `cvss_vector_string`, but not both.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository without the `.git` extension. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account owner of the repository. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: cwe_ids
inputs.desc: A list of Common Weakness Enumeration (CWE) identifiers relevant to the vulnerability.

inputs.name: summary
inputs.desc: A short, descriptive summary of the security vulnerability.

inputs.name: severity
inputs.desc: The advisory's severity level. Mutually exclusive with `cvss_vector_string`.

inputs.name: description
inputs.desc: A detailed explanation of the security vulnerability, its impact, and potential mitigations.

inputs.name: vulnerabilities
inputs.desc: JSON string representing a list of vulnerability objects detailing the affected packages or products. Each object should specify the package's ecosystem and name, the vulnerable version range, and optionally, patched versions and vulnerable functions.

inputs.name: cvss_vector_string
inputs.desc: The CVSS vector string for advisory severity. Mutually exclusive with `severity`.

inputs.name: start_private_fork
inputs.desc: Whether to create a temporary private fork to collaborate on a fix for the reported vulnerability.

name: Publish Sponsors Tier (GraphQL)
description: Tool to publish a draft GitHub Sponsors tier using GraphQL. Use when you need to make a draft sponsors tier visible to potential sponsors. The tier must already exist in draft status.

inputs.name: tierId
inputs.desc: The global Relay ID of the draft sponsors tier to publish. Must be a tier that is currently in draft status.

inputs.name: clientMutationId
inputs.desc: A unique identifier for the client performing the mutation. Used to track requests.

name: Redeliver a delivery for an organization webhook
description: Redelivers a specific webhook delivery for a webhook within an organization, to resend an event that previously failed or was not processed.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The name of the organization. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: hook_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the webhook. This ID can be found in the `X-GitHub-Hook-ID` header of a webhook delivery or by listing webhooks for an organization.

inputs.name: delivery_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the specific webhook delivery to be redelivered. This ID is typically obtained from the `X-GitHub-Delivery` header of the original delivery attempt or by listing deliveries for a webhook.

name: Redeliver a delivery for a repository webhook
description: Redelivers a specific, previously made webhook delivery (`delivery_id`) for a repository's webhook (`hook_id`).

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The repository name, without the `.git` extension (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account username that owns the repository (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: hook_id
inputs.desc: Unique identifier of the webhook (e.g., from `X-GitHub-Hook-ID` header or by listing repository webhooks).

inputs.name: delivery_id
inputs.desc: Unique identifier of a specific past delivery for the webhook (obtainable by listing deliveries for that webhook).

name: Remove a custom property for an organization
description: Deletes a custom property, specified by `custom_property_name`, from an existing organization (`org`) for which the property is currently defined, preventing its future assignment to repositories.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The name of the organization. This name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: custom_property_name
inputs.desc: The name of the custom property to remove. This name is case sensitive.

name: Remove a label from an issue
description: Removes a label currently applied to a specific issue in a repository.

inputs.name: name
inputs.desc: The name of the label to remove from the issue. Emoji reactions will not be removed and names with spaces or special characters must be URL-encoded.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository without the `.git` extension. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: issue_number
inputs.desc: The number that identifies the issue.

name: Remove all labels from an issue
description: Removes all labels from a specified issue in a GitHub repository; this operation is idempotent.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the '.git' extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The username of the account that owns the repository. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: issue_number
inputs.desc: The unique number that identifies the issue from which all labels will be removed.

name: Remove all organization roles for a team
description: Revokes all organization roles for a team in an organization; this is destructive and succeeds even if the team initially has no roles.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The name of the organization. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: team_slug
inputs.desc: The slug of the team name. Team slugs are typically all lowercase and replace spaces with hyphens.

name: Remove all organization roles for a user
description: Revokes all assigned organization-level roles from a specified user (who must be a member of the organization) within a GitHub organization, without removing the user from the organization or affecting repository-specific roles.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The organization name. This name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: username
inputs.desc: The handle for the GitHub user account.

name: Remove an organization member
description: Removes a member from a GitHub organization. This action permanently removes a user's membership from the specified organization, revoking their access to all organization resources, repositories, and teams. Requirements: - The authenticated user must be an organization owner - The target user must currently be a member of the organization Note: This action cannot be used to remove the last owner of an organization.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The organization name (case-insensitive). Must be an organization where the authenticated user has owner permissions.

inputs.name: username
inputs.desc: The GitHub username of the member to remove. The user must currently be a member of the organization.

name: Remove an organization role from a team
description: Revokes an organization role that a team currently possesses within an organization.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The name of the organization. This value is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: role_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the organization role to be removed from the team.

inputs.name: team_slug
inputs.desc: The slug (short name) of the team.

name: Remove an organization role from a user
description: Removes a custom organization role from a user within a GitHub organization, provided the organization exists, the user is a member, and the role_id corresponds to a valid custom role in that organization.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The GitHub organization's name (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: role_id
inputs.desc: Unique ID of the organization role to be removed. Use the 'Get all organization roles' action to retrieve valid role IDs.

inputs.name: username
inputs.desc: GitHub username of the user.

name: Remove app access restrictions
description: Removes all GitHub App access restrictions from a protected branch in a repository; the branch must have protection rules configured, and this action does not alter user or team restrictions.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The username of the account that owns the repository. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: branch
inputs.desc: The name of the branch from which all app access restrictions will be removed. Wildcard characters are not supported; for branches with wildcards, please use the GraphQL API.

name: Remove a project from a team
description: Removes a project from a team within an organization; this action requires the project to be currently associated with the team.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: team_slug
inputs.desc: The slug of the team name (URL-friendly version, e.g., 'justice-league').

inputs.name: project_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the project to be removed from the team.

name: Remove a repository collaborator
description: Removes a collaborator from a specified GitHub repository, provided the repository exists and the user is an existing collaborator.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account owner of the repository (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: username
inputs.desc: The GitHub username of the collaborator to remove.

name: Remove a repository from an app installation
description: Removes a repository from a GitHub App installation for the authenticated user, given a valid `installation_id` and the `repository_id` of a repository currently linked to that installation; this operation is idempotent.

inputs.name: repository_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the repository to be removed from the installation.

inputs.name: installation_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the GitHub App installation from which to remove the repository.

name: Remove a repository from a team
description: Disassociates a repository from a team; team members may lose access permissions, but the repository and team are not deleted.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The name of the organization where the team and repository exist. This field is case-insensitive.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the '.git' extension. This field is case-insensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The username of the account that owns the repository. This field is case-insensitive.

inputs.name: team_slug
inputs.desc: The URL-friendly identifier (slug) for the team. Team slugs are typically all lowercase with hyphens instead of spaces (e.g., 'justice-league' for 'Justice League').

name: Remove a selected repository from a user secret
description: Removes a selected repository's access to a user's Codespaces secret; the secret must exist and the repository must have previously been granted access.

inputs.name: secret_name
inputs.desc: Name of the user's Codespaces secret from which repository access will be revoked.

inputs.name: repository_id
inputs.desc: The unique integer identifier of the repository that will lose access to the specified Codespaces secret.

name: Remove assignees from an issue
description: Removes specified assignees from a GitHub issue; requires push access, and invalid removal attempts are silently ignored.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, excluding the `.git` extension. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account owner of the repository (username or organization name). This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: assignees
inputs.desc: Usernames to unassign from the issue. Note: Requires push access to the repository; attempts to remove users without permission or those not currently assigned are silently ignored.

inputs.name: issue_number
inputs.desc: The unique number that identifies the issue within the repository.

name: Remove custom label from repo runner
description: Removes a custom label from a repository's self-hosted runner; this operation is idempotent.

inputs.name: name
inputs.desc: Name of the custom label to remove from the self-hosted runner. Note: only custom labels can be removed; read-only system labels (like 'self-hosted', 'linux', 'x64') cannot be removed.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: Repository name, without the `.git` extension (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: Account owner of the repository (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: runner_id
inputs.desc: Unique identifier of the self-hosted runner. Get this from listing runners for the repository.

name: Remove custom label from self hosted runner
description: Removes a currently assigned custom label (`name`) from a self-hosted runner (`runner_id`) in an organization (`org`). This action allows the organization to manage the labels associated with their runners effectively.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The name of the organization. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: name
inputs.desc: The name of the custom label to remove from the self-hosted runner.

inputs.name: runner_id
inputs.desc: Unique identifier of the self-hosted runner.

name: Remove custom labels from self hosted repo runner
description: Removes all custom labels from a self-hosted runner registered at the repository level, leaving only its default labels and any labels inherited from its runner group or organization.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account owner of the repository. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: runner_id
inputs.desc: Unique identifier (ID) of the self-hosted runner.

name: Remove interaction restrictions for an organization
description: Removes all interaction restrictions from public repositories in the specified GitHub organization, allowing all users to resume interactions.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The name of the GitHub organization. This name is not case-sensitive.

name: Remove interaction restrictions for a repository
description: Removes all interaction restrictions for a repository, enabling all users to comment, open issues, and create pull requests by lifting any existing temporary interaction limits.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: Repository name, without the `.git` extension; case-insensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: Username or organization name of the repository owner; case-insensitive.

name: Remove org dev env secret by name
description: Deletes a GitHub Codespaces secret from an organization by its name; this operation is idempotent.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: Name of the GitHub organization (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: secret_name
inputs.desc: Name of the Codespaces secret to remove.

name: Remove org secret by name
description: Permanently removes a specific Dependabot secret, by its `secret_name`, from the GitHub `org`, making it unavailable to Dependabot for that organization.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: GitHub organization name (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: secret_name
inputs.desc: Dependabot secret name to remove from the organization.

name: Remove outside collaborator from an organization
description: Removes a user, who must be an outside collaborator, from the specified GitHub organization, revoking their access to all its repositories.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The name of the GitHub organization. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: username
inputs.desc: The GitHub username of the outside collaborator to remove.

name: Remove public org membership
description: Removes the authenticated user's public organization membership, making it private. The user remains a member of the organization but their membership is no longer publicly visible. This operation is idempotent - calling it when the membership is already private will succeed.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: Name of the GitHub organization (case-insensitive). The authenticated user must be a member of this organization.

inputs.name: username
inputs.desc: GitHub username of the authenticated user. Must match the currently authenticated user - you can only modify your own public membership status.

name: Remove user public repo interaction restrictions
description: Removes all interaction restrictions (limitations on comments, issues, or pull requests) from all public repositories owned by the authenticated user.

name: Remove repository from organization dev environment secret
description: Removes a repository's access to an organization-level Codespaces secret, if it was previously granted.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The GitHub organization name (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: secret_name
inputs.desc: The Codespaces secret name.

inputs.name: repository_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the repository.

name: Remove selected repo from org secret
description: Revokes a specific repository's access to an organization-level Dependabot secret, applicable only when the secret has 'selected' visibility and the specified repository currently has access.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: Name of the organization (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: secret_name
inputs.desc: Name of the Dependabot secret.

inputs.name: repository_id
inputs.desc: Unique numeric ID of the repository (not the repository name).

name: Remove requested reviewers from a pull request
