inputs.desc: The new state for the code scanning alert. Must be either 'open' or 'dismissed'. If 'dismissed', `dismissed_reason` is mandatory.

inputs.name: alert_number
inputs.desc: The number that identifies an alert. Found in the alert's URL or the `number` field of the GET alerts API response.

inputs.name: dismissed_reason
inputs.desc: The reason for dismissing or closing the alert. **Required if `state` is 'dismissed'.**

inputs.name: dismissed_comment
inputs.desc: An optional comment to provide further context when dismissing an alert. Maximum 280 characters.

name: Update a commit comment
description: Changes the body of an existing commit comment.

inputs.name: body
inputs.desc: The new content of the comment.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository without the `.git` extension. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: comment_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the comment.

name: Update a custom organization role
description: Updates an existing custom role in an organization by modifying its name, description, or permissions; at least one of these fields must be provided.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The organization name (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: name
inputs.desc: New name for the custom role. Unchanged if omitted.

inputs.name: role_id
inputs.desc: Unique identifier of the custom organization role.

inputs.name: base_role
inputs.desc: The system role from which this role inherits permissions. One of: 'none', 'read', 'triage', 'write', 'maintain', or 'admin'. Unchanged if omitted.

inputs.name: description
inputs.desc: New short description for the role. Unchanged if omitted.

inputs.name: permissions
inputs.desc: New list of GitHub organization permissions for the role, replacing existing ones. An empty list removes all permissions. Unchanged if omitted.

name: Update a deployment branch policy
description: Updates the name pattern of an existing deployment branch policy for a specific environment in a repository.

inputs.name: name
inputs.desc: The new name pattern that branches must match to deploy to this environment. Wildcard characters (`*`) can be used but will not match `/`. For example, to match branches that begin with `release/` and contain an additional single slash, use `release/*/*`. For more information about pattern matching syntax, refer to the Ruby File.fnmatch documentation (https://ruby-doc.org/core-2.5.1/File.html#method-c-fnmatch).

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account owner of the repository. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: branch_policy_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the deployment branch policy to be updated.

inputs.name: environment_name
inputs.desc: The name of the deployment environment. This name must be URL-encoded if it contains special characters such as slashes (e.g., replace `/` with `%2F`).

name: Update a discussion
description: Updates the title and/or body of a specific team discussion within an organization.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The organization name (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: body
inputs.desc: The new body text for the discussion post. Supports markdown formatting. Only provide this if you want to change the body content.

inputs.name: title
inputs.desc: The new title for the discussion post. Only provide this if you want to change the title.

inputs.name: team_slug
inputs.desc: The slug of the team name.

inputs.name: discussion_number
inputs.desc: The unique number that identifies the discussion within the team.

name: Update a discussion comment
description: Updates the body of a comment in a team's discussion within an organization.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The name of the organization. This is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: body
inputs.desc: The new body text for the discussion comment. Supports GitHub Flavored Markdown.

inputs.name: team_slug
inputs.desc: The slug of the team name. This is the team name converted to lowercase with spaces replaced by hyphens (e.g., 'justice-league').

inputs.name: comment_number
inputs.desc: The unique number identifying the comment within the discussion.

inputs.name: discussion_number
inputs.desc: The unique number identifying the discussion.

name: Update a gist
description: Updates a gist's description, and/or its files (including content, filename changes, or deletion).

inputs.name: files
inputs.desc: Specifies changes to gist files as a JSON string. Keys are current filenames (e.g., `hello.py`). For each file: update `content`, assign a new `filename` to rename (or both). To delete a file, set its value to `null` or an object without `content` or `filename`. Files not mentioned are preserved.

inputs.name: gist_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the gist to be updated.

inputs.name: description
inputs.desc: A new description for the gist.

name: Update a gist comment
description: Updates an existing comment on a specified gist.

inputs.name: body
inputs.desc: The new text content for the comment. Supports Markdown formatting.

inputs.name: gist_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the gist. This is typically a 32-character hexadecimal string found in the gist URL.

inputs.name: comment_id
inputs.desc: The unique numeric identifier of the comment to update.

name: Update a label
description: Updates an existing label's name, color, or description within a specified repository.

inputs.name: name
inputs.desc: Current name of the label to be updated; value is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: Name of the repository, without the .git extension; value is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: color
inputs.desc: Hexadecimal color code for the label, without the leading '#' (e.g., 'f29513').

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: Account owner of the repository; value is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: new_name
inputs.desc: New name for the label (not case-sensitive). Emoji can be added using native characters or colon-style markup (e.g., ':strawberry:'). Refer to GitHub's emoji cheat sheet for a full list of codes.

inputs.name: description
inputs.desc: Short description for the label (max 100 characters).

name: Update a milestone
description: Updates a milestone in a repository, identified by `owner`, `repo`, and `milestone_number`, by allowing modification of its `title`, `state`, `description`, or `due_on`; at least one of these four attributes must be provided to perform an update.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The username of the account that owns the repository. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: state
inputs.desc: The new state of the milestone. If not provided, the state will remain unchanged. Defaults to 'open'.

inputs.name: title
inputs.desc: The new title for the milestone. If not provided, the title will remain unchanged.

inputs.name: due_on
inputs.desc: The milestone due date in ISO 8601 format: `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`. If not provided, the due date will remain unchanged. To remove the due date, pass an empty string or `null`.

inputs.name: description
inputs.desc: A new description for the milestone. If not provided, the description will remain unchanged.

inputs.name: milestone_number
inputs.desc: The unique number that identifies the milestone.

name: Update an environment variable
description: Updates an existing environment variable's name and/or value in a specific GitHub repository environment; requires providing either a new name or a new value.

inputs.name: name
inputs.desc: Current name of the environment variable to update.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository without the `.git` extension. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: value
inputs.desc: New value for the environment variable; if omitted, the value is not changed.

inputs.name: new_name
inputs.desc: New name for the environment variable. If provided, the variable will be renamed to this value. If omitted, the name remains unchanged.

inputs.name: environment_name
inputs.desc: The name of the environment. The name must be URL encoded (e.g., replace slashes `/` with `%2F`).

name: Update an issue
description: Updates an existing GitHub issue's title, body, state, milestone, labels, or assignees; `state_reason` is only processed if `state` also changes, and use `null` or `[]` to clear applicable fields.

inputs.name: body
inputs.desc: New issue body; send `null` to clear, omit to leave unchanged.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: Repository name, without the `.git` extension (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: Owner of the repository (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: state
inputs.desc: New issue state; omit to leave unchanged.

inputs.name: title
inputs.desc: New issue title; send `null` to clear, omit to leave unchanged.

inputs.name: labels
inputs.desc: Replaces all existing labels. Send `[]` to remove all; omit to leave unchanged. Requires push access for changes.

inputs.name: assignee
inputs.desc: DEPRECATED: Username to assign; use `assignees`. Send `null` to unassign, omit to leave unchanged.

inputs.name: assignees
inputs.desc: Replaces all existing assignees. Send `[]` to remove all; omit to leave unchanged. Requires push access for changes.

inputs.name: milestone
inputs.desc: Milestone ID (as string) or title. Send `null` to clear; omit to leave unchanged. Numeric ID recommended.

inputs.name: issue_number
inputs.desc: Unique number identifying the issue.

inputs.name: state_reason
inputs.desc: Reason for state change, considered only if `state` also changes. Valid reasons depend on the new `state`.

name: Update an issue comment
description: Updates an existing comment on an issue or pull request within a specified repository. You can only update comments that the authenticated user has permission to edit. The comment_id can be obtained from the list_issue_comments or get_an_issue_comment actions.

inputs.name: body
inputs.desc: New content for the issue comment (GitHub Flavored Markdown supported).

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: Name of the repository, without the `.git` extension (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: Username of the account owning the repository (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: comment_id
inputs.desc: Unique ID of the issue comment to update.

name: Update an organization
description: Updates an organization's settings; changing security-related fields requires admin, owner, or security manager permissions.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The organization's unique, case-insensitive identifier.

inputs.name: blog
inputs.desc: URL of the organization's blog.

inputs.name: name
inputs.desc: Display name.

inputs.name: email
inputs.desc: Publicly visible email address.

inputs.name: company
inputs.desc: Company name.

inputs.name: location
inputs.desc: Geographical location.

inputs.name: description
inputs.desc: Short description.

inputs.name: billing_email
inputs.desc: Billing email address (not publicly visible).

inputs.name: twitter_username
inputs.desc: Twitter username.

inputs.name: has_repository_projects
inputs.desc: Whether repository-level projects can be used within the organization.

inputs.name: members_can_create_pages
inputs.desc: Whether members can create GitHub Pages sites. Does not affect existing sites.

inputs.name: has_organization_projects
inputs.desc: Whether organization-level projects can be used.

inputs.name: web_commit_signoff_required
inputs.desc: Whether web interface commits require sign-off.

inputs.name: default_repository_permission
inputs.desc: Default permission for members on organization repositories.

inputs.name: members_can_create_public_pages
inputs.desc: Whether members can create public GitHub Pages sites. Does not affect existing sites.

inputs.name: members_can_create_repositories
inputs.desc: Whether non-admin members can create repositories. Overridden by `members_allowed_repository_creation_type`.

inputs.name: members_can_create_private_pages
inputs.desc: Whether members can create private GitHub Pages sites. Does not affect existing sites.

inputs.name: members_can_fork_private_repositories
inputs.desc: Whether members can fork private repositories of the organization.

inputs.name: members_can_create_public_repositories
inputs.desc: Whether members can create public repositories.

inputs.name: members_can_create_private_repositories
inputs.desc: Whether members can create private repositories.

inputs.name: members_allowed_repository_creation_type
inputs.desc: Types of repositories non-admin members can create. `private` option only on GitHub Enterprise Cloud. Deprecated; overrides `members_can_create_repositories`.

inputs.name: members_can_create_internal_repositories
inputs.desc: Whether members can create internal repositories. Only for organizations in an enterprise account.

inputs.name: secret_scanning_push_protection_custom_link
inputs.desc: URL displayed if `secret_scanning_push_protection_custom_link_enabled` is true and a push is blocked.

inputs.name: secret_scanning_enabled_for_new_repositories
inputs.desc: Automatically enable secret scanning for new repositories.

inputs.name: dependency_graph_enabled_for_new_repositories
inputs.desc: Automatically enable dependency graph for new repositories.

inputs.name: advanced_security_enabled_for_new_repositories
inputs.desc: Automatically enable GitHub Advanced Security for new repositories.

inputs.name: dependabot_alerts_enabled_for_new_repositories
inputs.desc: Automatically enable Dependabot alerts for new repositories.

inputs.name: secret_scanning_push_protection_custom_link_enabled
inputs.desc: Show custom link to contributors blocked by secret scanning push protection.

inputs.name: dependabot_security_updates_enabled_for_new_repositories
inputs.desc: Automatically enable Dependabot security updates for new repositories.

inputs.name: secret_scanning_push_protection_enabled_for_new_repositories
inputs.desc: Automatically enable secret scanning push protection for new repositories.

name: Update an organization variable
description: Updates an existing GitHub Actions organization variable's name, value, or visibility (`all`, `private`, `selected`), requiring `selected_repository_ids` with valid repository IDs if visibility is `selected`.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: name
inputs.desc: The current name of the organization variable to be updated. This name is case-sensitive.

inputs.name: value
inputs.desc: Optional: The new value for the organization variable. If provided, the variable's value will be updated. If omitted or null, the current value is preserved.

inputs.name: new_name
inputs.desc: Optional: The new name for the organization variable. If provided, the variable will be renamed. If omitted or null, the current name is preserved.

inputs.name: visibility
inputs.desc: Optional: Specifies the new visibility of the organization variable. Determines which repositories can access this variable: 'all' (all repositories), 'private' (only private repositories), or 'selected' (specific repositories). When set to 'selected', you must also provide `selected_repository_ids`. If omitted or null, visibility remains unchanged.

inputs.name: selected_repository_ids
inputs.desc: Optional: A list of repository IDs (integers) that can access this organization variable. Required when `visibility` is set to 'selected'. Ignored for other visibility values. If omitted when visibility='selected', the API may reject the request or use existing repository access list.

name: Update an organization webhook
description: Updates the configuration (URL, content type, secret, SSL verification), subscribed events, active status, or name of an existing webhook for a specified organization.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The name of the organization. This value is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: name
inputs.desc: Must be passed as 'web' for organization webhooks. This is a fixed value required by the GitHub API.

inputs.name: active
inputs.desc: Set to `true` to send notifications, or `false` to disable them.

inputs.name: events
inputs.desc: List of events that trigger this webhook. GitHub REPLACES the full event list when this is sent — omit (leave unset) to preserve the existing event subscription. Use `['*']` to subscribe to all events. See GitHub docs for available event types.

inputs.name: hook_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the webhook. This ID can be found in the `X-GitHub-Hook-ID` header of a webhook delivery.

inputs.name: config__url
inputs.desc: Target URL for webhook payloads. Required if updating other `config` properties (e.g., `content_type`, `secret`), as GitHub mandates `url` when `config` is modified.

inputs.name: config__secret
inputs.desc: Secret key for HMAC digest to ensure payload integrity. Refer to GitHub documentation on delivery headers for details.

inputs.name: config__content__type
inputs.desc: Media type for serializing payloads (`json` or `form`). The API defaults to `form` if this field is not provided.

inputs.name: config__insecure__ssl
inputs.desc: Controls SSL certificate verification for payload delivery. Set to '1' to skip verification (insecure), or '0' to enforce (API default). Use with caution.

name: Update a project
description: Updates an existing GitHub project (V2) attributes like title, description, visibility, and state. Note: This action uses GitHub's Projects V2 GraphQL API. The legacy Projects (classic) REST API was sunset on April 1, 2025. The project is identified by its number (visible in the project URL) and owner (either a username or organization name). If no owner is specified, the authenticated user is assumed. At least one field to update must be provided.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The organization that owns the project. If provided, this takes precedence over username.

inputs.name: title
inputs.desc: The new title for the project. If not provided, the title will not be changed.

inputs.name: closed
inputs.desc: Sets whether the project is closed. `True` closes the project, `False` reopens it. If not provided, the state will not be changed.

inputs.name: public
inputs.desc: Sets the visibility of the project. `True` makes it public (visible to everyone), `False` makes it private. If not provided, the visibility will not be changed.

inputs.name: readme
inputs.desc: The project's README content (supports Markdown formatting). If not provided, the README will not be changed.

inputs.name: username
inputs.desc: The GitHub username who owns the project. If not provided, defaults to the authenticated user.

inputs.name: project_number
inputs.desc: The project's sequential number (e.g., 1, 2, 3). This is displayed in the project URL as https://github.com/users/{username}/projects/{project_number}.

inputs.name: short_description
inputs.desc: A short description for the project (displayed in project cards and listings). If not provided, the description will not be changed.

name: Update a pull request
description: Updates an existing pull request, allowing changes to attributes like title, body, state, base branch, and maintainer modification settings.

inputs.name: base
inputs.desc: The name of the branch to which you want your changes pulled. This must be an existing branch in the current repository. Cannot update the base branch to point to another repository. If None, the base branch remains unchanged.

inputs.name: body
inputs.desc: The new contents of the pull request. To remove the body, pass an empty string. If None, the body remains unchanged.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account owner of the repository. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: state
inputs.desc: The new state of the pull request. Can be 'open' or 'closed'. If None, the state remains unchanged.

inputs.name: title
inputs.desc: The new title for the pull request. If None, the title remains unchanged.

inputs.name: pull_number
inputs.desc: The unique number that identifies the pull request within the repository.

inputs.name: maintainer_can_modify
inputs.desc: Indicates whether [maintainers can modify](https://docs.github.com/articles/allowing-changes-to-a-pull-request-branch-created-from-a-fork/) the pull request. If None, this setting remains unchanged.

name: Update a pull request branch
description: Updates an open pull request's head branch by merging the latest changes from its base branch, if mergeable and repository merging is enabled; operates asynchronously.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: Repository name, without the `.git` extension (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: Account owner of the repository (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: pull_number
inputs.desc: Unique number identifying the pull request.

inputs.name: expected_head_sha
inputs.desc: SHA of the expected head commit of the pull request's branch. If this SHA does not match the actual head SHA, the request fails (422 error), ensuring the branch was not updated unexpectedly. If omitted, the API uses the current head SHA of the branch.

name: Update a reference
description: Updates an existing Git reference (e.g., a branch or tag) to point to a specific commit SHA. By default, only allows fast-forward updates (where the new commit is a descendant of the current one). Set `force=true` to allow non-fast-forward updates that rewrite history. IMPORTANT: The reference must already exist - use 'Create a reference' action to create new refs.

inputs.name: ref
inputs.desc: The name of the Git reference to update WITHOUT the 'refs/' prefix. For branches: use 'heads/<branch-name>' (e.g., 'heads/main', 'heads/feature-branch'). For tags: use 'tags/<tag-name>' (e.g., 'tags/v1.0.0'). Note: Branch references require 'heads/' (plural), not 'head/' (singular). Bare branch names will be automatically prefixed with 'heads/'. The reference must already exist - this endpoint only updates existing refs.

inputs.name: sha
inputs.desc: The SHA1 commit hash to point this reference to. Must be exactly 40 hexadecimal characters (0-9, a-f) representing a valid commit that exists in the repository.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: Repository name, excluding `.git` extension (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: force
inputs.desc: Controls whether to allow non-fast-forward updates. When `false` (default), the new commit must be a direct descendant of the current commit (fast-forward only) - this prevents accidental history rewriting. When `true`, allows moving the reference to any commit, even if it means rewriting history or moving backward. Set to `true` only when you need to force-push changes, reset a branch, or intentionally rewrite commit history.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: Account owner of the repository (case-insensitive).

name: Update a release
description: Updates an existing release in a GitHub repository, allowing modification of its attributes; if linking a discussion, the `discussion_category_name` must refer to an existing category in the repository.

inputs.name: body
inputs.desc: Text describing the contents of the release. Supports GitHub Flavored Markdown.

inputs.name: name
inputs.desc: The title of the release.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: draft
inputs.desc: Set to `true` to mark the release as a draft, or `false` to publish it. Default: `false`.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account owner of the repository. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: tag_name
inputs.desc: The name of the tag. This can be an existing tag or a new tag name.

inputs.name: prerelease
inputs.desc: Set to `true` to identify the release as a pre-release, or `false` for a full release. Default: `false`.

inputs.name: release_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the release to be updated.

inputs.name: make_latest
inputs.desc: Specifies whether this release should be set as the latest release for the repository. Accepts 'true', 'false', or 'legacy'. Drafts and pre-releases cannot be set as latest. Defaults to `true` for newly published releases. `legacy` specifies that the latest release should be determined based on the release creation date and higher semantic version.

inputs.name: target_commitish
inputs.desc: Specifies the commitish value (e.g., branch name or commit SHA) that determines where the Git tag is created from. Can be any branch or commit SHA. This parameter is unused if the Git tag specified by `tag_name` already exists. Defaults to the repository's default branch if omitted.

inputs.name: discussion_category_name
inputs.desc: If specified, a discussion of this category is created and linked to the release. The category must already exist in the repository. This parameter is ignored if a discussion is already linked to the release. For more information, see "[Managing categories for discussions in your repository](https://docs.github.com/discussions/managing-discussions-for-your-community/managing-categories-for-discussions-in-your-repository)."

name: Update a release asset
description: Updates the name, label, or state of a release asset in a GitHub repository, requiring at least one of these properties to be provided for modification.

inputs.name: name
inputs.desc: The new file name for the asset. This updates the display name of the asset.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository without the `.git` extension. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: label
inputs.desc: An alternate short description for the asset. This is used in place of the filename if provided, offering a more descriptive label.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: state
inputs.desc: The new state of the release asset. While the GitHub API documentation primarily shows 'state' in responses (e.g., 'uploaded'), this field allows attempting to update it. The effect and accepted values might be specific.

inputs.name: asset_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the release asset to be updated.

name: Update a repository
description: Updates settings for an existing GitHub repository, such as name, description, visibility, merge strategies, and security configurations.

inputs.name: name
inputs.desc: New name for the repository (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: ACTUAL name of an existing repository, without the `.git` extension (not case-sensitive). MUST be a real repository that exists and is accessible - placeholder values like 'your-repo-name' will cause 404 errors.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: ACTUAL GitHub username or organization name that owns the repository (not case-sensitive). MUST be a real, existing account - placeholder values like 'your-username' will cause 404 errors.

inputs.name: private
inputs.desc: Whether the repository is private. IMPORTANT: Public forks cannot be made private - this is a GitHub platform restriction. Changing visibility may also be restricted by organization policies.

inputs.name: archived
inputs.desc: Archives the repository (making it read-only) or unarchives it.

inputs.name: has_wiki
inputs.desc: Enables the GitHub Wiki.

inputs.name: homepage
inputs.desc: URL of the project's homepage.

inputs.name: has_issues
inputs.desc: Enables GitHub Issues for this repository.

inputs.name: visibility
inputs.desc: Repository visibility. IMPORTANT: Public forks cannot be made private - this is a GitHub platform restriction.

inputs.name: description
inputs.desc: Brief summary of the repository.

inputs.name: is_template
inputs.desc: Designates this repository as a template repository.

inputs.name: has_projects
inputs.desc: Enables GitHub Projects. Note: Organization settings might override this and cause an error if projects are disabled organization-wide.

inputs.name: allow_forking
inputs.desc: Allows private forks of this repository. IMPORTANT: This setting can ONLY be changed for organization-owned repositories. Attempting to set this for user-owned repositories will result in a 422 error. Omit this field if you don't want to change the forking setting.

inputs.name: default_branch
inputs.desc: Name of the default branch.

inputs.name: allow_auto_merge
inputs.desc: Enables auto-merge for pull requests.

inputs.name: allow_merge_commit
inputs.desc: Allows merging pull requests via merge commits.

inputs.name: allow_rebase_merge
inputs.desc: Allows rebase-merging pull requests.

inputs.name: allow_squash_merge
inputs.desc: Allows squash-merging pull requests.

inputs.name: merge_commit_title
inputs.desc: Default title for merge commits: 'PR_TITLE' or 'MERGE_MESSAGE'. CONSTRAINT: If set to 'PR_TITLE', merge_commit_message must be 'PR_BODY' or 'BLANK'. If set to 'MERGE_MESSAGE', merge_commit_message must be blank/unset.

inputs.name: allow_update_branch
inputs.desc: Allows updating a pull request's head branch from the base branch if not strictly required for merging.

inputs.name: merge_commit_message
inputs.desc: Default message for merge commits: 'PR_BODY', 'PR_TITLE', or 'BLANK'. CONSTRAINT: If merge_commit_title is 'PR_TITLE', this must be 'PR_BODY' or 'BLANK'. Cannot be set when merge_commit_title is 'MERGE_MESSAGE'.

inputs.name: delete_branch_on_merge
inputs.desc: Automatically deletes head branches after merging pull requests.

inputs.name: squash_merge_commit_title
inputs.desc: Default title for squash merge commits: 'PR_TITLE' or 'COMMIT_OR_PR_TITLE'.

inputs.name: squash_merge_commit_message
inputs.desc: Default message for squash merge commits: 'PR_BODY', 'COMMIT_MESSAGES', or 'BLANK'.

inputs.name: web_commit_signoff_required
inputs.desc: Requires contributors to sign off on web-based commits.

inputs.name: use_squash_pr_title_as_default
inputs.desc: DEPRECATED: Use `squash_merge_commit_title`. If true, squash merge commits use PR title; otherwise, commit message.

inputs.name: security__and__analysis__secret__scanning__status
inputs.desc: Manages secret scanning. Set to 'enabled' or 'disabled'.

inputs.name: security__and__analysis__advanced__security__status
inputs.desc: Manages GitHub Advanced Security. Set to 'enabled' or 'disabled'.

inputs.name: security__analysis__secret__scanning__push__protection__status
inputs.desc: Manages secret scanning push protection. Set to 'enabled' or 'disabled'.

name: Update a repository invitation
description: Updates an active repository invitation to modify the invited user's permissions; the specified repository and invitation must exist.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account owner of the repository (e.g., a username or organization name). This name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: permissions
inputs.desc: The new permissions to grant the invited user on the repository.

inputs.name: invitation_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the repository invitation to be updated.

name: Update a repository ruleset
description: Updates an existing repository ruleset, identified by `ruleset_id` for a given repository, allowing partial updates to its configuration such as name, target, enforcement, bypass actors, conditions, and rules.

inputs.name: name
inputs.desc: The new name for the ruleset.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository without the `.git` extension. This name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account owner of the repository. This name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: rules
inputs.desc: String representing rules to enforce. Each rule has a `type` and optional `parameters` (e.g., `[{'type': 'creation'}, {'type': 'pull_request', 'parameters': {'required_approving_review_count': 2}}]`). Accepts both JSON format with double quotes or Python list syntax with single quotes. Refer to GitHub API documentation for available rule types and their parameters.

inputs.name: target
inputs.desc: The target of the ruleset (e.g., branch or tag).

inputs.name: ruleset_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the existing ruleset to be updated.

inputs.name: enforcement
inputs.desc: Specifies the enforcement level: `disabled` (ruleset is ignored), `active` (ruleset is enforced), or `evaluate` (allows testing rules before enforcement - GitHub Enterprise only).

inputs.name: bypass_actors
inputs.desc: String representing actors that can bypass ruleset. Each actor specifies `actor_id`, `actor_type` (`RepositoryRole`, `Team`, `Integration`, `OrganizationAdmin`), and `bypass_mode` (`always`, `pull_request`). Accepts both JSON format with double quotes or Python list syntax with single quotes. Example: `[{'actor_id': 1, 'actor_type': 'RepositoryRole', 'bypass_mode': 'always'}]`.

inputs.name: conditions__ref__name__exclude
inputs.desc: Ref names or patterns (fnmatch-style globs) to exclude. The ruleset will not apply if any of these patterns match.

inputs.name: conditions__ref__name__include
inputs.desc: Ref names or patterns (fnmatch-style globs) to include for the ruleset to apply. `~DEFAULT_BRANCH` targets the default branch; `~ALL` targets all branches.

name: Update a repository variable
description: Updates the name and/or value of an existing GitHub Actions variable in a repository.

inputs.name: name
inputs.desc: The current name of the Actions variable to update (path parameter). Variable names are case-insensitive and can only contain alphanumeric characters or underscores.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension; case-insensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account owner of the repository (e.g., GitHub username or organization name); case-insensitive.

inputs.name: value
inputs.desc: The new value for the Actions variable. If omitted, the current value is retained. Must be a non-empty string if provided. You must provide either new_name or value (or both).

inputs.name: new_name
inputs.desc: Optional new name for the variable. If provided, renames the variable. Can only contain alphanumeric characters or underscores; cannot start with GITHUB_ prefix.

name: Update a repository webhook
description: Updates the URL, content type, secret, SSL verification, events, or active status for an existing repository webhook, specified by `owner`, `repo`, and `hook_id`.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: Repository name, without the `.git` extension (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: Username or organization name of the repository owner (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: active
inputs.desc: Set `true` if active and sending notifications, `false` otherwise.

inputs.name: events
inputs.desc: List of events to trigger the webhook. GitHub REPLACES the full event list when this is sent — omit (leave unset) to preserve the existing subscription. Use `add_events`/`remove_events` for incremental changes. See GitHub webhook event payloads docs for available event types.

inputs.name: hook_id
inputs.desc: Unique identifier of the webhook (found in `X-GitHub-Hook-ID` header of a delivery).

inputs.name: add_events
inputs.desc: Events to add to current subscriptions.

inputs.name: config__url
inputs.desc: New URL for webhook payload delivery.

inputs.name: remove_events
inputs.desc: Events to remove from current subscriptions.

inputs.name: config__secret
inputs.desc: Optional secret for HMAC digest of delivery signature headers (see GitHub docs).

inputs.name: config__content__type
inputs.desc: Media type for serializing payloads ('json' or 'form').

inputs.name: config__insecure__ssl
inputs.desc: Set to '1' to disable SSL verification (insecure), '0' to enable.

name: Update a team
description: Updates a team's settings (e.g., name, description, privacy, parent team) within a GitHub organization, identified by its slug and organization name.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The organization's name (case-insensitive). This is a required path parameter.

inputs.name: name
inputs.desc: New display name for the team.

inputs.name: privacy
inputs.desc:
The desired privacy level for the team. Editing teams without specifying this parameter leaves `privacy` intact. When a team is nested, the `privacy` for parent teams cannot be `secret`.
  - For a non-nested team:
    - `secret`: Visible only to organization owners and members of this team.
    - `closed`: Visible to all members of this organization.
    - `public`: Visible to all members of this organization (equivalent to `closed`).
  - For a parent or child team:
    - `closed`: Visible to all members of this organization.
    - `public`: Visible to all members of this organization (equivalent to `closed`).

inputs.name: team_slug
inputs.desc: The team's unique slug identifier (typically URL-friendly version of team name). This is a required path parameter.

inputs.name: permission
inputs.desc: **Deprecated**. Default permission (pull, push, or admin) for new repositories added to this team. This may be removed in future versions.

inputs.name: description
inputs.desc: New team description; an empty string clears the existing description.

inputs.name: parent_team_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier (ID) of an existing team to set as this team's parent. This establishes a nested team structure. To remove an existing parent team relationship and make this a top-level team, explicitly pass `null` for this field (e.g., by setting this parameter to Python `None`). If this parameter is not provided in the request, the current parent team, if any, remains unchanged.

inputs.name: notification_setting
inputs.desc:
The notification setting the team has chosen. Editing teams without specifying this parameter leaves `notification_setting` intact.
  - `notifications_enabled`: Team members receive notifications when the team is @mentioned.
  - `notifications_disabled`: No one receives notifications for team @mentions.

name: Update branch protection
description: Updates the protection settings for an existing branch in a repository, which must not contain wildcard characters.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account owner of the repository (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: branch
inputs.desc: Name of the branch to protect; wildcard characters are not allowed (use GraphQL API for wildcard support).

inputs.name: lock_branch
inputs.desc: If `True`, sets the branch to read-only, preventing pushes.

inputs.name: enforce_admins
inputs.desc: Enforces configured restrictions for repository administrators. Set to `True` to enforce for admins, or `None`/`null` to disable.

inputs.name: allow_deletions
inputs.desc: Allows deletion of the protected branch.

inputs.name: block_creations
inputs.desc: If `True`, push restrictions also apply to creating matching new branches, unless pusher has explicit push access.

inputs.name: allow_force_pushes
inputs.desc: Permits force pushes to the protected branch. Set to `True` to allow, `None`/`null` to block.

inputs.name: allow_fork_syncing
inputs.desc: Allows users to pull upstream changes into their fork when the branch is locked.

inputs.name: restrictions__apps
inputs.desc: App `slug`s with push access. Cannot be used with `restrictions_users` or `restrictions_teams`. Empty list removes all.

inputs.name: restrictions__teams
inputs.desc: Team `slug`s with push access. Cannot be used with `restrictions_apps` or `restrictions_users`. Empty list removes all.

inputs.name: restrictions__users
inputs.desc: User `login`s with push access. Cannot be used with `restrictions_apps` or `restrictions_teams`. Empty list removes all.

inputs.name: required_linear_history
inputs.desc: Enforces linear commit history by preventing merge commits. Repository must allow squash or rebase merging.

inputs.name: required__status__checks__checks
inputs.desc: List of status checks required to merge. Each check object needs a `context` string and optionally an `app_id` integer. **Mutually exclusive with `contexts`** - use one or the other, not both.

inputs.name: required__status__checks__strict
inputs.desc: Require branches to be up to date with the base branch before merging; if `True`, merges are blocked if the head branch is behind the base branch.

inputs.name: required_conversation_resolution
inputs.desc: Requires all conversations on code to be resolved before merging a pull request.

inputs.name: required__status__checks__contexts
inputs.desc: **Deprecated**: List of status check contexts required to merge. If set by a GitHub App, future checks must come from that app. Use `checks` for finer control. **Mutually exclusive with `checks`** - use one or the other, not both.

inputs.name: required__pull__request__reviews__dismiss__stale__reviews
inputs.desc: If `True`, new commits dismiss previous review approvals on a pull request branch.

inputs.name: required__pull_request__reviews__approving__review__count
inputs.desc: Number of approving reviews (0-6) required to merge a pull request; 0 disables.

inputs.name: required__pull_request__reviews__bypass__allowances__apps
inputs.desc: App `slug`s allowed to bypass pull request requirements. Empty list removes all.

inputs.name: required__pull_request__reviews__bypass__allowances__teams
inputs.desc: Team `slug`s allowed to bypass pull request requirements. Empty list removes all.

inputs.name: required__pull_request__reviews__bypass__allowances__users
inputs.desc: User `login`s allowed to bypass pull request requirements. Empty list removes all.

inputs.name: required__pull_request__reviews__dismiss__restrictions__apps
inputs.desc: App `slug`s allowed to dismiss pull request reviews. Empty list removes all.

inputs.name: required__pull_request__reviews__dismiss__restrictions__teams
inputs.desc: Team `slug`s allowed to dismiss pull request reviews. Empty list removes all.

inputs.name: required__pull_request__reviews__dismiss__restrictions__users
inputs.desc: User `login`s allowed to dismiss pull request reviews. Empty list removes all.

inputs.name: required__pull_request__reviews__require__code__owner__reviews
inputs.desc: If `True`, pull requests affecting files with code owners must be reviewed by them.

inputs.name: required__pull_request__reviews__require__last__push__approval
inputs.desc: If `True`, the most recent push must be approved by someone other than the pusher.

name: Update a code scanning default setup configuration
description: Updates the default setup configuration for code scanning in a repository; GitHub Advanced Security must be enabled for the repository.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: Repository name (case-insensitive, without `.git` extension).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: Account owner of the repository (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: state
inputs.desc: Specifies whether to enable ('configured') or disable ('not-configured') code scanning default setup.

inputs.name: languages
inputs.desc: CodeQL languages for analysis; if omitted, all supported repository languages are analyzed.

inputs.name: query_suite
inputs.desc: CodeQL query suite to be used ('default' for curated, 'extended' for comprehensive queries).

inputs.name: runner_type
inputs.desc: Type of runner to use for code scanning. 'standard' uses GitHub-hosted runners, 'labeled' uses self-hosted runners with a specific label, 'not_set' resets to default.

inputs.name: runner_label
inputs.desc: Label of the self-hosted runner to use when runner_type is 'labeled'. Ignored when runner_type is 'standard'.

name: Update a codespace for the authenticated user
description: Updates an existing GitHub Codespace's machine type, display name, or recent folders for the authenticated user; machine type changes take effect on the next start.

inputs.name: machine
inputs.desc: Target machine type for the codespace; must be valid and available for the user or organization.

inputs.name: display_name
inputs.desc: New display name for the codespace.

inputs.name: codespace_name
inputs.desc: The unique name of the codespace to be updated (e.g., 'octocat-codespace-githu-org-name-abcdef12').

inputs.name: recent_folders
inputs.desc: Up to three recently opened folder paths (e.g., '/workspaces/project/src') to suggest which folder to load upon opening.

name: Update Git LFS Preference
description: Sets the Git Large File Storage (LFS) preference for a repository during a source import. NOTE: This endpoint has been deprecated by GitHub as of April 12, 2024, and is no longer available. The Source Imports REST API has been retired due to very low usage levels. Please use the GitHub Importer tool at https://github.com/new/import for repository imports.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The username or organization name that owns the repository. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: use_lfs
inputs.desc: Specifies whether to store large files using Git LFS during the import. `opt_in` enables Git LFS, storing large files. `opt_out` disables Git LFS, removing large files during the import.

name: Update issue type
description: Update an existing issue type for a GitHub organization. Requires the authenticated user to be an organization administrator. OAuth tokens need admin:org scope.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The organization name (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: name
inputs.desc: The name of the issue type.

inputs.name: color
inputs.desc: The color of the issue type.

inputs.name: is_enabled
inputs.desc: Whether the issue type is enabled at the organization level.

inputs.name: description
inputs.desc: A short description of the issue type.

inputs.name: issue_type_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the issue type to update.

name: Update a webhook configuration for an organization
description: Updates the configuration (URL, content type, secret, SSL verification) for an existing webhook within a specified organization.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The organization name (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: url
inputs.desc: The URL to which the webhook payloads will be delivered.

inputs.name: secret
inputs.desc: Optional secret for HMAC hex digest in delivery signature headers.

inputs.name: hook_id
inputs.desc: Unique identifier of the webhook (from `X-GitHub-Hook-ID` header of a delivery).

inputs.name: content_type
inputs.desc: Media type for serializing payloads (`json` or `form`). Defaults to `form`.

inputs.name: insecure_ssl
inputs.desc: Set to `1` to disable SSL certificate verification for the webhook URL, or `0` to enable. Defaults to `0`.

name: Update an existing project card (Classic - Deprecated)
description: Updates an existing project card's note and/or archived status (GitHub Classic Projects). DEPRECATION NOTICE: GitHub Projects (classic) and its REST API were sunset on April 1, 2025. This action will return 404 for most requests on GitHub.com. However, it may still work on GitHub Enterprise Server instances where classic projects are enabled. For new projects, use GitHub Projects V2 which uses the GraphQL API instead. See: https://github.blog/changelog/2024-05-23-sunset-notice-projects-classic/

inputs.name: note
inputs.desc: New note content (supports GitHub Flavored Markdown). Use empty string or `null` to clear the note. If omitted, the note remains unchanged.

inputs.name: card_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the project card to be updated. This ID must correspond to an existing classic project card.

inputs.name: archived
inputs.desc: Archive status: `true` to archive the card, `false` to unarchive it. If omitted, the archived status remains unchanged.

name: Update an existing project column (Classic - Deprecated)
description: Updates the name of an existing column, identified by `column_id`, in a GitHub project (classic). DEPRECATION NOTICE: GitHub Classic Projects (V1) and its REST API were sunset on April 1, 2025. This action will return a 404 error on GitHub.com. For new projects, use GitHub Projects V2 which uses the GraphQL API instead. See: https://github.blog/changelog/2024-05-23-sunset-notice-projects-classic/

inputs.name: name
inputs.desc: The new name for the project column.

inputs.name: column_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the project column to be updated.

name: Update a review for a pull request
description: Updates the body text of an existing pull request review.

inputs.name: body
inputs.desc: The new body text for the pull request review. This will replace the existing review body.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This is case-insensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The username of the account that owns the repository. This is case-insensitive.

inputs.name: review_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the pull request review to be updated.

inputs.name: pull_number
inputs.desc: The number that uniquely identifies the pull request within the repository.

name: Update a review comment for a pull request
description: Updates the body of an existing review comment on a pull request.

inputs.name: body
inputs.desc: The new text of the pull request review comment. Must be a non-empty string.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The username or organization name that owns the repository. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: comment_id
inputs.desc: The unique identifier of the pull request review comment to be updated.

name: Update pull request review protection
description: Updates pull request review protection settings (e.g., required approvals, review dismissal, bypass allowances) for a branch; branch protection features must be available for the repository.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: Repository name, without the `.git` extension (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: Account owner of the repository (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: branch
inputs.desc: Branch name for protection rules (no wildcards; use GraphQL API for wildcards).

inputs.name: dismiss_stale_reviews
inputs.desc: If `true`, new commits dismiss previous approving reviews; `false` keeps approvals valid.

inputs.name: require_code_owner_reviews
inputs.desc: If `true`, pull requests affecting files with code owners require their review. See [about code owners](https://docs.github.com/articles/about-code-owners/).

inputs.name: require_last_push_approval
inputs.desc: If `true`, the most recent push requires approval by someone other than the pusher (GitHub Enterprise feature).

inputs.name: dismissal__restrictions__apps
inputs.desc: App `slug`s permitted to dismiss pull request reviews. Empty list removes restrictions.

inputs.name: dismissal__restrictions__teams
inputs.desc: Team `slug`s permitted to dismiss pull request reviews. Empty list removes restrictions.

inputs.name: dismissal__restrictions__users
inputs.desc: User `login`s permitted to dismiss pull request reviews. Empty list removes restrictions.

inputs.name: required_approving_review_count
inputs.desc: Number of approving reviews (1-6) required for merging; 0 to disable.

inputs.name: bypass__pull__request__allowances__apps
inputs.desc: App `slug`s allowed to bypass pull request requirements. Empty list removes allowances.

inputs.name: bypass__pull__request__allowances__teams
inputs.desc: Team `slug`s allowed to bypass pull request requirements. Empty list removes allowances.

inputs.name: bypass__pull__request__allowances__users
inputs.desc: User `login`s allowed to bypass pull request requirements. Empty list removes allowances.

name: Update repository preferences for check suites
description: Updates repository preferences for automatic Check Suite creation on code pushes, allowing configuration for specific GitHub Apps that must be installed on the repository with `checks:write` permission.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account owner of the repository (e.g., username or organization). The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: auto_trigger_checks
inputs.desc: A list of preference objects as a JSON string for configuring automatic CheckSuite creation for specific GitHub Apps. Each object in the list must contain an `app_id` (integer: the ID of the GitHub App) and a `setting` (boolean: `true` to enable automatic triggers for that app on code push, `false` to disable). Example: '[{"app_id": 12345, "setting": true}]'

name: Update a webhook configuration for a repository
description: Updates the configuration (e.g., payload URL, content type, secret, SSL verification) for an existing webhook in a specified repository.

inputs.name: url
inputs.desc: The URL to which webhook payloads will be delivered.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account owner of the repository (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: secret
inputs.desc: Optional secret for generating an HMAC hex digest of the payload, included in the `X-Hub-Signature-256` header to verify authenticity.

inputs.name: hook_id
inputs.desc: Unique identifier of the webhook, typically found in the `X-GitHub-Hook-ID` header of a webhook delivery.

inputs.name: content_type
inputs.desc: Media type for payload serialization (e.g., `json`, `form`). GitHub defaults to `form` if this field is not provided.

inputs.name: insecure_ssl
inputs.desc: Specifies SSL certificate verification: '0' for enabled (default behavior if omitted), '1' for disabled (not recommended).

name: Update resource access with tokens
description: Revokes organization access for the personal access tokens identified by `pat_ids`; this action must be performed by a GitHub App, and `pat_ids` must be valid and associated with the organization. AUTHENTICATION REQUIREMENT: This endpoint requires GitHub App authentication with 'Personal access tokens' organization permissions (write). Standard personal access tokens CANNOT use this endpoint. The GitHub App must also have access to the organization.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: Organization name (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: action
inputs.desc: Operation to perform on the fine-grained personal access tokens, resulting in removal of their access.

inputs.name: pat_ids
inputs.desc: Fine-grained personal access token IDs for which access will be revoked.

name: Update status check protection
description: Updates required status checks for a branch, optionally requiring it to be up-to-date before merging.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account owner of the repository. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: branch
inputs.desc: The name of the branch (required, cannot be null or empty). Wildcard characters are not allowed. To use wildcard characters in branch names, please use the GraphQL API.

inputs.name: checks
inputs.desc: A list of status checks to require in order to merge into this branch. Each check object should have a `context` (string) and an optional `app_id` (integer). Example: `[{"context": "continuous-integration/jenkins", "app_id": 12345}]`

inputs.name: strict
inputs.desc: Specifies whether to require branches to be up to date before merging.

inputs.name: contexts
inputs.desc: **Deprecated**: A list of status checks to require in order to merge into this branch. If any of these checks were recently set by a particular GitHub App, they will be required to come from that app in the future for the branch to merge. For more fine-grained control, use `checks` instead of `contexts`. Can be provided as a single string or a list of strings.

name: Update Subscription
description: Tool to update notification subscription state for issues, pull requests, or discussions. Use when you need to subscribe, unsubscribe, or ignore notifications for a specific GitHub item.

inputs.name: state
inputs.desc: The new subscription state. SUBSCRIBED: receive notifications for all activity. UNSUBSCRIBED: stop receiving notifications. IGNORED: never receive notifications even when mentioned.

inputs.name: subscribableId
inputs.desc: The Node ID of the subscribable object (issue, pull request, discussion, etc.) to modify. This is a global GraphQL ID, not a repository-local number.

inputs.name: clientMutationId
inputs.desc: A unique identifier for the client performing the mutation. Used for tracking and deduplication.

name: Update the authenticated user
description: Updates the authenticated user's GitHub profile; a new public email must be verified, and existing private emails remain private even if specified.

inputs.name: bio
inputs.desc: The new short biography for the user. Provide `null` to clear the existing bio.

inputs.name: blog
inputs.desc: The new URL for the user's blog. Provide `null` to clear the existing blog URL. For example, 'https://github.blog'.

inputs.name: name
inputs.desc: The new full name for the user. For example, 'Mona Lisa Octocat'.

inputs.name: email
inputs.desc: The user's new publicly visible email address. This email must be verified on the user's GitHub account. If the provided email is not verified or not set to public, the update for this field might be ignored.

inputs.name: company
inputs.desc: The new company affiliation for the user. Provide `null` to clear the existing company. For example, 'GitHub'.

inputs.name: hireable
inputs.desc: Indicates whether the user is available for hire. Set to `true` if available, or `false` if not.

inputs.name: location
inputs.desc: The new geographical location of the user. Provide `null` to clear the existing location. For example, 'San Francisco'.

inputs.name: twitter_username
inputs.desc: The user's new Twitter username (without the leading '@'). Provide `null` to clear the existing Twitter username. For example, 'github'.

name: Revoke Token Organization Access
description: Revokes a fine-grained personal access token's access to an organization. IMPORTANT: This endpoint can ONLY be called by GitHub Apps with 'Personal access tokens' organization permissions (write). Personal access tokens (PATs) cannot use this endpoint. Use GITHUB_LIST_ORG_RESOURCE_ACCESS_TOKENS to get valid pat_id values for tokens that have access to your organization.

inputs.name: org
inputs.desc: The organization name (case-insensitive). The authenticated GitHub App must have 'Personal access tokens' organization permissions (write) for this organization.

inputs.name: action
inputs.desc: The action to perform. Currently only 'revoke' is supported, which removes the token's access to the organization.

inputs.name: pat_id
inputs.desc: The unique numeric identifier of the fine-grained personal access token to revoke. Obtain this from the GITHUB_LIST_ORG_RESOURCE_ACCESS_TOKENS action.

name: Update User List
description: Tool to update an existing GitHub user list. Use when you need to modify the name, description, or privacy settings of a user list.

inputs.name: name
inputs.desc: The new name for the user list. Only provide if you want to change the name.

inputs.name: list_id
inputs.desc: The unique ID of the user list to update. This is the GraphQL node ID (e.g., 'UL_kwDOCNqc1s4AbuJ8').

inputs.name: is_private
inputs.desc: Whether the list should be private. Set to true to make private, false to make public. Only provide if you want to change visibility.

inputs.name: description
inputs.desc: The new description for the user list. Only provide if you want to change the description.

inputs.name: client_mutation_id
inputs.desc: A unique identifier for the client performing the mutation. Used for idempotency and tracking.

name: Update user lists for item
description: Tool to update which of the viewer's lists an item belongs to on GitHub. Use when you need to add an item (repository, issue, pull request, etc.) to specific user lists or move it between lists.

inputs.name: item_id
inputs.desc: The global node ID of the item to add to the lists (e.g., 'R_kgDOKl1DMA' for a repository). This is the GraphQL global identifier returned by GitHub's API.

inputs.name: list_ids
inputs.desc: The list of user list IDs that this item should belong to. The item will be added to these lists and removed from any other lists it previously belonged to.

inputs.name: client_mutation_id
inputs.desc: A unique identifier for the client performing the mutation. This can be used to track the request.

inputs.name: suggested_list_ids
inputs.desc: Optional list of suggested list IDs to create and add this item to. These lists will be created if they don't exist.

name: Update Project V2 Item for User
description: Tool to update a field value for an item in a user's GitHub ProjectV2. Use when you need to modify a field value of an existing project item.

inputs.name: value
inputs.desc: The value to set. Use {'text': 'value'} for text fields, {'number': 123} for number fields, {'singleSelectOptionId': 'option_id'} for single select, {'date': '2024-01-01'} for date fields, {'iterationId': 'iteration_id'} for iteration fields.

inputs.name: itemId
inputs.desc: The global node ID of the project item to update (e.g., 'PVTI_lAHOCNqc1s4BN3oqzgldG_w').

inputs.name: fieldId
inputs.desc: The global node ID of the field to update (e.g., 'PVTSSF_lAHOCNqc1s4BN3oqzg8vTnA').

inputs.name: projectId
inputs.desc: The global node ID of the Project (e.g., 'PVT_kwHOCNqc1s4BN3oq').

name: Change User Status
description: Tool to update your status on GitHub. Use when you need to set or clear your GitHub profile status with an emoji, message, and availability indicator.

inputs.name: emoji
inputs.desc: The emoji to represent your status. Can either be a native Unicode emoji or an emoji name with colons, e.g., :rocket:, :smiley:. If omitted, any existing emoji will be cleared.

inputs.name: message
inputs.desc: A short description of your current status. Maximum 80 characters. If omitted, any existing message will be cleared.

inputs.name: expiresAt
inputs.desc: If set, the user status will not be shown after this date. Must be a valid ISO 8601 datetime string (e.g., 2024-12-31T23:59:59Z). If omitted, the status will persist indefinitely.

inputs.name: organizationId
inputs.desc: The global node ID of the organization whose members will be allowed to see the status. If omitted, the status will be publicly visible to everyone. Use this to limit status visibility to organization members only.

inputs.name: clientMutationId
inputs.desc: A unique identifier for the client performing the mutation. This value will be returned in the response and can be used for request tracking or idempotency.

inputs.name: limitedAvailability
inputs.desc: Whether this status should indicate you are not fully available on GitHub (e.g., you are away or busy). When true, shows a busy indicator. Defaults to false if omitted.

name: Upload a release asset
description: Tool to upload a binary asset to an existing GitHub release. Use when you need to attach files like installers or backups to a release programmatically.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: Repository name without the .git extension; not case sensitive.

inputs.name: asset
inputs.desc: Binary file to upload as the release asset.

inputs.name: label
inputs.desc: Short description for the asset; displayed instead of filename.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: Account owner of the repository; not case sensitive.

inputs.name: release_id
inputs.desc: Unique identifier of the release. The release must have a valid upload URL (i.e., not already finalized without one). Asset size must not exceed 2 GB.

inputs.name: content_type
inputs.desc: Media type of the asset, for example 'application/zip'.

name: Upload an analysis as sarif data
description: Uploads a gzipped and Base64 encoded SARIF file to a GitHub repository for a specific commit and reference; use `checkout_uri` if SARIF paths are absolute.

inputs.name: ref
inputs.desc: The full Git reference for the analysis, formatted as `refs/heads/<branch name>`, `refs/tags/<tag>`, `refs/pull/<number>/merge`, or `refs/pull/<number>/head`.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This is case-insensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account owner of the repository. This is case-insensitive.

inputs.name: sarif
inputs.desc: A Base64 encoded string of a gzipped SARIF file. For more information, see '[SARIF support for code scanning](https://docs.github.com/code-security/secure-coding/sarif-support-for-code-scanning)'.

inputs.name: validate
inputs.desc: Specifies whether the uploaded SARIF file should be validated against the code scanning specifications. This is useful for integrators to ensure their SARIF files are correctly processed and rendered by GitHub.

inputs.name: tool_name
inputs.desc: Name of the tool that generated the analysis. GitHub defaults this to "API" if omitted. A tool GUID in the SARIF can be used for filtering alerts.

inputs.name: commit_sha
inputs.desc: The SHA of the commit to which the analysis you are uploading relates. Must be a 40-character Git SHA.

inputs.name: started_at
inputs.desc: The timestamp indicating when the analysis run began, in ISO 8601 format: `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`.

inputs.name: checkout_uri
inputs.desc: The base URI of the checkout directory as it appears in the SARIF file. This property is used to convert absolute file paths to relative paths, enabling alerts to be correctly mapped to their location in the repository.

name: Create or update repository custom property values
description: Creates or updates up to 30 custom property values for a repository; custom properties must be predefined at the organization or repository level, and setting a property's value to `null` removes it.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: The account owner of the repository. This name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: properties
inputs.desc: A list of custom property objects as a JSON string to create or update for the repository. Each object in the list must contain `property_name` (string identifying the custom property) and `value` (the value for the property, which can be a string, number, or a single-select option, matching the property's defined type). Example: '[{"property_name": "environment", "value": "production"}]'

name: Verify dev container permissions accepted
description: Verifies if the authenticated user has accepted permissions for a specific devcontainer configuration in a repository, typically to ensure awareness before a codespace is created or used.

inputs.name: ref
inputs.desc: Git reference (branch, tag, or commit SHA) for the devcontainer configuration.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.desc: Repository name, without `.git` extension (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.desc: Username of the repository owner (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: devcontainer_path
inputs.desc: Path to the `devcontainer.json` file in the repository.
